description:
## What to build

A /dashboard route as the post-login landing page for doctors. Cards grid layout (2x2).

## Cards

1. **Revenue last 30 days** — call GET /api/v1/commissions/current-month, show total_revenue + commission_amount. Graceful empty state if commissions DB not set up.
2. **Recent sessions** — call GET /api/v1/sessions/?limit=5, show patient name + status icon. Each row links to the session results.
3. **Order test** — CTA card. 'Ordenar teste para paciente' button that opens a modal: search patients (from MedSimples via new proxy endpoint), select, pick test/battery, create session.
4. **Available tests** — call GET /api/v1/tests/definitions, list test names with domain badges.

## Backend needed

- GET /api/v1/patients/search?q= — proxy to MedSimples GET /patients?search= using doctor's JWT (forward ct_token or call with service creds). Returns list of {id, name, dob}.
- Sessions list already works but needs patient name included in response (join Patient.cached_name).

## Frontend

- New route: cognitest/frontend/src/routes/(app)/dashboard/+page.svelte
- Change DEFAULT_POST_LOGIN_ROUTE from '/commissions' to '/dashboard'
- i18n: add pt-BR and en keys for dashboard section
- Tailwind + existing card/btn-primary CSS classes

## Design

- 2x2 grid on desktop, single column on mobile
- Revenue card: large number, muted label, link to /commissions for detail
- Recent sessions card: compact list with status dots, 'Ver todos' link
- Order test card: centered CTA button, opens inline modal
- Available tests card: tag/pill list of test names
title: Doctor dashboard: revenue, recent sessions, order test, available tests

description:
## What

Add GET /api/v1/patients/search?q= endpoint to CogniTest backend. Proxies to MedSimples GET /patients?search={q} by forwarding the doctor's Bearer token (ct_token cookie).

## Context

- MedSimples GET /patients?search= is protected by get_current_user_with_rls (RLS, not webhook secret)
- CogniTest frontend never calls MedSimples directly — must go through CogniTest backend
- The ct_token cookie IS a valid MedSimples JWT — forward it as Bearer in the Authorization header
- MedSimples patient fields: id (int), name (str), birth (date), sex, email1, tel1
- cognitest/backend/app/services/medsimples_client.py already has _get_headers() for service-to-service; add a new bearer_headers(token) method instead
- Add new method to MedSimplesClient: search_patients(q: str, bearer_token: str, http_client: httpx.AsyncClient) -> list[dict]
- Endpoint requires authentication (Depends(get_current_doctor))

### Simplicity (UPDATED: dropped over-engineering)

- **NO X-Upstream-Available header** — return empty list [] on any MedSimples error (network, 4xx, 5xx). Simple and sufficient for v1.
- **NO PatientSearchResult Pydantic schema** — passthrough list[dict] from MedSimples directly. Pydantic validation of a trusted upstream adds fragility with no benefit.
- **NO schemas/patient.py file** — not needed without the schema
- **Use IP-based rate limiting** (existing slowapi pattern via get_remote_address) — do NOT implement per-user key function. 300ms frontend debounce already limits abuse surface. Per-user rate limiting requires custom key boilerplate not justified for v1.

### SSRF Prevention (UPDATED)

- Validate settings.medsimples_api_url at startup against expected hostname format (http/https scheme, no redirects)
- Set allow_redirects=False (or follow_redirects=False in httpx) on the shared AsyncClient — prevents redirect-based SSRF
- Do NOT log the Authorization header value (token leakage risk)

### Shared Client (CRITICAL)

- **search_patients MUST use self._shared_client** — do NOT follow the per-request pattern in get_patient() (line 110-113) which creates a new httpx.AsyncClient per call (TLS handshake ~50-150ms each)
- Check self._shared_client is not None and use it; only fall back to per-request client if explicitly unavailable
- Configure in main.py lifespan: app.state.http_client = httpx.AsyncClient(timeout=30.0, follow_redirects=False)

### httpx async pattern:

- Use shared httpx.AsyncClient from request.app.state.http_client (configured in lifespan), NOT a per-request client
- Per-request clients incur TLS handshake cost (~50-150ms) every call

### Graceful degradation:

- Return empty list for any error (404, network errors, MedSimples unreachable) — log the error server-side only
- No response headers for degraded state (v1 simplicity)

### Query parameter validation:

- Query(min_length=2, max_length=200)
- Add regex to reject non-printable characters

## Implementation

Add to cognitest/backend/app/services/medsimples_client.py:
  async def search_patients(self, q: str, bearer_token: str, http_client: httpx.AsyncClient) -> list[dict]
  - GET {self.base_url}/patients?search={q}
  - Authorization: Bearer {bearer_token} header
  - Returns raw list[dict] from MedSimples, empty list on any error
  - MUST use self._shared_client (not per-request client)

New file: cognitest/backend/app/routers/patients.py
  GET /patients/search?q= (min 2 chars, max 200 chars)
  - Requires get_current_doctor dependency
  - Rate limit: IP-based (existing get_remote_address pattern)
  - Extracts bearer token from cookie (request.cookies.get(CT_COOKIE_NAME))
  - Returns list[dict] passthrough from MedSimples

Register router in cognitest/backend/app/main.py
Also: configure shared httpx.AsyncClient in app lifespan with follow_redirects=False if not already done.

## Testing

- [ ] GET /patients/search?q=Jo returns list of patients matching 'Jo'
- [ ] GET /patients/search?q=x (1 char) returns 422 validation error
- [ ] No bearer token (unauthenticated) returns 401
- [ ] MedSimples unavailable returns empty list (not 500)
- [ ] Rate limiting active on endpoint
- [ ] Authorization header value is never logged

## Validation

- [ ] Endpoint exists at GET /api/v1/patients/search
- [ ] Bearer token forwarded correctly (uses ct_token cookie value)
- [ ] Does NOT use webhook secret for this endpoint
- [ ] Returns empty list (not error) when MedSimples is unreachable
- [ ] search_patients uses self._shared_client (confirmed in code review)
- [ ] follow_redirects=False on shared client

## Files

- cognitest/backend/app/services/medsimples_client.py
- cognitest/backend/app/routers/patients.py (NEW)
- cognitest/backend/app/main.py (lifespan shared client + router registration)
title: Backend: GET /api/v1/patients/search — proxy patient search to MedSimples

description:
## Issue

create_session sets clinician_user_id from the request body, not from the authenticated doctor:
```python
session = TestSession(
    ...
    clinician_user_id=session_data.clinician_user_id,  # from request body, not from doctor JWT
    ...
)
```

A caller can create sessions attributed to any clinician_user_id they supply. Those sessions will not appear in the creator's list_sessions (which filters by doctor.user_id) nor in the impersonated user's list unless their user_id matches. This is a data integrity issue and partial IDOR.

## Fix
Derive clinician_user_id from the authenticated doctor:
```python
session = TestSession(
    ...
    clinician_user_id=UUID(doctor.user_id),
    ...
)
```
Remove clinician_user_id from SessionCreate schema if it has no legitimate caller use case.

## Severity
P2 — authenticated users can create sessions attributed to other clinicians.

## Location
- cognitest/backend/app/routers/sessions.py (create_session, ~line 240)
- cognitest/backend/app/schemas/session.py (SessionCreate)

## Validation Criteria
- [ ] Session.clinician_user_id always equals the authenticated doctor.user_id
- [ ] SessionCreate no longer accepts clinician_user_id from callers (or it is ignored)
title: Security: clinician_user_id accepted from request body instead of enforced from JWT

description:
## Issue

patients.py and sessions.py create router-level Limiter instances using get_remote_address (slowapi's default), which reads request.client.host directly with no X-Forwarded-For handling.

The app-level limiter in main.py correctly uses get_real_ip (the project's proxy-aware implementation). In production behind a reverse proxy, request.client.host is always the proxy IP. Any client can also spoof X-Forwarded-For to any value they want with the router-level limiters, bypassing per-IP rate limits on:
- GET /api/v1/patients/search (30/minute)
- POST /api/v1/sessions/ (10/hour)

This enables patient enumeration and session spam.

## Fix
```python
# Replace in patients.py and sessions.py:
from ..core.ip import get_real_ip
limiter = Limiter(key_func=get_real_ip)
```
Or reuse the app-level limiter from main.py via request.app.state.limiter.

## Severity
P2 — rate limit bypass enables patient enumeration.

## Location
- cognitest/backend/app/routers/patients.py:14
- cognitest/backend/app/routers/sessions.py:33

## Validation Criteria
- [ ] Spoofed X-Forwarded-For does not bypass rate limit
- [ ] Rate limits apply correctly when behind a reverse proxy
title: Security: rate limiters use get_remote_address instead of get_real_ip — bypassable via X-Forwarded-For
Fix: handleConfirm in OrderTestModal silently swallows errors — no user feedback

description:
## Issue

The migration a1b2c3d4e5f6 has downgrade() as a no-op (pass). After a downgrade, Alembic marks the DB as being at revision 07c4a1e01d85, but the schema still has the INTEGER columns and new indexes. A subsequent upgrade will attempt to re-create indexes that already exist. The ix_patients_medsimples_patient_id and ix_patients_uq_org_patient creates in steps 3 and 4 have no IF NOT EXISTS guards, so they will fail.

```python
def downgrade() -> None:
    pass  # silent, misleading
```

## Fix
```python
def downgrade() -> None:
    raise NotImplementedError(
        'Downgrade not supported: INTEGER -> UUID conversion requires original UUID values. '
        'Restore from a database snapshot taken before this migration.'
    )
```

Also add IF NOT EXISTS to index creates in steps 3 and 4 to make upgrade re-entrant.

## Severity
P2 — a silent downgrade leaves DB in inconsistent state; next upgrade crashes.

## Location
- cognitest/backend/alembic/versions/a1b2c3d4e5f6_fix_patient_medsimples_id_to_integer.py:120-123

## Validation Criteria
- [ ] alembic downgrade -1 raises a clear error explaining why it is not supported
- [ ] Index creates in upgrade() have IF NOT EXISTS
- [ ] alembic upgrade head is idempotent (can be re-run safely)
title: Fix: migration downgrade() should raise NotImplementedError, not pass silently

description:
## Issue

PatientService.get_or_create_by_medsimples_id() calls self.db.rollback() on IntegrityError. This is the same SQLAlchemy Session object that create_session uses. A rollback at this point rolls back the entire outer transaction — including the credit deduction that may have already happened (CreditsService.consume_credit() is called before patient resolution).

The correct pattern is to use a savepoint:
```python
with self.db.begin_nested():  # SAVEPOINT
    self.db.add(patient)
    self.db.flush()
```
On IntegrityError, begin_nested()'s context manager rolls back only to the savepoint, leaving the outer transaction intact.

## Severity
P2 — race condition can cause credit deduction without session creation, or IntegrityError in patient creation causes the whole session creation to fail unexpectedly.

## Location
- cognitest/backend/app/services/patient_service.py:67-79

## Fix
Replace self.db.rollback() with self.db.begin_nested() savepoint pattern.

## Validation Criteria
- [ ] Concurrent patient creation for same (org, medsimples_patient_id) succeeds for both callers
- [ ] Credit deduction is not rolled back when patient race condition is handled
- [ ] IntegrityError retry works correctly
title: Fix: PatientService.db.rollback() rolls back outer create_session transaction

description:
## Issues

1. Malformed org_id in token raises 403 (forbidden) — should be 401 (token integrity issue)
2. org-not-found raises 422 (unprocessable entity) — should be 403 or 404
3. Detail message leaks internal MedSimples org ID: 'No CogniTest organization found for MedSimples org {ms_org_id}'

## Fixes

```python
# ValueError/TypeError on int() cast:
raise HTTPException(status_code=401, detail='Invalid token: malformed organization claim')

# org not found:
raise HTTPException(status_code=403, detail='Organization not enrolled in CogniTest')
# Log ms_org_id server-side only
```

## Severity
P3 — wrong status codes confuse clients; internal ID disclosure is low-risk but violates defence-in-depth for a medical platform.

## Location
- cognitest/backend/app/utils/org_utils.py:10, 15
title: Fix: org_utils.py uses wrong HTTP status codes and leaks internal org ID

description:
## Issues (all small, batch fix)

1. **In-function import** — sessions.py: `from datetime import date` inside a try block. Should be added to line 5 existing import: `from datetime import date, datetime, timezone`

2. **Unreachable raise** — patient_service.py line 97: `raise RuntimeError(...)` after the for loop is dead code. The loop always raises or returns before exhausting naturally.

3. **getattr inconsistency** — sessions.py line 146 uses `getattr(request.app.state, 'http_client', None)` while patients.py line 36 uses `request.app.state.http_client` directly. The fallback hides a startup misconfiguration. Use direct access everywhere.

4. **cached_dob type** — schemas/session.py: PatientInfo.cached_dob is typed `datetime | None` but the DB column (Patient.cached_dob) is Date, not DateTime. Should be `date | None`.

5. **Unused current_doctor parameter** — patients.py: current_doctor is declared for auth gating but never referenced. Rename to `_: DoctorInfo = Depends(get_current_doctor)` to signal intent.

## Locations
- cognitest/backend/app/routers/sessions.py:5, 146, 162
- cognitest/backend/app/services/patient_service.py:97
- cognitest/backend/app/schemas/session.py (PatientInfo.cached_dob)
- cognitest/backend/app/routers/patients.py:23
title: Fix: minor Python issues — in-function import, unreachable raise, getattr inconsistency, cached_dob type

description:
## Issues (frontend quality, batch fix)

1. **API_BASE duplicated** — defined identically in patients.ts, OrderTestModal.svelte, and dashboard/+page.svelte. Should live in one place (e.g. $lib/utils/api.ts).

2. **Hardcoded Portuguese strings** — OrderTestModal.svelte has several hardcoded strings outside i18n:
   - 'Nenhum paciente encontrado.'
   - 'Paciente:' labels in confirm step
   - 'Teste:' labels in confirm step
   - 'Copiado!' (mixed with $_() in same ternary)
   - 'Nenhum teste disponível.'
   All should use $_() with keys in the existing dashboard.modal.* namespace.

3. **statusLabel map recreated per call** — dashboard/+page.svelte: the Record<string, string> map is an object literal inside the function body. Should be a module-level const (same pattern as STATUS_DOT and STATUS_SHOW_LABEL already are).

4. **testDisplayName duplicated** — defined identically in OrderTestModal.svelte and dashboard/+page.svelte. Extract to shared utility.

## Locations
- cognitest/frontend/src/lib/utils/patients.ts:3
- cognitest/frontend/src/lib/components/OrderTestModal.svelte:8, 205, 227, 250, 254, 277
- cognitest/frontend/src/routes/(app)/dashboard/+page.svelte:56-68
- cognitest/frontend/src/lib/i18n/pt-BR.json and en.json (missing keys)
title: Fix: API_BASE duplicated, hardcoded Portuguese strings, statusLabel map recreated per call

description:
## Issues

1. **Missing covering index for status filter**: The new composite index is (clinician_user_id, created_at DESC). When list_sessions is called with a status filter, PostgreSQL scans the index by clinician_user_id then filters status as a post-scan step. A (clinician_user_id, status, created_at DESC) covering index would eliminate the filter step.
   ```sql
   CREATE INDEX ix_test_sessions_clinician_status_created
   ON test_sessions (clinician_user_id, status, created_at DESC);
   ```

2. **count() fires on every call by default**: skip_count defaults to False, meaning every list_sessions call runs two queries (COUNT + SELECT). The dashboard card uses skip_count=True, but any other caller pays double. Consider changing the default to True and making pagination-aware callers opt in.

3. **UUID(doctor.user_id) conversion per query**: The .filter() call does UUID(doctor.user_id) on every request. Normalize to UUID at the auth layer (DoctorInfo construction) so it is not re-parsed per endpoint.

## Severity
P3 — performance degradation at scale. Not urgent, but the status index is cheap to add.

## Location
- cognitest/backend/app/routers/sessions.py:289 (filter), 298 (skip_count default)
- cognitest/backend/alembic (new migration needed for covering index)
- cognitest/backend/app/auth/dependencies.py (UUID normalization)
title: Performance: list_sessions index doesn't cover status filter + count() fires by default
Fix: dashboard sessions list not refreshed after OrderTestModal creates a session

description:
## What

Extend SessionResponse and the list_sessions endpoint to include the patient's cached_name so the dashboard can show patient names without a second round-trip per session.

## Context

- cognitest/backend/app/routers/sessions.py list_sessions does db.query(TestSession) with no relationship loading
- cognitest/backend/app/schemas/session.py has PatientInfo schema (id, cached_name, cached_dob) but NOT used in SessionResponse
- cognitest/backend/app/models/test_session.py: relationship to Patient already declared (line 54): patient = relationship('Patient', ...)
- TestSession.patient_id is a UUID FK to Patient.id, nullable=True
- Fix: add selectinload(TestSession.patient) to list_sessions query, add patient: PatientInfo | None to SessionResponse

### CRITICAL: Auth + Org Scoping Required (UPDATED)

- list_sessions currently has NO Depends(get_current_doctor) — returns sessions across ALL organizations
- This must be fixed in this bead, not deferred: add Depends(get_current_doctor) to list_sessions
- Add WHERE clinician_user_id = doctor.user_id filter to scope results to the authenticated doctor
- Without this: (a) dashboard shows wrong data, (b) total = query.count() is a full table scan on all sessions, (c) session UUIDs are publicly enumerable (security issue)

### Dashboard: Skip count() for the recent sessions card

- The dashboard card shows 'last 5 sessions' only — pagination metadata (total count) is unnecessary
- For limit=5 dashboard queries, skip the count() round-trip: return rows only, no total/pages
- The full paginated endpoint (for a sessions management page) can retain count()
- Consider: either a query param ?dashboard=true to skip count, or a separate dedicated endpoint

### selectinload vs joinedload — use selectinload here:

- selectinload issues a 2nd SELECT ... WHERE id IN (...) — for limit=5 this means 5 UUIDs in one IN clause (not N+1)
- joinedload uses JOIN and can cause Cartesian explosion when combined with LIMIT
- selectinload = 2 queries total regardless of limit value. Correct choice.

### Options order is critical:

- .options() MUST come before .filter() and before .offset()/.limit()
- Count BEFORE applying offset/limit to avoid pagination breakage

### Composite index: consolidate to 0ekz.5 (UPDATED)

- Do NOT add the composite index migration in this bead
- The composite index on test_sessions belongs in the 0ekz.5 migration only (to avoid duplication)
- If a migration is created in this bead, limit it to schema-only changes

## Implementation

In cognitest/backend/app/schemas/session.py:
  Add patient: PatientInfo | None = None to SessionResponse

In cognitest/backend/app/routers/sessions.py list_sessions:
  1. Add Depends(get_current_doctor) parameter
  2. Add WHERE clinician_user_id = doctor.user_id filter
  3. from sqlalchemy.orm import selectinload
  4. query = db.query(TestSession).options(selectinload(TestSession.patient))
  (options BEFORE filter, count BEFORE offset/limit)
  5. For dashboard-style limit=5 queries: skip count() call

In cognitest/backend/app/models/test_session.py:
  Verify relationship to Patient exists — it should at line 54

## Testing

- [ ] GET /api/v1/sessions/ requires authentication (401 without valid token)
- [ ] Authenticated doctor only sees own sessions (clinician_user_id filter)
- [ ] GET /api/v1/sessions/ returns items with patient.cached_name populated (when patient exists)
- [ ] Sessions with no patient record return patient: null (not 500)
- [ ] Pagination and filtering still work correctly
- [ ] SQL echo confirms exactly 2 queries (not N+1) for a 5-item page

## Validation

- [ ] list_sessions requires get_current_doctor dependency
- [ ] list_sessions filters by clinician_user_id = doctor.user_id
- [ ] SessionResponse.patient.cached_name is a non-null string for sessions with cached patients
- [ ] No N+1 queries (selectinload not lazy load)
- [ ] count() called before offset/limit (pagination correct)

## Files

- cognitest/backend/app/routers/sessions.py
- cognitest/backend/app/schemas/session.py
- cognitest/backend/app/models/test_session.py (verify relationship)
title: Backend: include patient cached_name in sessions list response

description:
## Issue
update_session (sessions.py) accepts any valid SessionStatus value without verifying the current state. A patient holding a valid access_key can POST `{"access_key":"...", "status":"completed"}` on an AWAITING_PAYMENT or PENDING session, bypassing the payment gate. The start, complete, and abandon endpoints share the same gap.

## Severity
P1 CRITICAL — financial bypass. A patient can complete a session before paying.

## Location
- cognitest/backend/app/routers/sessions.py update_session (~line 319), complete_session (~line 501), abandon_session (~line 525)

## Fix
Add an explicit state-machine transition allowlist before any status mutation:
```python
ALLOWED_TRANSITIONS = {
    SessionStatus.PENDING: {SessionStatus.IN_PROGRESS, SessionStatus.ABANDONED},
    SessionStatus.IN_PROGRESS: {SessionStatus.COMPLETED, SessionStatus.ABANDONED},
}
current = session.status
target = SessionStatus(update_data.status)
if target not in ALLOWED_TRANSITIONS.get(current, set()):
    raise HTTPException(422, f"Cannot transition from {current} to {target}")
```
Apply same guard in complete_session (assert IN_PROGRESS) and abandon_session (assert PENDING or IN_PROGRESS).

## Validation Criteria
- [ ] AWAITING_PAYMENT session cannot be set to COMPLETED via update_session
- [ ] PENDING session cannot be set to COMPLETED directly (must go through IN_PROGRESS)
- [ ] IN_PROGRESS session can be set to COMPLETED or ABANDONED
- [ ] Invalid transitions return 422

## Testing Steps
1. Create session with requires_payment=True
2. POST /{session_id} with {access_key, status:completed} — expect 422
3. Start session normally, then complete — expect 200
title: Security: update_session missing status-machine guard — payment bypass

description:
## Issue
SessionResponse includes access_key (session.py:65) which is returned by list_sessions, get_session, update_session, start_session, complete_session, abandon_session. The access_key is the sole auth mechanism for all patient-facing endpoints. Any XSS, browser extension, or log capture of a session list response exposes keys for every session.

## Severity
P1 CRITICAL — access_key exposure enables session takeover for all listed sessions.

## Location
- cognitest/backend/app/schemas/session.py SessionResponse
- cognitest/backend/app/routers/sessions.py list_sessions, get_session

## Fix
Create SessionResponseDoctor schema (omits access_key) for list_sessions and clinician-facing endpoints. Only return access_key in the create_session response (and optionally the single-session GET when authenticated as the session owner).

```python
class SessionResponseDoctor(SessionResponse):
    access_key: ClassVar[None] = None  # excluded
    # or use model_config with exclude
```

## Validation Criteria
- [ ] GET /sessions/ does not include access_key in any item
- [ ] POST /sessions/ response includes access_key (needed to build session_url)
- [ ] GET /sessions/{id} with doctor JWT does not include access_key

## Testing Steps
1. List sessions as authenticated doctor
2. Verify response items have no access_key field
title: Security: access_key exposed in all session list/detail responses

description:
## Issue In OrderTestModal.svelte, the `{#if submitError}` block is opened OUTSIDE and AFTER the `{:else if step === 'confirm'}` closing tag, not nested inside it. This makes the `{:else if step === 'success'}` branch a child of `{#if submitError}` instead of the main step chain. The success screen only renders when submitError is simultaneously non-null — which never happens since submitError is cleared on success. Result: sessions are created server-side but the user sees nothing and the form stays frozen on confirm. ## Severity P1 CRITICAL — session creation appears broken to every user. The success screen is completely unreachable. ## Location - cognitest/frontend/src/lib/components/OrderTestModal.svelte ~lines 254-269 ## Fix Move `{#if submitError}...{/if}` INSIDE the confirm step `
`: ```svelte {:else if step === 'confirm'}
... {#if submitError}

{submitError}

{/if}
{:else if step === 'success'} ... ``` ## Validation Criteria - [ ] After successful session creation, the success step renders - [ ] submitError message appears inside the confirm step on failure - [ ] All 4 steps (patient, test, confirm, success) are reachable ## Testing Steps 1. Open OrderTestModal, fill out all fields, click Confirmar 2. If session creation succeeds, success screen should appear 3. If it fails, error message should appear in the confirm step
title: Bug: {#if submitError} breaks step chain — success screen never renders

description:
## Issue
In OrderTestModal.svelte the debounced search sets `searching = true` before the await, then sets `searching = false` after storing results. The serial guard `if (serial !== searchSerial) return;` returns early WITHOUT resetting searching to false. When a second keystroke fires before the first response, the first callback exits early and `searching` stays true permanently — the spinner never disappears and the empty-state block is blocked.

## Severity
P1 — the patient search UI becomes unusable after typing more than one character quickly.

## Location
- cognitest/frontend/src/lib/components/OrderTestModal.svelte ~lines 39-45

## Fix
```typescript
if (serial !== searchSerial) { searching = false; return; }
```

## Validation Criteria
- [ ] Typing quickly clears the spinner after the last request resolves
- [ ] searchResults shows only the final query's results
- [ ] Spinner does not remain visible after search completes

## Testing Steps
1. Open patient search, type 'ma' quickly followed by 'r'
2. Verify spinner disappears and results for 'mar' are shown
title: Bug: searching spinner stuck forever when serial guard fires

description:
## Issue
OrderTestModal dispatches `close` with `{sessionCreated: false}` from the backdrop click and Escape keydown handlers, even if the user is already on the success step. The dashboard's `on:close` handler skips `loadDashboard()` when `sessionCreated` is false, so the new session never appears in the sessions card if the user dismisses via backdrop or Escape after a successful creation.

## Severity
P1 — the dashboard refresh mechanism (0ekz.19 fix) is bypassed for the majority of dismiss paths.

## Location
- cognitest/frontend/src/lib/components/OrderTestModal.svelte handleBackdropClick, handleKeydown (~lines 108-118)

## Fix
```typescript
function handleBackdropClick() {
    dispatch('close', { sessionCreated: step === 'success' });
}
function handleKeydown(e: KeyboardEvent) {
    if (e.key === 'Escape') dispatch('close', { sessionCreated: step === 'success' });
}
```

## Validation Criteria
- [ ] Clicking backdrop after success step triggers loadDashboard
- [ ] Pressing Escape after success step triggers loadDashboard
- [ ] Clicking backdrop before success step does NOT trigger loadDashboard

## Testing Steps
1. Create a session successfully (reach success step)
2. Press Escape — verify sessions card refreshes
title: Bug: backdrop/Escape key always dispatch sessionCreated:false even after success

description:
## Issue
list_sessions filters only on `clinician_user_id == doctor.user_id_uuid` without an additional `organization_id == org.id` filter. A doctor who changes organizations (or whose user_id is reused across orgs) would see sessions from their previous organization — cross-org data leakage.

## Severity
P1 — HIPAA/LGPD violation. Clinical session data crosses org boundaries.

## Location
- cognitest/backend/app/routers/sessions.py list_sessions (~line 270)

## Fix
Add org resolution and filter:
```python
org = get_ct_org_for_doctor(doctor, db)
query = (
    db.query(TestSession)
    .options(selectinload(TestSession.patient))
    .filter(
        TestSession.clinician_user_id == doctor.user_id_uuid,
        TestSession.organization_id == org.id,  # ADD THIS
    )
)
```

## Validation Criteria
- [ ] Doctor in org A cannot see sessions created under org B (even with same user_id)
- [ ] list_sessions returns only sessions matching both user AND org

## Testing Steps
1. Create session under org A with doctor X
2. Simulate doctor X joining org B
3. List sessions — session from org A should NOT appear
title: Security: list_sessions missing organization_id scope filter

description:
## Issue
get_session_summary (results.py) fetches TestResult rows without eager loading test_definition, then accesses `result.test_definition` inside the loop. SQLAlchemy issues one SELECT per result row — N+1 proportional to the number of tests in the session. Same pattern in the PDF generator.

## Severity
P1 — O(N) queries per report request. A 10-test session fires 10 extra queries. Every clinician viewing results is affected.

## Location
- cognitest/backend/app/routers/results.py lines 103-119
- cognitest/backend/app/services/pdf_generator.py (wherever test_definition is accessed in loop)

## Fix
Add selectinload to the results query:
```python
results = (
    db.query(TestResult)
    .options(selectinload(TestResult.test_definition))
    .filter(TestResult.session_id == session_id)
    .all()
)
```
Apply same option in get_session_results and pdf_generator.

## Validation Criteria
- [ ] get_session_summary triggers exactly 2 DB queries regardless of test count (sessions + results with defs)
- [ ] PDF generation does not trigger N+1 on test_definition

## Testing Steps
1. Enable SQLAlchemy query logging
2. GET /results/session/{id}/summary — count queries
3. Verify count is fixed, not proportional to number of tests
title: Performance: N+1 query on test_definition in results summary and PDF

description:
## Issue
Migration c4b8e1f2d9a7 uses `USING NULL::INTEGER` for the ALTER COLUMN type change. This expression evaluates to NULL for every row regardless of source value. The guard check aborts if non-null rows exist — but if that guard is ever bypassed or the migration is re-applied after a manual data fix, all medsimples_patient_id values would be silently nulled. The correct USING expression preserves actual integer values.

## Severity
P1 — data loss risk on the migration safety guard path.

## Location
- cognitest/backend/alembic/versions/c4b8e1f2d9a7_fix_patient_medsimples_id_to_integer.py lines 64-68

## Fix
Change to:
```sql
ALTER TABLE patients
    ALTER COLUMN medsimples_patient_id TYPE INTEGER
    USING medsimples_patient_id::text::integer;
```
The guard check before this step still protects against non-numeric UUID values — if the cast fails, the migration aborts cleanly. This is strictly safer than USING NULL.

## Validation Criteria
- [ ] Migration converts non-null integer-valued strings correctly
- [ ] Migration aborts cleanly if any value is non-numeric
- [ ] alembic upgrade head succeeds on a clean DB

## Testing Steps
1. Test migration on a DB with some non-null integer-valued medsimples_patient_id rows
2. Verify they survive as integers after migration
title: Migration: USING NULL::INTEGER discards data — should use text::integer cast

description:
## Issue
create_session fetches Patient by UUID with no org filter (line 168), then checks org ownership (line 176). A patient in another org returns 403; a non-existent patient returns 404. This leaks org membership: an attacker enumerating patient UUIDs can infer which belong to which org.

## Location
- cognitest/backend/app/routers/sessions.py ~lines 168-177

## Fix
Add org filter to the initial query so non-members get 404:
```python
patient = db.query(Patient).filter(
    Patient.id == session_data.patient_id,
    Patient.organization_id == org.id,
).first()
if not patient:
    raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail='Patient not found')
```

## Validation Criteria
- [ ] Patient in another org returns 404, not 403
- [ ] Own patient returns normal session creation
title: Security: patient_id path leaks org membership via 403 vs 404 distinction

description:
## Issue
consume_credit (line 190 sessions.py) and db.commit() for the session (line 230) are separate operations. If the process crashes between them, a credit is consumed but no session exists. Credits are also consumed before the custom battery is flushed — a battery ORM error would leave a consumed credit with no session.

## Location
- cognitest/backend/app/routers/sessions.py ~lines 182-230

## Fix
Move consume_credit after db.add(session) but before db.commit() so they are in the same unit of work. Or use a compensating transaction: wrap create_session in a try/except that calls credits_service.refund_credit() on any exception after consume_credit.

## Validation Criteria
- [ ] Crash/exception after consume_credit but before commit does not lose a credit
- [ ] Failed session creation returns the credit
title: Bug: credit consumption not atomic with session creation — credit lost on crash

description:
## What

Build /dashboard route as the post-login landing page for CogniTest doctors. 2x2 card grid (single column on mobile). Replace /commissions as DEFAULT_POST_LOGIN_ROUTE.

Use the frontend-design and design-taste skills for all UI work.

## Context

- Stack: SvelteKit + TypeScript + Tailwind CSS + svelte-i18n
- Existing CSS classes: .card, .btn-primary, .btn-secondary, .btn-lg, .input, .label
- All API calls via PUBLIC_API_BASE_URL (relative /api/v1 in prod)
- CreditsWidget.svelte exists — include it
- Auth layout returns {} — dashboard page must fetch /auth/me in onMount for doctor identity

### Design System (from design-taste-frontend skill)

**Page background:** bg-slate-100 (NOT white — creates visible card separation)
**Card surface:** bg-white rounded-xl border border-slate-200
**Shadow:** shadow-[0_1px_4px_0_rgba(15,23,42,0.06)] hover:shadow-[0_4px_12px_0_rgba(15,23,42,0.08)]
**Grid:** grid grid-cols-1 md:grid-cols-2 gap-5 max-w-4xl mx-auto
**Card padding:** p-6
**Page outer:** px-4 sm:px-6 py-8 min-h-[100dvh] (use 100dvh not h-screen — prevents iOS Safari layout jump)

**Section label:** text-[11px] font-semibold text-slate-400 uppercase tracking-widest
**KPI value:** text-[32px] font-bold text-slate-900 font-mono tabular-nums
**Body text:** text-[13px] text-slate-600
**Card title:** text-sm font-semibold text-slate-700

**Status dot colors (all WCAG AA on white):**
- completed: bg-green-600 (#16a34a, ratio 4.6:1)
- in_progress: bg-primary-600 (#2563eb, ratio 4.7:1)
- awaiting_payment: bg-amber-600 (#d97706, ratio 4.6:1) — NOT amber-500 (fails WCAG)
- abandoned/cancelled: bg-gray-400 (#9ca3af, ratio 2.9:1 FAILS) — pair with text label, never rely on dot alone

**ANTI-EMOJI policy: no emoji anywhere (from design-taste skill)**
**ANTI-EMOJI: replace all emoji with SVG icons**

**NO stagger animations (UPDATED):** Do not implement CSS card entry animations in v1. Cosmetic on a clinical utility tool — add only if users request it.

### Performance

**CRITICAL: Use Promise.all for all dashboard API calls:**
- commissions, sessions, tests/definitions are fully independent
- Sequential awaits = sum of RTTs. Promise.all = slowest of the three (60-70% faster)
- In SvelteKit: use load() in +page.ts or Promise.all in onMount

### File location (UPDATED)

- API client function: cognitest/frontend/src/lib/utils/patients.ts (NOT lib/api/) — matches existing lib/utils/api convention
- Modal: Extract as cognitest/frontend/src/lib/components/OrderTestModal.svelte (NOT inline in +page.svelte — will be hard to extract later)

### Modal patterns (from SvelteKit research)

**Debounce:** setTimeout + clearTimeout, 300ms, min 2 chars before firing
**Focus trap:** keydown listener traversing focusable elements, Escape to close
**Backdrop:** click on backdrop closes; click on modal content does not
**Transition:** fade for backdrop (150ms), fly for modal panel (y: 50, 200ms)
**Clipboard:** navigator.clipboard.writeText() with execCommand fallback

### Cards

#### Card 1: Revenue (top-left) — bg-white
- Fetch GET /api/v1/commissions/current-month
- KPI: large R$ amount with 3px vertical primary-600 accent bar
- Secondary metric: test count
- Error/empty state: 'Dados indisponíveis' — catch and show placeholder, no crash
- Verify this endpoint exists and returns total_revenue + commission_amount before implementing

#### Card 2: Sessões Recentes (top-right) — bg-white
- Fetch GET /api/v1/sessions/?limit=5
- Compact list with status dots + patient.cached_name (or 'Paciente')
- divide-y divide-slate-100 list, truncate long names
- Empty: 'Nenhuma sessão ainda'
- gray-400 dots MUST be accompanied by text label

#### Card 3: Solicitar Teste (bottom-left) — bg-primary-50/40 border-primary-100
- ONLY card with tinted background (signals primary CTA)
- Large 'Solicitar avaliação' btn-primary + optional 'Selecionar paciente existente' btn-secondary
- Opens OrderTestModal component (separate .svelte file)
- Modal flow: search patient (debounced 300ms, min 2 chars) → select → pick test → confirm → show link + copy button
- Patient search calls GET /api/v1/patients/search?q= (from bead 0ekz.1)
- Session create posts with medsimples_patient_id (from bead 0ekz.4)
- On success: auto-copy link to clipboard + show success notice (NOT a toast — inline in modal)
- active:scale-[0.98] on buttons for tactile feedback

#### Card 4: Testes Disponíveis (bottom-right) — bg-white
- Fetch GET /api/v1/tests/definitions?active_only=true
- Pill tags: bg-primary-50 text-primary-700 border border-primary-100 rounded-md
- If domain categories available: 3px left border in category color per pill
- Footer: count of active tests
- Empty state: 'Nenhum teste disponível'

## i18n

Add to cognitest/frontend/src/lib/i18n/pt-BR.json and en.json under 'dashboard' key:
- title: 'Painel' / 'Dashboard'
- revenue.title, revenue.noData, revenue.thisMonth, revenue.commission, revenue.tests
- sessions.title, sessions.empty, sessions.viewAll
- orderTest.title, orderTest.button, orderTest.searchPatient, orderTest.selectTest
- orderTest.confirm, orderTest.success, orderTest.copyLink, orderTest.copied
- orderTest.successMessage
- tests.title, tests.empty
- status.completed, status.in_progress, status.awaiting_payment, status.pending, status.abandoned

## Files

- cognitest/frontend/src/routes/(app)/dashboard/+page.svelte (NEW)
- cognitest/frontend/src/lib/components/OrderTestModal.svelte (NEW — extracted modal)
- cognitest/frontend/src/lib/constants.ts (change DEFAULT_POST_LOGIN_ROUTE to '/dashboard')
- cognitest/frontend/src/lib/i18n/pt-BR.json
- cognitest/frontend/src/lib/i18n/en.json
- cognitest/frontend/src/lib/utils/patients.ts (NEW — search function, in lib/utils/ not lib/api/)

## Dependencies

Blocked by: medsimples-0ekz.1 (patient search endpoint), medsimples-0ekz.2 (session patient names), medsimples-0ekz.4 (session create with MS patient ID)
title: Frontend: /dashboard page — 2x2 cards grid with revenue, sessions, order test, available tests

description:
## Issue
check_eligibility and consume_credit are two separate DB operations. Under concurrent session creation requests, both can read free_tests_remaining=1, both find eligible, both decrement — resulting in free_tests_remaining=-1.

## Location
- cognitest/backend/app/services/credits_service.py consume_credit

## Fix
Use SELECT FOR UPDATE to make check+decrement atomic:
```python
credits = self.db.query(OrgCredits).filter(
    OrgCredits.organization_id == organization_id,
    OrgCredits.free_tests_remaining > 0,
).with_for_update().first()
if not credits:
    raise ValueError('No free credits remaining')
credits.free_tests_remaining -= 1
```

## Validation Criteria
- [ ] Concurrent session creation for same org with 1 credit: only 1 succeeds
- [ ] free_tests_remaining never goes below 0
title: Security: consume_credit TOCTOU — concurrent requests can overdraft free credits

description:
## Issue
get_or_create_by_medsimples_id returns the existing patient record without updating cached_name, cached_dob, or cached_education_years. If a patient's name changes in MedSimples, CogniTest forever shows the original cached name on all sessions and reports.

## Location
- cognitest/backend/app/services/patient_service.py ~line 53

## Fix
On the existing-patient path, update cached fields if the caller provides non-None values:
```python
if existing:
    if cached_name is not None:
        existing.cached_name = cached_name
    if cached_dob is not None:
        existing.cached_dob = cached_dob
    db.flush()
    return existing
```

## Validation Criteria
- [ ] Patient name change in MedSimples is reflected on next session creation
- [ ] No unnecessary flush if no fields changed
title: Bug: existing patient cached_name/dob never updated with fresh MedSimples data

description:
## Issue
The commissions index guard in c4b8e1f2d9a7 queries information_schema.columns WHERE table_name='commissions' without scoping to table_schema='public'. In a multi-schema PostgreSQL cluster, a commissions table in another schema could produce a false positive: the guard passes, the index creation fails because the public.commissions table has different columns.

## Location
- cognitest/backend/alembic/versions/c4b8e1f2d9a7_fix_patient_medsimples_id_to_integer.py ~lines 121-136

## Fix
Add AND table_schema = 'public' to each information_schema.columns subquery in the DO block.

## Validation Criteria
- [ ] Guard correctly identifies columns in the public schema only
- [ ] Migration is safe in multi-schema deployments
title: Migration: information_schema column check missing table_schema scope

description:
## Issue
validateDemoAccess in api.ts manually builds `?key=...` params, then calls `request()` which calls `buildUrl()` which ALSO appends the current access key. The result is a URL like `/sessions/demo/validate?key=abc&key=abc`. The second key value (from buildUrl) is redundant and the first may be stale if currentAccessKey changed.

## Location
- cognitest/frontend/src/lib/utils/api.ts ~lines 409-412

## Fix
Remove the manual params construction entirely:
```typescript
export async function validateDemoAccess(): Promise<{ valid: boolean }> {
    return request<{ valid: boolean }>('/sessions/demo/validate');
}
```
buildUrl will append the current access key automatically.

## Validation Criteria
- [ ] validateDemoAccess sends exactly one key= query param
- [ ] The key value is the current access key from the store
title: Bug: validateDemoAccess double-appends access_key to URL

description:
## Issue
SessionUpdate.status is typed as str | None, so Pydantic accepts any string. The router does manual SessionStatus() coercion with try/except ValueError. Using the enum type directly gives automatic 422 validation with correct OpenAPI docs.

## Location
- cognitest/backend/app/schemas/session.py ~line 41

## Fix
```python
status: SessionStatus | None = None
```
Remove the manual try/except coercion in update_session.

## Validation Criteria
- [ ] Invalid status value returns 422 with clear Pydantic error
- [ ] OpenAPI schema documents valid status values
title: Quality: SessionUpdate.status should be SessionStatus enum, not str

description:
## Issue
STATUS_LABEL map in dashboard/+page.svelte has 5 hardcoded Portuguese strings (Concluído, Em andamento, Aguardando pagamento, etc.) that are not run through the svelte-i18n $_() function. The dashboard always displays Portuguese regardless of locale.

## Location
- cognitest/frontend/src/routes/(app)/dashboard/+page.svelte STATUS_LABEL const

## Fix
Add keys under dashboard.sessions.status.* in both pt-BR.json and en.json, and look them up with $_().

## Validation Criteria
- [ ] Status labels display in the active locale
- [ ] Both pt-BR.json and en.json have all 5 status keys
title: Quality: status labels hardcoded in Portuguese — not i18n-wrapped

description:
## Issue
searchPatients in patients.ts catches all errors and returns []. A 401, 500, or network failure is indistinguishable from 'no results found' by the calling component. The user sees an empty list with no indication that search is broken.

## Location
- cognitest/frontend/src/lib/utils/patients.ts ~lines 12-18

## Fix
Return a discriminated result or re-throw non-404 errors so OrderTestModal can show an error state for search failures vs empty results.

## Validation Criteria
- [ ] 401 during search shows an error message, not an empty list
- [ ] 500 during search shows an error message
title: Quality: patients.ts silently returns [] for auth failures and server errors

description:
## Issue
Migration c4b8e1f2d9a7 creates ix_test_sessions_clinician_created (clinician_user_id, created_at DESC). Migration a3f7c912e845 adds the covering index (clinician_user_id, status, created_at DESC) which is a strict superset. The 2-column index is now largely redundant, causing double write amplification on test_sessions inserts.

## Location
- cognitest/backend/alembic/versions/ (next migration)

## Fix
Add a new migration that drops ix_test_sessions_clinician_created. Verify via EXPLAIN that the covering index is used for all list_sessions queries (with and without status filter).

## Validation Criteria
- [ ] alembic upgrade head succeeds
- [ ] EXPLAIN on list_sessions (with and without status filter) uses only the covering index
- [ ] Benchmark shows no regression in query performance
title: Quality: redundant ix_test_sessions_clinician_created index after covering index added

description:
## Issue

The PostgreSQL enum types created by the initial migration use UPPERCASE values, but the Python model enums use lowercase string values. This mismatch will cause every INSERT to `test_sessions`, `test_results`, and `cognitest_payments` to fail with `invalid input value for enum` in production.

Confirmed in dev DB:
- `sessionstatus`: {PENDING, AWAITING_PAYMENT, IN_PROGRESS, COMPLETED, ABANDONED}
- `resultstatus`: {PENDING, IN_PROGRESS, COMPLETED, SKIPPED}
- `paymentstatus`: {AWAITING_PAYMENT, PAID, FAILED, REFUNDED}

Python model (`test_session.py:16`):
```python
class SessionStatus(str, enum.Enum):
    PENDING = "pending"  # lowercase
    IN_PROGRESS = "in_progress"  # lowercase
```

Migration (`b30cbbeb8cdf_initial_schema.py:170`):
```python
sa.Column('status', sa.Enum('PENDING', 'AWAITING_PAYMENT', ..., name='sessionstatus'))
```

## Severity
P1 CRITICAL — No sessions can be created in any environment that ran the migration. Dev DB is currently empty (no sessions yet) which is why this hasn't been caught.

## Location
- `cognitest/backend/alembic/versions/b30cbbeb8cdf_initial_schema.py:170, 195, 235`
- `cognitest/backend/app/models/test_session.py:13-20`
- `cognitest/backend/app/models/test_result.py:23-29`
- `cognitest/backend/app/models/cognitest_payment.py:22-29`

## Fix Options
1. Change Python enum values to uppercase (breaking change to API responses)
2. Write a migration that drops and recreates enum types with lowercase values (preferred — keeps API stable)

## Validation Criteria
- [ ] `INSERT INTO test_sessions (status, ...) VALUES ('pending', ...)` succeeds
- [ ] `SessionStatus.PENDING.value == 'pending'` matches DB enum value
- [ ] Existing test data is unaffected (DB is currently empty)

## Testing Steps
1. `psql` to dev DB: `SELECT enum_range(NULL::sessionstatus);` — should show lowercase
2. Run `POST /api/v1/sessions/` from dashboard — should return 200 not 500
title: Bug: DB enums are UPPERCASE but Python SessionStatus uses lowercase — all session INSERTs will fail

description:
## Issue

`PATCH /sessions/{session_id}` has no `@limiter.limit()` decorator and no `get_current_doctor` auth dependency. The only protection is knowledge of `access_key`. Two separate problems:

**1. No rate limiting:** An attacker with a session UUID can attempt unlimited requests. While brute-forcing a 131-bit access key is infeasible, the lack of rate limiting is a defense-in-depth failure. All other mutating endpoints have rate limiters.

**2. Unbounded device_info:** `SessionUpdate.device_info: dict | None` has no size constraint. A client can send arbitrarily large JSON payloads that get stored verbatim via `json.dumps(update_data.device_info)`.

## Severity
P1 — Missing rate limit on an unauthenticated write endpoint; potential DB write amplification via device_info.

## Location
- `cognitest/backend/app/routers/sessions.py:330` (missing @limiter.limit decorator)
- `cognitest/backend/app/schemas/session.py:41` (device_info dict with no size bound)

## Fix
Add `@limiter.limit("20/minute")` decorator. Add a Pydantic model with bounded fields for `device_info` instead of raw `dict`.

## Validation Criteria
- [ ] Sending >20 PATCH requests/minute from same IP returns 429
- [ ] Sending device_info with 1MB payload returns 422
title: Security: update_session has no rate limit and unbounded device_info payload

description:
## What

Extend POST /api/v1/sessions/ to accept a medsimples_patient_id (integer) in addition to existing patient_id (UUID). When medsimples_patient_id is provided, backend looks up or creates a CogniTest Patient record, then uses that Patient.id for session creation.

## Context

- cognitest/backend/app/schemas/session.py SessionCreate currently requires patient_id: UUID
- cognitest/backend/app/models/patient.py Patient has medsimples_patient_id field and UniqueConstraint(organization_id, medsimples_patient_id)
- Dashboard patient search returns MedSimples integer IDs — frontend has no way to know the CogniTest Patient UUID
- Organization linking: doctor.org_id (str from JWT) is the MedSimples org integer ID

### CRITICAL Security Fixes

**CRITICAL: create_session has no authentication — IDOR on patient_id (sessions.py:120)**
- Current create_session has no get_current_doctor dependency
- Any caller who knows a valid CogniTest patient.id UUID can create sessions for any patient
- Fix: add Depends(get_current_doctor) to create_session AND enforce patient.organization_id == doctor's org after patient lookup

**CRITICAL: get_or_create_default_org is wrong for multi-tenant (sessions.py:33)**
- db.query(Organization).first() returns whichever org comes first — NOT the authenticated doctor's org
- Fix: derive organization_id from get_current_doctor() — look up CogniTest Organization by medsimples_org_id == int(doctor.org_id)
- Only use the default org fallback in single-org dev mode, guarded by settings.dev_mode flag

**CRITICAL: org_id type-safety**
- doctor.org_id is Optional[str] from the JWT claim (e.g. '42')
- Organization.medsimples_org_id is Integer
- Cross-org check MUST use int(doctor.org_id) for comparison — '42' == 42 is False in Python and the guard silently fails
- Add explicit 403 guard if doctor.org_id is None before attempting org lookup

**TOCTOU in get-or-create Patient — must use IntegrityError retry:**
- The UNIQUE(org_id, medsimples_patient_id) constraint prevents data corruption from concurrent requests
- BUT without catching IntegrityError and retrying with SELECT, the second concurrent request returns 500
- Correct pattern:
    try:
        db.add(Patient(...))
        db.flush()
    except IntegrityError:
        db.rollback()
        patient = db.query(Patient).filter_by(org_id=org_id, medsimples_patient_id=ms_patient_id).one()
- Bound retry to 1-2 attempts maximum; do not retry infinitely

**SSRF / httpx — MedSimples API call outside transaction:**
- MedSimples API call to fetch patient name/dob must happen BEFORE the DB transaction (not inside it)
- Use self._shared_client (not per-request httpx.AsyncClient pattern from get_patient())

### Inline resolution pattern (UPDATED: replaces XOR Pydantic validator)

Instead of a model_validator XOR in the schema, use inline resolution at the top of the route handler:
  1. If medsimples_patient_id is provided: resolve to local patient_id at handler start
  2. Proceed with normal session creation using patient_id
  This is simpler, produces clearer error messages, and avoids model_validator(mode='before') complexity.
  patient_id remains required in the schema; route handler resolves medsimples_patient_id before validation.

### Org resolution utility

Create a shared utility function: get_ct_org_for_doctor(doctor: DoctorInfo, db: Session) -> Organization
  - Queries Organization by medsimples_org_id == int(doctor.org_id)
  - Raises 403 if doctor.org_id is None
  - Raises 422 if no matching Organization found
  - Call this from session creation and any future endpoints needing org context
  - This pattern will repeat; extract it rather than inline per endpoint

### Architecture: service layer placement

- The get-or-create Patient logic MUST go in a service layer or repository method
- Do NOT embed it directly in the router (violates established layered architecture: router -> service -> repository)
- Create or use session_service.py / patient_service.py for this logic

### Async httpx for MedSimplesClient:

- get_patient() currently creates a new httpx.AsyncClient per call (TLS handshake cost per call)
- Pass request.app.state.http_client through; use self._shared_client
- MedSimples API call to fetch name/dob should run BEFORE starting the DB transaction

## Implementation

In cognitest/backend/app/schemas/session.py SessionCreate:
  Add: medsimples_patient_id: int | None = None
  (No XOR validator — resolution handled inline in route handler)

In cognitest/backend/app/routers/sessions.py create_session:
  1. Add Depends(get_current_doctor) — REQUIRED for IDOR fix
  2. If medsimples_patient_id provided:
     a. Resolve org using get_ct_org_for_doctor(doctor, db) utility
        - Raise 422 if org not found
        - Allow dev_mode fallback only if settings.dev_mode is True
     b. Fetch patient data from MedSimples BEFORE transaction (async, don't block transaction)
        - Use self._shared_client in medsimples_client
     c. get-or-create Patient with IntegrityError retry pattern (max 2 attempts)
        - This logic in service layer, not directly in router
  3. Enforce patient.organization_id == ct_org.id after resolution
  4. Use patient.id as session patient_id

Extract: get_ct_org_for_doctor() utility for reuse across endpoints

## Testing

- [ ] POST /sessions/ with medsimples_patient_id=8 creates session and new Patient record
- [ ] Second call with same medsimples_patient_id reuses existing Patient (no duplicate)
- [ ] Concurrent requests for same medsimples_patient_id: both succeed (no 500)
- [ ] POST /sessions/ with patient_id (UUID) still works unchanged
- [ ] POST /sessions/ with neither patient_id nor medsimples_patient_id returns 422
- [ ] Unauthenticated POST /sessions/ returns 401 (not 422)
- [ ] Doctor from org A cannot create session for patient in org B (IDOR test)
- [ ] doctor.org_id = None returns 403 (not AttributeError)
- [ ] If MedSimples fetch fails, session still created with cached_name=None

## Validation

- [ ] Session created successfully with medsimples_patient_id input
- [ ] Patient.cached_name populated from MedSimples fetch
- [ ] No duplicate Patient records for same (org, medsimples_patient_id) even under concurrent load
- [ ] organization_id derived from authenticated doctor, not db.query(Organization).first()
- [ ] int(doctor.org_id) used in comparison (type-safe)

## Files

- cognitest/backend/app/schemas/session.py
- cognitest/backend/app/routers/sessions.py
- cognitest/backend/app/models/patient.py (verify medsimples_patient_id field)
- cognitest/backend/app/services/medsimples_client.py (use existing get_patient method)
- cognitest/backend/app/utils/org_utils.py or similar (NEW — get_ct_org_for_doctor utility)

## Dependencies

- Requires medsimples-0ekz.5 (UUID→Integer migration) for Organization.medsimples_org_id lookup to work
title: Backend: SessionCreate accepts medsimples_patient_id, resolves or creates CogniTest Patient

description:
## Issue

`update_session` has two separate status checks causing a logic bug:

```python
# Block 1 (line 346): converts status, checks transition, raises 422 if invalid
if update_data.status is not None:
    target = SessionStatus(update_data.status)  # raises ValueError or succeeds
    if target not in ALLOWED_TRANSITIONS.get(session.status, set()):
        raise HTTPException(422, ...)

# Block 2 (line 354): redundant check with wrong operator
if update_data.status:   # truthiness, not identity — wrong
    try:
        new_status = SessionStatus(update_data.status)  # never raises here (already validated above)
        ...
    except ValueError:
        raise HTTPException(400, "Invalid status value")  # UNREACHABLE
```

The `ValueError` catch is unreachable because Block 1 already successfully converted the value. If it raises in Block 1, execution never reaches Block 2. If it succeeds, Block 2 won't raise either.

The `if update_data.status:` uses truthiness instead of `is not None` — if status were an empty string (won't happen with enums but shows the pattern is wrong), it would pass Block 1 but skip Block 2.

## Severity
P1 — Logic defect in the core session state machine endpoint.

## Location
- `cognitest/backend/app/routers/sessions.py:346-370`

## Fix
Merge into one block:
```python
if update_data.status is not None:
    target = SessionStatus(update_data.status)
    if target not in ALLOWED_TRANSITIONS.get(session.status, set()):
        raise HTTPException(422, ...)
    session.status = target
    if target == SessionStatus.IN_PROGRESS and not session.started_at:
        session.started_at = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
    elif target == SessionStatus.COMPLETED:
        session.completed_at = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
```

## Validation Criteria
- [ ] Valid status transition succeeds
- [ ] Invalid status value returns 422 (not 400)
- [ ] Invalid transition returns 422
title: Bug: update_session has double status check with unreachable ValueError catch

description:
## Issue

`MedSimplesClient.__init__` accepts a shared `httpx.AsyncClient` and stores it as `self._shared_client`, but 8 of ~16 methods ignore it and open `async with httpx.AsyncClient()` unconditionally — creating and tearing down a full TLS connection per call.

Affected methods (all use `async with httpx.AsyncClient()` regardless of `self._shared_client`):
- `register()`
- `validate_token()`
- `get_patient()` — called on every session create
- `get_doctor()` — called by NotificationService
- `send_results_webhook()`
- `get_organization()`
- `get_org_stripe_payment_entry()`
- `void_invoice_payment()` + `record_payment()` — `@retry` decorated, creates new client per retry

Additionally, `NotificationService.__init__` instantiates `MedSimplesClient()` with no argument (line 30), so it can never use the shared client regardless.

The newer methods (`search_patients`, `create_invite`, `login`, etc.) correctly check `self._shared_client is not None` first.

## Severity
P1 — Each call to `create_session` triggers at minimum one `get_patient` call + notification calls, all creating new TLS connections. Under load this exhausts file descriptors and adds hundreds of ms latency per session.

## Location
- `cognitest/backend/app/services/medsimples_client.py:62, 84, 110, 137, 164, 191, 218, 473, 559`
- `cognitest/backend/app/services/notification_service.py:30`

## Fix
Apply the existing guard pattern to all affected methods:
```python
if self._shared_client is not None:
    response = await self._shared_client.get(url, ...)
else:
    async with httpx.AsyncClient(timeout=10.0) as client:
        response = await client.get(url, ...)
```

Pass `http_client=request.app.state.http_client` when constructing `NotificationService` in the router, and add `http_client` parameter to `NotificationService.__init__`.

## Validation Criteria
- [ ] `create_session` with a `get_patient` call reuses the shared client connection pool
- [ ] `NotificationService` passes shared client to `MedSimplesClient`
title: Bug: MedSimplesClient — 8 methods ignore self._shared_client, create new httpx client per call
Bug: loading=true in loadDashboard causes full-page spinner after successful session creation

description:
## Issue

`notification_service.py:802` does:
```python
doctor_data = await self.medsimples.get_doctor(
    int(str(session.clinician_user_id)), ms_org_id
)
```

`session.clinician_user_id` is stored as a UUID. When the doctor's MedSimples `user_id` claim is an integer like `42`, `DoctorInfo` converts it to a UUID5: `uuid5(NAMESPACE_OID, 'medsimples-user:42')`. This UUID5 value (e.g. `a1b2c3d4-...`) cannot be parsed back to an integer — `int(str(uuid5_value))` raises `ValueError`.

The result is that WhatsApp notifications silently fail for all sessions because the notification service can't fetch the doctor's info. The try/except in `create_session` swallows this error (line 240), so it fails silently.

`patient.medsimples_patient_id:782` has the same int cast: `int(str(session.patient.medsimples_patient_id))` — but `Patient.medsimples_patient_id` is stored as an `Integer` column, so `str()` then `int()` round-trips correctly. Only `clinician_user_id` is affected.

## Severity
P1 — Silent notification failure. Doctors won't know WhatsApp notifications failed; patients won't receive test links.

## Location
- `cognitest/backend/app/services/notification_service.py:801-802`
- `cognitest/backend/app/auth/dependencies.py:28-32` (UUID5 derivation)

## Fix
Add `clinician_medsimples_user_id: Integer` column to `TestSession` model, populated from `doctor.user_id` (the original integer) at session creation. Use this column in `notification_service` instead of reversing the UUID5.

Alternatively, store the original integer in `DoctorInfo` and use it directly.

## Validation Criteria
- [ ] Create a session as a doctor with integer MedSimples user_id
- [ ] WhatsApp notification is sent (no ValueError in logs)
- [ ] `notification_service` successfully fetches doctor data from MedSimples
title: Bug: notification_service.py:802 int(str(clinician_user_id)) raises ValueError for UUID5-derived IDs

description:
## Issue

`validate_access_key()` in `sessions.py:72-73` has an unconditional bypass:
```python
if settings.dev_access_key and key == settings.dev_access_key:
    return  # skips all access_key checks
```

This bypass applies to: `get_session`, `update_session`, `submit_consent`, `get_consent`, `start_session`, `complete_session`, `abandon_session`. If `DEV_ACCESS_KEY` is accidentally set in production, a single leaked key grants full read/write access to every test session across all organizations.

There is no guard like `if settings.env != 'production'`, and settings.py documents it only as 'Set in .env to bypass session key validation' with no production warning.

## Severity
P2 — Requires misconfiguration to exploit, but impact is complete session data compromise across all tenants.

## Location
- `cognitest/backend/app/routers/sessions.py:72-73`
- `cognitest/backend/app/settings.py:56`

## Fix
Add an environment guard:
```python
if settings.dev_access_key and key == settings.dev_access_key:
    if settings.env == 'production':
        logger.error('DEV_ACCESS_KEY is set in production — ignoring bypass')
    else:
        return
```
Or raise a startup error if both `DEV_ACCESS_KEY` and `env=production` are set.

## Validation Criteria
- [ ] With `env=production` and `DEV_ACCESS_KEY` set, dev key bypass is rejected
- [ ] Warning/error logged at startup if `DEV_ACCESS_KEY` set in production
title: Security: dev_access_key has no production guard — leaked key grants full access to all sessions

description:
## Issue

`OrderTestModal` sends only `medsimples_patient_id` + `test_ids` in session creation:
```typescript
body: JSON.stringify({
    medsimples_patient_id: selectedPatient.id,
    test_ids: [selectedTest.id]
    // requires_payment defaults to False
    // test_code not sent
})
```

Because `requires_payment` defaults to `False` in `SessionCreate`, sessions created from the dashboard:
- Always get `PENDING` status (not `AWAITING_PAYMENT`)
- Never trigger credit consumption (credit check is gated on `session_data.requires_payment`)
- Never send WhatsApp notification to the patient

The patient receives no notification and must be given the session link manually. This may be intentional for v1 (doctor-sends-link flow) but is undocumented and diverges from the backend's designed payment flow.

## Severity
P2 — Silent behavioral gap between documented backend contract and actual UI usage.

## Location
- `cognitest/frontend/src/lib/components/OrderTestModal.svelte:73-81`

## Fix
Either:
1. Document the intent (add comment: sessions from dashboard are always manual-link flow)
2. Add a payment toggle in the Confirm step, and send `requires_payment: true` + `test_code` when enabled

## Validation Criteria
- [ ] Behavior is documented OR payment toggle is implemented
- [ ] When requires_payment=true, WhatsApp notification is sent
title: Fix: OrderTestModal omits requires_payment/test_code — payment path and notifications unreachable from dashboard

description:
## Issue

In `OrderTestModal.svelte:102`, double-clicking 'Copy link' overwrites `copyLinkTimeout` without cancelling the first timer:
```typescript
copyLinkTimeout = setTimeout(() => (linkCopied = false), 2000);
// If clicked again < 2s later, first timer still fires at its original time
```

The first timer fires at 2s from the first click, resetting `linkCopied = false` while the user expects 2 full seconds from their last click.

## Severity
P2 — Minor UX bug, obvious regression when copying the link multiple times.

## Location
- `cognitest/frontend/src/lib/components/OrderTestModal.svelte:102`

## Fix
```typescript
if (copyLinkTimeout) clearTimeout(copyLinkTimeout);
copyLinkTimeout = setTimeout(() => (linkCopied = false), 2000);
```

## Validation Criteria
- [ ] Clicking Copy twice within 2s resets the 2s timer from the second click
title: Fix: copyLinkTimeout overwritten without clearing previous — feedback resets at wrong time

description:
## Issue

`test_sessions.patient_id` FK references `patients.id` with no `ON DELETE` clause (defaults to RESTRICT). Same for `consents.patient_id` and `consents.session_id`.

`patients` is a cache of MedSimples data. If any sync/cleanup operation tries to DELETE a patient record and re-insert it with fresh data, the FK RESTRICT will block the DELETE as long as any session references that patient. This silently traps stale patient cache records forever.

## Severity
P2 — Blocks any future patient data refresh strategy that uses DELETE+INSERT pattern.

## Location
- `cognitest/backend/alembic/versions/b30cbbeb8cdf_initial_schema.py:179, 225-226`

## Fix
Add a migration adding `ON DELETE RESTRICT` with a comment documenting the intent, OR switch patients cache refresh to UPDATE (never DELETE) and document that constraint explicitly.

## Validation Criteria
- [ ] Intent is documented in migration comment
- [ ] Patient cache refresh path (if any) uses UPDATE not DELETE+INSERT
title: Fix: missing ON DELETE behavior on patients/consents FKs blocks patient cache refresh

description:
## Issue

`cognitest_payments.iof_amount` and `net_amount` are `NOT NULL Numeric(10,2)` with no CHECK constraints at DB level. A refund bug or accounting error could persist negative amounts silently.

## Severity
P2 — Financial data integrity risk; negative amounts could affect payment reconciliation reports.

## Location
- `cognitest/backend/app/models/cognitest_payment.py:43-45`
- `cognitest/backend/alembic/versions/b30cbbeb8cdf_initial_schema.py:193-194`

## Fix
Add migration:
```sql
ALTER TABLE cognitest_payments ADD CONSTRAINT chk_iof_amount_non_negative CHECK (iof_amount >= 0);
ALTER TABLE cognitest_payments ADD CONSTRAINT chk_net_amount_positive CHECK (net_amount > 0);
```

## Validation Criteria
- [ ] INSERT with negative iof_amount raises constraint violation
- [ ] INSERT with negative net_amount raises constraint violation
title: Fix: financial columns iof_amount/net_amount lack CHECK constraints preventing negative values

description:
## Issue

`create_session` in `routers/sessions.py` is 160 lines and directly orchestrates: patient resolution, custom battery creation, credit eligibility check, credit consumption, session DB write, and notification dispatch. This violates the declared layered architecture (routers → services → repositories).

A `SessionService` already exists at `cognitest/backend/app/services/session_service.py` with lifecycle methods, but the router bypasses it entirely and duplicates session state machine logic inline.

## Severity
P2 — Maintainability debt. Any change to credit logic, battery naming, or notification triggers requires editing the 160-line handler. Unit testing is impossible without a full ASGI stack.

## Location
- `cognitest/backend/app/routers/sessions.py:102-260`
- `cognitest/backend/app/services/session_service.py` (underused)

## Fix
Extract `SessionService.create_session(session_data, doctor, db, http_client)` owning: patient resolution, battery creation, credit check+consume (atomically), session insert, notification dispatch. Router becomes: validate → delegate → return HTTP response.

## Validation Criteria
- [ ] Router `create_session` handler is < 30 lines
- [ ] All business logic is in `SessionService`
- [ ] Behavior is identical to current implementation
title: Refactor: create_session router handler is 160 lines — extract to SessionService

description:
## What

Change Organization.medsimples_org_id from UUID to Integer and Patient.medsimples_patient_id from UUID to Integer. Add Alembic migration. Also add composite indexes for query performance.

## Context

- cognitest/backend/app/models/organization.py: medsimples_org_id = Column(UUID(), nullable=True, unique=True, index=True)
  NOTE: Verify actual DB column type before writing migration — the model may already show Integer but the DB migration may have been missed
- cognitest/backend/app/models/patient.py: medsimples_patient_id = Column(UUID(), nullable=True)
- MedSimples org IDs are integers (e.g. 36). JWT org_id claim is '36' (string of integer).
- Organization.medsimples_org_id lookup in session create (0ekz.4) requires this to be Integer
- Same root cause as medsimples-jsx8 (doctor_id/organization_id UUID mismatch)
- Check alembic history — latest is 07c4a1e01d85. New migration must come after that.
- Commission.organization_id is a FK to organizations.id (UUID PK) — that is fine, don't change it

### Pre-migration Verification (CRITICAL)

Before writing the migration:
1. Run: alembic current — check which migrations have run
2. Check the actual DB column type for organizations.medsimples_org_id and patients.medsimples_patient_id
3. If organizations.medsimples_org_id is already Integer in the DB, skip that column from the migration
4. Only migrate columns that are actually UUID type in the DB

### Correct Alembic pattern for UUID→Integer type change:

- Use op.execute() with raw SQL, NOT op.alter_column() — the latter cannot handle USING clause
- Always wrap in: if connection.dialect.name == 'postgresql':
- Order: drop constraints/indexes → alter type → recreate constraints/indexes
- For NULL-only columns: USING NULL::INTEGER (no complex casting needed)
- Add safety check before altering: SELECT COUNT(*) WHERE column IS NOT NULL — raise RuntimeError if non-zero

### Partial unique index for nullable columns:

Add WHERE column IS NOT NULL to unique constraint so NULLs don't conflict:
  ALTER TABLE organizations ADD CONSTRAINT organizations_medsimples_org_id_key
  UNIQUE (medsimples_org_id) WHERE medsimples_org_id IS NOT NULL

### Downgrade: leave empty (lossy conversion):

UUID→Integer is irreversible without data loss. Document and leave downgrade() as pass.

## Performance Indexes (UPDATED: additional indexes added from review)

Add ALL of the following in this migration:

1. **Composite index on test_sessions (status, created_at)** — consolidate here from 0ekz.2 description
   - Allows Postgres to satisfy both status filter AND created_at sort in one index scan
   - CREATE INDEX ix_test_sessions_status_created_at ON test_sessions (status, created_at)

2. **Composite index on test_sessions (clinician_user_id, created_at DESC)** — NEW from performance review
   - Dashboard session list filters by clinician_user_id, orders by created_at DESC
   - Without this: full table scan per dashboard load as sessions accumulate
   - CREATE INDEX ix_test_sessions_clinician_created ON test_sessions (clinician_user_id, created_at DESC)

3. **Composite index on commissions (doctor_id, period_year, period_month)** — NEW from performance review
   - current-month summary query filters by all three; individual indexes are insufficient at scale
   - CREATE INDEX ix_commissions_doctor_period ON commissions (doctor_id, period_year, period_month)

## Implementation

1. In cognitest/backend/app/models/organization.py:
   medsimples_org_id = Column(Integer, nullable=True, unique=True, index=True)

2. In cognitest/backend/app/models/patient.py:
   medsimples_patient_id = Column(Integer, nullable=True)

3. New alembic migration (after 07c4a1e01d85):
   - Pre-flight: check alembic current + DB column types
   - Safety check: verify no non-NULL values exist in columns being altered
   - Drop unique constraint + index on organizations.medsimples_org_id (only if UUID in DB)
   - Drop unique constraint + index on patients.medsimples_patient_id (if any)
   - ALTER COLUMN to INTEGER using USING NULL::INTEGER (only for columns confirmed as UUID in DB)
   - Recreate unique constraints as partial (WHERE IS NOT NULL)
   - Recreate indexes
   - Add: ix_test_sessions_status_created_at
   - Add: ix_test_sessions_clinician_created (clinician_user_id, created_at DESC)
   - Add: ix_commissions_doctor_period (doctor_id, period_year, period_month)
   - downgrade: pass (document irreversibility)

## Testing

- [ ] alembic upgrade head succeeds
- [ ] Organization can be looked up by medsimples_org_id=36 (integer)
- [ ] Patient can be looked up by medsimples_patient_id=8 (integer)
- [ ] alembic downgrade -1 does not crash (pass is valid)
- [ ] EXPLAIN on sessions list query shows index scan on ix_test_sessions_clinician_created
- [ ] EXPLAIN on commissions current-month shows index scan on ix_commissions_doctor_period

## Validation

- [ ] Organization.medsimples_org_id is Integer type in DB
- [ ] Patient.medsimples_patient_id is Integer type in DB
- [ ] Unique constraint preserved (partial, WHERE IS NOT NULL)
- [ ] Existing rows unaffected (dev DB is empty for these tables)
- [ ] All 3 performance indexes created

## Files

- cognitest/backend/app/models/organization.py
- cognitest/backend/app/models/patient.py
- cognitest/backend/alembic/versions/019_fix_medsimples_id_types_to_integer.py (NEW)
title: Backend: fix Organization.medsimples_org_id from UUID to Integer + migration

description:
## Issue

`list_sessions` accepts `status: str | None` as a query parameter and filters directly:
```python
if status:
    query = query.filter(TestSession.status == status)
```

An invalid value like `?status=invalid` silently returns an empty list instead of a 400 error. The parameter should be typed as `SessionStatus | None` so FastAPI validates and rejects invalid values with a proper 422.

## Severity
P2 — Silent error masking; clients with typos in status values get empty responses with no indication of the mistake.

## Location
- `cognitest/backend/app/routers/sessions.py:268, 288`

## Fix
Change `status: str | None = None` to `status: SessionStatus | None = None` in the function signature.

## Validation Criteria
- [ ] `GET /sessions/?status=invalid` returns 422
- [ ] `GET /sessions/?status=pending` (after enum fix) returns sessions correctly
title: Quality: status query param in list_sessions accepts invalid values silently — returns empty instead of 400

description:
## Issue

`consent_text_version` on the `Consent` model is `nullable=True`. Under LGPD (Brazil's privacy law), the exact version of the consent text shown at time of consent must be auditable. Records with NULL `consent_text_version` cannot prove which version was agreed to.

## Location
- `cognitest/backend/app/models/consent.py:24`

## Fix
Make `consent_text_version` NOT NULL and default to the current version string at the application layer before insert. Add migration.

## Validation Criteria
- [ ] INSERT without consent_text_version raises constraint violation
- [ ] All new consents have a non-null version string
title: Bug: consent_text_version nullable breaks LGPD audit trail

description:
## Issue

There is no unique constraint on `consents.session_id`. A patient can theoretically submit multiple consent records for the same session. The `TestSession` backref is named `consent` (singular) but returns a list, contradicting the one-consent-per-session intent.

## Location
- `cognitest/backend/app/models/consent.py:17`
- `cognitest/backend/alembic/versions/b30cbbeb8cdf_initial_schema.py:212`

## Fix
Add `UniqueConstraint('session_id', name='uq_consents_session_id')` and a migration.

## Validation Criteria
- [ ] Second consent INSERT for same session_id raises IntegrityError
title: Quality: no UNIQUE constraint on consents(session_id) — duplicate consents possible

description:
## Issue

`create_session` returns `access_key` twice in the response:
1. As `response.access_key` (raw string field)
2. Embedded in `response.session_url = f'...?key={session.access_key}'`

Any HTTP access log that captures response bodies logs the key in two places. Prefer one canonical location.

## Location
- `cognitest/backend/app/routers/sessions.py:253`
- `cognitest/backend/app/schemas/session.py:65`

## Fix
Either remove the standalone `access_key` field from `SessionResponse` (clients extract it from `session_url`), or omit the key from `session_url` and deliver only via `access_key`. Either is fine; consistency is the goal.

## Validation Criteria
- [ ] access_key appears exactly once in create_session response
title: Quality: access_key double-exposed in create_session response (raw field + embedded in session_url)

description:
## Issue

`testDisplayName(t)` falls back through 4 optional fields ending at `t.id` (a UUID string). If the backend returns test definitions without any display field, users see UUID strings in the dashboard cards and order modal.

## Location
- `cognitest/frontend/src/lib/utils/tests.ts`

## Fix
Enforce `display_name` is always populated in `GET /tests/definitions` serialization. Tighten `TestDef` type to `{ id: string; display_name: string }`.

## Validation Criteria
- [ ] All test definitions in API response have non-empty display_name
- [ ] No UUID strings visible in UI test lists
title: Quality: testDisplayName falls back to raw UUID — enforce display_name server-side
