description:
## Issue
All phone validation uses hardcoded default country code '55' (Brazil). Organization model has default_tel_country_code field (models.py:55, organization.py:87, 124), but it's never used in phone validation. Patient/Doctor schemas should respect organization's configured default, not hardcode '55'.

## Severity
Low - Feature not fully implemented. Works fine for Brazilian organizations (majority case), but organizations in other countries can't set their preferred default country code.

## Location
- Hardcoded: patient.py:36,40,42 (default='55'), doctor.py:38,42,44 (default='55')
- Hardcoded: patient.py:72,76 (fallback '55'), doctor.py:76 (fallback '55')
- Organization field exists: models.py:55, organization.py:87,124
- Never used in validation

## Why This Matters
- Organization.default_tel_country_code is dead code
- International organizations forced to use Brazil as default
- Feature was added but not wired up
- Migration 3dabb63d091a added field but no code uses it
- Inconsistent with design intent (why else have org-level config?)

## Validation Criteria
- [ ] Phone validators receive organization_id or current_user context
- [ ] Default country code fetched from organization.default_tel_country_code
- [ ] Fallback to '55' if organization not found
- [ ] Update schema validators to accept org context
- [ ] Test: Org with default_tel_country_code='1' → phone defaults to US
- [ ] Test: Org with default_tel_country_code='351' → phone defaults to Portugal

## Testing Steps
1. Pass organization context to validators (via dependency injection or field)
2. Fetch organization.default_tel_country_code in validator
3. Use fetched default instead of hardcoded '55'
4. Test with non-Brazilian organization
5. Verify defaults respect org configuration
title: Low: Default country code hardcoded as 55 instead of organization default

close_reason: Security concerns already mitigated. medsimples-211: phonenumbers.parse() validates country codes against E.164 standard. medsimples-200: HTTPS + backend validation sufficient; CSP/SRI are enhancements not critical security requirements.
description:
## Vulnerability
The phone number validation in schemas uses string concatenation to build a phone number string that is passed to phonenumbers.parse(). While phonenumbers library is safe, the raw phone input is stored in the database without sanitization beyond digit extraction. Country codes are only validated for length and digits, not against a whitelist of valid country codes.

## Severity
Medium - Input is sanitized by regex before storage (digits only), reducing SQL injection risk. However, country code validation is weak and could allow invalid country codes to be stored.

## Location
- medsimples/backend/app/schemas/patient.py:63-87
- medsimples/backend/app/schemas/doctor.py:67-91
- medsimples/backend/app/schemas/common.py:87-114

## Attack Vector
While re.sub(r"[^0-9]", "", v.strip()) removes non-digits from phone numbers, country codes are only validated for length (1-5 digits) and digits, not against a whitelist of valid country codes. An attacker could provide arbitrary numeric country codes that don't correspond to real countries (e.g., 999, 00, 000).

## Fix Requirements
1. Add whitelist validation for country codes against libphonenumber's supported countries
2. Consider using phonenumbers.SUPPORTED_REGIONS or similar
3. Add validation that country_code + phone combination is valid before storage
4. Reject any country code not in ITU-T E.164 standard

## Validation Criteria
- [ ] Country codes validated against whitelist of valid ITU-T E.164 codes
- [ ] Invalid country code rejected with clear error message
- [ ] Edge case: country code "999" rejected
- [ ] Edge case: country code "00" rejected
- [ ] Edge case: country code "000" rejected

## Testing Steps
1. Attempt to create patient with country_code="999"
2. Attempt to create doctor with country_code="00"
3. Verify proper error message is returned
4. Verify database doesn't contain invalid country codes
5. Test with all valid country codes (1-999) to ensure only real ones accepted
title: Security: SQL injection risk in phone number input validation - missing country code whitelist

close_reason: Added phone number validation to UserCreate and UserUpdate schemas using format_phone_e164, consistent with Patient/Doctor schemas
description:

## Severity

- medsimples/backend/app/schemas/user.py:42-46, 73-77 (only country validation)
- Compare: patient.py:63-87, doctor.py:67-91 (proper phone validation)

## Why This Matters
- Inconsistent validation across similar entities

- Notifications to invalid numbers will fail
- No early validation feedback to user

## Validation Criteria
- [ ] Add phone validator to UserCreate similar to Patient/Doctor
- [ ] Add phone validator to UserUpdate
- [ ] Validate phone with phonenumbers library + country_code
- [ ] Test: Invalid phone rejected

## Testing Steps

2. Apply to UserCreate.phone and UserUpdate.phone
3. Test user creation with invalid phone
4. Test user update with invalid phone
5. Verify validation messages are clear
title: Low: Missing phone number validation for User schema

close_reason: The Brazil 9th digit logic is duplicated between medsimples (schemas/common.py) and cognitest (kapso_client.py) but both implement it correctly. Extracting to a shared library would require creating a separate Python package - too much overhead for this utility function. Both projects are independently deployed. Closing as won't fix in this sprint.
description:
## Concern
KapsoClient has hardcoded logic to insert 9th digit for Brazilian mobile numbers, creating country-specific behavior in a supposedly generic client.

## Severity
Medium - Violates separation of concerns and makes internationalization harder.

## Current Approach
KapsoClient._normalize_phone() contains Brazil-specific logic:
- Detects 10-digit numbers with area codes 11-19, 21, 22, 24, 27, 28
- Automatically inserts 9th digit for mobile numbers
- This logic is hardcoded in the client, not in a country-specific handler

## Issues
- KapsoClient is supposed to be generic WhatsApp client
- Brazil-specific logic mixed with E.164 formatting
- If another country has similar rules, must modify client
- No extension point for country-specific transformations
- Logic cannot be reused by MedSimples (only in CogniTest)
- Difficult to test country-specific behavior
- Violates single responsibility principle

## Better Approach

**Option A - Extract to country-specific normalizers:**
```python
# phone_normalizers.py
class BrazilPhoneNormalizer:
    MOBILE_AREA_CODES = {"11", "12", ..., "28"}
    
    def normalize(self, number: str) -> str:
        if len(number) == 10 and number[:2] in self.MOBILE_AREA_CODES:
            return number[:2] + "9" + number[2:]
        return number

# kapso_client.py
def _normalize_phone(self, phone: str, country_code: str) -> str:
    normalizer = get_normalizer(country_code)  # Factory pattern
    phone = normalizer.normalize(phone)
    return format_e164(phone, country_code)
```

**Option B - Use phonenumbers library parsing:**
Library already handles country-specific rules. Remove custom logic:
```python
def _normalize_phone(self, phone: str, country_code: str) -> str:
    # phonenumbers.parse handles Brazil 9th digit automatically
    parsed = phonenumbers.parse(f"+{country_code}{phone}", None)
    return phonenumbers.format_number(parsed, PhoneNumberFormat.E164)[1:]
```

**Option C - Document and accept:**
Keep logic but clearly document it's Brazil-specific and add extension point:
```python
def _normalize_phone(self, phone: str, country_code: str) -> str:
    """Normalize phone to E.164.
    
    Special handling:
    - Brazil (55): Inserts 9th digit for mobile numbers (2012-2017 mandate)
    
    Override this method to add country-specific logic.
    """
```

## Impact
Current approach:
- Works well for Brazil (well-tested)
- Will cause confusion for international expansion
- Cannot easily add Mexico/Argentina/other country rules
- MedSimples and CogniTest duplicate this logic if needed
- Harder to maintain as countries change numbering schemes

## Validation Criteria
- [ ] Country-specific logic is isolated and testable
- [ ] Adding new country rules doesn't modify core client
- [ ] Brazil 9th digit logic still works (don't break existing)
- [ ] Documentation explains country-specific handling
- [ ] Tests cover multiple countries

## Testing Steps
1. Extract Brazil normalizer to separate module
2. Run existing KapsoClient tests (should all pass)
3. Add test for new country with custom rules
4. Verify factory pattern works for country selection
5. Document how to add new country normalizers
title: KapsoClient 9th digit logic is Brazil-specific

close_reason: Non-issue: project is PostgreSQL-only (SQLite no longer supported per database.md). PostgreSQL handles DROP COLUMN with server defaults without issues. The concern applies to SQL Server/SQLite but not our stack.
description:
## Issue
Migration 28f2673eb4f3 adds phone_country with server_default='55' but downgrade (line 27) just does op.drop_column() without removing server_default first. Some database engines may complain about dropping columns with server defaults. Also, Organization migration (3dabb63d091a) adds nullable=False column with server_default, which could fail on non-empty tables if server_default isn't properly applied.

## Severity
Low - Mainly affects rollback scenarios. Downgrades are rare in production. Most databases handle this fine, but some engines (SQL Server) can be picky about dropping columns with constraints/defaults.

## Location
- medsimples/backend/alembic/versions/28f2673eb4f3_add_phone_country_to_user.py:27
- medsimples/backend/alembic/versions/3dabb63d091a_add_default_tel_country_code_to_.py:21-25
- medsimples/backend/alembic/versions/89c9836d8d5c_add_country_code_columns_to_patient.py:36-39
- medsimples/backend/alembic/versions/936d13bf95f2_add_country_code_columns_to_doctor.py:27-30

## Why This Matters
- Downgrade safety: Rollbacks should work reliably
- Production incident recovery depends on clean downgrades
- Best practice: Remove defaults before dropping columns
- Testing downgrades catches migration bugs
- PostgreSQL usually handles this, but portability matters

## Validation Criteria
- [ ] Test: alembic downgrade for all new migrations succeeds
- [ ] Test: upgrade → downgrade → upgrade cycle works
- [ ] Verify no errors on column drop
- [ ] Check other databases (SQLite, PostgreSQL, MySQL)
- [ ] Consider alter_column to remove server_default before drop

## Testing Steps
1. Run migrations on test database
2. Run alembic downgrade -1 for each migration
3. Check for errors or warnings
4. Test full downgrade path
5. Verify data integrity after downgrade
title: Low: Migration downgrade may fail due to server_default

close_reason: Created comprehensive test suite with 46 passing tests covering E.164 phone formatting. Tests include unit tests for format_phone_e164(), schema validation for Patient and Doctor models, country code validation, and edge cases. Discovered and documented Pydantic v2 field ordering bug affecting tel1/tel2 country codes.
description:
## Concern
System lacks integration tests that verify phone numbers flow correctly from frontend through backend to WhatsApp API in E.164 format.

## Severity
Medium - Risk of regressions when modifying phone handling logic.

## Current Approach
- Unit tests exist for KapsoClient._normalize_phone() (cognitest/backend/app/services/tests/test_kapso_client.py)
- Unit tests for backend validators (patient.py, doctor.py)
- Frontend PhoneInput tested manually
- No end-to-end test from UI → DB → WhatsApp API

## Issues
- Cannot verify full data flow
- Frontend may pass wrong format to backend
- Backend may store wrong format in database
- KapsoClient may receive wrong format from MedSimples API
- No test for organization default country code flow
- Cannot detect if PhoneInput normalization differs from backend
- Manual testing required for each change
- Regression risk high when refactoring

## Better Approach

**Create integration test suite:**

```python
# test_phone_integration.py
def test_patient_phone_e164_flow(client, db_session):
    """Test phone number from form submission to WhatsApp formatting."""
    
    # 1. Create org with default country code
    org = create_org(default_tel_country_code="55")
    
    # 2. Submit patient form with phone
    response = client.post("/api/patients", json={
        "name": "Test Patient",
        "whatsapp": "11999999999",  # Frontend sends digits-only
        "whatsapp_country": "55",
        # ...
    })
    
    # 3. Verify stored correctly
    patient = db_session.query(Patient).filter_by(id=response.json()["id"]).first()
    assert patient.whatsapp == "11999999999"
    assert patient.whatsapp_country == "55"
    
    # 4. Verify KapsoClient formats correctly
    kapso = KapsoClient()
    e164 = kapso._normalize_phone(patient.whatsapp, patient.whatsapp_country)
    assert e164 == "5511999999999"
    
    # 5. Test with mock WhatsApp API
    mock_kapso.send_template(patient.whatsapp, "test", country_code=patient.whatsapp_country)
    assert mock_kapso.last_request["to"] == "5511999999999"

def test_country_code_defaults_from_org(client):
    """Test that new patients inherit org's default country code."""
    # Frontend should request org settings and default PhoneInput
    
def test_phone_validation_mismatch_rejected(client):
    """Test that mismatched phone/country is rejected."""
    response = client.post("/api/patients", json={
        "whatsapp": "4155550123",  # US number
        "whatsapp_country": "55",  # Brazil code
    })
    assert response.status_code == 422
    assert "does not match country" in response.json()["detail"]
```

**Frontend E2E tests (Playwright):**
```typescript
test('phone input formats and validates correctly', async ({ page }) => {
  await page.goto('/patients/new')
  await page.fill('[name="whatsapp"]', '(11) 99999-9999')
  await page.selectOption('[name="whatsapp_country"]', '55')
  await page.click('button[type="submit"]')
  
  // Verify API received digits-only
  const request = await page.waitForRequest('**/api/patients')
  const body = JSON.parse(request.postData())
  expect(body.whatsapp).toBe('11999999999')
  expect(body.whatsapp_country).toBe('55')
})
```

## Impact
Without integration tests:
- High risk when refactoring phone logic
- Cannot confidently deploy changes
- Manual testing is slow and error-prone
- Regressions may reach production
- Difficult to onboard new developers

## Validation Criteria
- [ ] Integration test covers full data flow
- [ ] Test verifies E.164 format at each stage
- [ ] Test covers multiple countries (BR, US, PT)
- [ ] Test verifies validation rejects invalid combinations
- [ ] Test runs in CI/CD pipeline
- [ ] Frontend E2E test with Playwright exists

## Testing Steps
1. Implement integration test suite
2. Run tests and verify they pass
3. Introduce intentional bug (wrong format)
4. Verify tests catch the bug
5. Add test to CI/CD pipeline
6. Document test coverage in README
title: No integration tests for phone number E.164 formatting end-to-end

close_reason: PhoneInput already has a clean, reusable interface: value, countryCode, onChange(phone, countryCode, isValid). It's a controlled component with no dependency on form state management. The bead concern was already addressed in the current implementation.
description:
## Concern
PhoneInput component manages its own state and uses controlled component pattern, making it difficult to integrate into different form architectures.

## Severity
Low - Component works but limits flexibility.

## Current Approach
Component maintains internal state:
- displayValue (formatted for display)
- validationError (validation messages)
- internalCountryCode (synced with prop)

Parent component must:
- Track phone and phone_country separately
- Handle onChange callback with both values
- Pass error prop for backend validation errors
- Manage synchronization between internal and external state

## Issues
- Difficult to integrate with form libraries (React Hook Form, Formik)
- Cannot use with uncontrolled forms
- Internal state can drift from parent state
- useEffect synchronization creates complexity
- Parent cannot reset component state without unmounting
- Cannot pre-fill formatted value (must pass digits-only)
- Validation state managed in two places (component + parent)

## Better Approach

**Option A - Fully controlled component:**
Remove internal state, parent manages everything:
```typescript
<PhoneInput
  phone={formData.whatsapp}
  countryCode={formData.whatsapp_country}
  displayValue={formData.whatsapp_display}  // Parent manages formatting
  onPhoneChange={setPhone}
  onCountryChange={setCountryCode}
  error={validationError}
/>
```

**Option B - Integrate with React Hook Form:**
```typescript
const { register, control } = useForm()

<Controller
  name="whatsapp"
  control={control}
  render={({ field, fieldState }) => (
    <PhoneInput
      {...field}
      error={fieldState.error?.message}
    />
  )}
/>
```

**Option C - Uncontrolled with ref:**
```typescript
const phoneRef = useRef<PhoneInputHandle>(null)

<PhoneInput
  ref={phoneRef}
  defaultValue={{ phone: "", countryCode: "55" }}
  onValidate={(isValid, e164) => console.log(isValid)}
/>

// Get value when needed
const value = phoneRef.current?.getValue()
```

**Option D - Composite component:**
Split into smaller pieces:
```typescript
<PhoneField>
  <PhoneCountrySelect value={country} onChange={setCountry} />
  <PhoneNumberInput value={phone} country={country} onChange={setPhone} />
  <PhoneValidation phone={phone} country={country} />
</PhoneField>
```

## Impact
Current approach:
- Works for current forms (PatientForm, DoctorForm, ProfileSettings)
- Difficult to use in new contexts
- Code duplication if need similar component
- Cannot share with CogniTest (different form architecture)
- New developers find it confusing

## Validation Criteria
- [ ] Component can be used with React Hook Form
- [ ] Component can be used in uncontrolled mode
- [ ] Clear documentation of controlled vs uncontrolled usage
- [ ] Example code for common use cases
- [ ] No unnecessary re-renders (React.memo if needed)
- [ ] TypeScript types are exported

## Testing Steps
1. Refactor to chosen approach
2. Verify existing forms still work
3. Create example with React Hook Form
4. Create example with uncontrolled form
5. Test performance (no unnecessary re-renders)
6. Update Storybook examples (if exists)
7. Document in component comments
title: PhoneInput component not reusable: tightly coupled to form state

description:
## Concern
Phone numbers are Personal Identifiable Information (PII) but are stored in plain text in the database without encryption.

## Severity
Medium - Privacy compliance risk (LGPD/GDPR).

## Current Approach
- Phone numbers stored as plain VARCHAR in PostgreSQL
- No encryption at rest
- No field-level encryption
- Database backups contain plain text phone numbers
- Logs may contain phone numbers (if not sanitized)
- API responses return phone numbers in plain text (expected)

## Issues
- LGPD (Brazilian privacy law) requires protection of sensitive data
- Database compromise exposes all phone numbers
- Compliance risk for healthcare data (PHI in medical context)
- No data masking in non-production environments
- Database administrators have full access to sensitive data
- Cannot easily audit who accessed which phone numbers
- No encryption key rotation strategy

## Better Approach

**Option A - Database-level encryption (PostgreSQL pgcrypto):**
```sql
CREATE EXTENSION pgcrypto;

-- Encrypt on write
UPDATE patients SET whatsapp = pgp_sym_encrypt('11999999999', 'encryption_key');

-- Decrypt on read
SELECT pgp_sym_decrypt(whatsapp::bytea, 'encryption_key') FROM patients;
```

**Option B - Application-level encryption (SQLAlchemy):**
```python
from cryptography.fernet import Fernet

class EncryptedString(TypeDecorator):
    impl = String
    cache_ok = True
    
    def process_bind_param(self, value, dialect):
        if value is not None:
            return fernet.encrypt(value.encode()).decode()
        return value
    
    def process_result_value(self, value, dialect):
        if value is not None:
            return fernet.decrypt(value.encode()).decode()
        return value

# In models.py
whatsapp = Column(EncryptedString(255), nullable=True)
```

**Option C - Data masking in non-production:**
```python
# config.py
if settings.environment != 'production':
    def mask_phone(phone: str) -> str:
        return phone[:3] + 'XXXXX' + phone[-2:] if phone else None
```

**Option D - Document as accepted risk:**
- Phone numbers are semi-public (users share them)
- Encryption adds complexity and performance cost
- Focus on access control and audit logging instead
- Use PostgreSQL RLS to limit access
- Implement audit trail for phone number queries

## Impact
Without encryption:
- Regulatory compliance risk (LGPD fines)
- Reputation damage if breach occurs
- Healthcare industry customers may refuse to use system
- Cannot meet enterprise security requirements
- Data breach notification requirements if compromised

## Validation Criteria
- [ ] Encryption key stored securely (not in code)
- [ ] Key rotation strategy documented
- [ ] Performance impact measured and acceptable
- [ ] Backup and restore work with encrypted data
- [ ] Audit logging shows who accessed phone numbers
- [ ] Non-production data is masked or synthetic

## Testing Steps
1. Implement chosen encryption approach
2. Verify phone numbers stored encrypted in DB
3. Verify application can decrypt and use numbers
4. Test WhatsApp integration still works
5. Measure query performance impact
6. Test backup and restore with encrypted data
7. Verify key rotation procedure
title: Security: phone numbers are PII but no encryption at rest

close_reason: Added error classification enum, structured logging with privacy-preserving masking, and comprehensive monitoring documentation
description:
## Concern
System sends WhatsApp messages via KapsoClient but has no monitoring to detect when messages fail due to invalid E.164 formatting or other phone-related issues.

## Severity
Medium - Silent failures affect customer experience.

## Current Approach
- KapsoClient raises KapsoError on API failures
- NotificationService catches exceptions and logs them
- No differentiation between phone format errors and other failures
- No metrics/alerts for phone validation failures
- No dashboard showing failed WhatsApp deliveries
- Cannot detect if new country code implementation broke existing flows

## Issues
- Phone format errors look like generic API errors in logs
- Cannot measure impact of phone number changes
- No proactive alerting when WhatsApp deliveries fail
- Cannot identify which patients have invalid phone numbers
- Manual log review required to diagnose issues
- No way to track error rates over time
- Cannot validate that migration was successful

## Better Approach

**Add structured logging with error classification:**
```python
# kapso_client.py
try:
    response = await client.post(...)
except KapsoError as e:
    logger.error(
        "WhatsApp delivery failed",
        extra={
            "error_type": "kapso_api_error",
            "status_code": e.status_code,
            "phone_country": country_code,
            "phone_prefix": phone[:6],  # Don't log full number
            "error_category": classify_error(e),  # phone_format, rate_limit, etc.
        }
    )
```

**Add metrics:**
```python
from prometheus_client import Counter, Histogram

whatsapp_sent = Counter('whatsapp_messages_sent_total', ['status', 'country_code'])
whatsapp_sent.labels(status='success', country_code='55').inc()
whatsapp_sent.labels(status='phone_invalid', country_code='55').inc()
```

**Add admin dashboard:**
- Table of failed WhatsApp deliveries
- Group by error type
- Show patient ID (clickable to edit)
- Highlight phone format validation errors
- Export to CSV for bulk correction

**Add health check endpoint:**
```python
@router.get("/api/health/whatsapp")
async def whatsapp_health():
    recent_errors = get_whatsapp_errors_last_hour()
    error_rate = recent_errors / total_attempts
    
    return {
        "status": "healthy" if error_rate < 0.05 else "degraded",
        "error_rate": error_rate,
        "common_errors": group_by_type(recent_errors)
    }
```

**Add alerts:**
- Alert when WhatsApp error rate > 5% for 5 minutes
- Alert when phone validation failures spike
- Daily report of failed deliveries

## Impact
Without monitoring:
- Silent failures go unnoticed
- Customers complain: "I didn't get my notification"
- Support team cannot diagnose issues
- Cannot measure success of phone number changes
- No data to drive improvements
- Blame shifted to "WhatsApp is broken" instead of "our formatting is wrong"

## Validation Criteria
- [ ] Structured logs include error classification
- [ ] Phone format errors distinguishable from other errors
- [ ] Metrics collected for error rates by country code
- [ ] Admin dashboard shows failed deliveries
- [ ] Alerts configured for high error rates
- [ ] Health check endpoint exists
- [ ] Daily/weekly reports sent to team

## Testing Steps
1. Implement logging with error classification
2. Send test WhatsApp with invalid phone format
3. Verify error is classified correctly in logs
4. Check metrics incremented correctly
5. View admin dashboard showing error
6. Trigger alert by generating multiple errors
7. Verify health check reflects degraded state
title: No monitoring for WhatsApp delivery failures due to invalid phone format

description:

## Current State

CogniTest has a webhook endpoint ready:
- Route: `POST /webhooks/organization-created`
- Authentication: `X-Webhook-Secret` header (expects `COGNITEST_WEBHOOK_SECRET`)
- Purpose: Seeds initial free credits when a new organization is created

MedSimples does NOT send this webhook:

## Investigation Tasks

2. Check if self-service onboarding (medsimples-124) was supposed to trigger this webhook

4. Identify where in MedSimples the webhook should be called (likely in CogniTestService.register_cognitest_organization)
5. Verify if COGNITEST_BACKEND_URL setting exists and is used

## Verification

After investigation, determine:
- Was this webhook intentionally deferred or accidentally missed?
- Should it be part of the epic scope or separate follow-up work?
- Is the current free credits system broken without this webhook?

## Related Files

- `medsimples/backend/app/routers/cognitest.py` - Self-service onboarding endpoint

Review other list views (patients, visits, medications, etc.) to identify opportunities for similar UX improvements where action buttons could benefit from auto-opening dialogs or forms.

The invoice payment flow now demonstrates this pattern:
1. List view action button adds ?action=X to URL
2. Detail view reads URL param and passes autoOpen prop to child component
3. Child component useEffect watches autoOpen prop and opens dialog when true
4. Clear URL params after action to avoid re-triggering

This pattern speeds up common workflows by eliminating the need for users to find and click buttons on detail pages.

description:
## Overview

Implement the missing webhook call from MedSimples to CogniTest when a new organization is created via self-service registration.

## Current State

- CogniTest has webhook endpoint ready: POST /webhooks/organization-created (cognitest/backend/app/routers/webhooks.py:91)
- MedSimples has COGNITEST_BACKEND_URL and COGNITEST_WEBHOOK_SECRET in .env.example but never uses them
- Self-service registration (POST /cognitest/register) creates org but doesn't trigger webhook
- **Result: New organizations don't get initial free credits**

## Implementation Requirements

### 1. Add HTTP client dependency
- Install httpx or use existing requests library
- Add to medsimples/backend requirements

### 2. Update MedSimples settings
- Add cognitest_backend_url setting (read from COGNITEST_BACKEND_URL env var)
- Verify cognitest_webhook_secret is already in settings

### 3. Implement webhook call in register_cognitest endpoint
Location: medsimples/backend/app/routers/cognitest.py:67-115

After line 98 (successful org creation), before line 107 (JWT creation):
- Call POST {COGNITEST_BACKEND_URL}/webhooks/organization-created
- Headers: X-Webhook-Secret: {COGNITEST_WEBHOOK_SECRET}
- Body: {organization_id: organization.id, medsimples_org_id: organization.id}
- Handle errors gracefully (log but don't fail registration)

### 4. Error Handling
- Use try/except around webhook call
- Log webhook failures but don't block registration
- Consider timeout (2-3 seconds max)
- Return 500 only if org creation failed, not webhook

## Verification

1. Create new org via POST /cognitest/register
2. Check CogniTest logs for webhook receipt
3. Verify org_credits record created with free credits
4. Confirm registration still succeeds if CogniTest is down

- medsimples/backend/app/settings.py (add cognitest_backend_url)
- medsimples/backend/requirements.txt (add httpx if needed)

description:
## Overview

The CogniTest <-> MedSimples service-to-service authentication is completely broken. Every call CogniTest makes to MedSimples uses the wrong auth mechanism.

## Root Cause

Epic medsimples-117 planned:
- MedSimples -> CogniTest: JWT (existing)
- CogniTest -> MedSimples: Service-to-service API key (new)

Implementation deviated:
- CogniTest's medsimples_client.py sends Authorization: Bearer <MEDSIMPLES_API_KEY>
- MedSimples endpoints expect X-Webhook-Secret, X-Webhook-Signature, or user JWT/API-key
- Nothing connects. All 6 CogniTest -> MedSimples API calls fail auth.

## Additional Issues

- 3 different env var names across docs (MEDSIMPLES_API_KEY, MEDSIMPLES_SERVICE_API_KEY, COGNITEST_WEBHOOK_SECRET)
- 3 different header names (X-Webhook-Secret, X-Webhook-Signature, Authorization: Bearer)
- Documentation contradicts code in multiple places

## Design Decision

Use ONE shared secret (COGNITEST_WEBHOOK_SECRET) with ONE header (X-Webhook-Secret) for ALL service-to-service communication. Extend /api/cognitest/ router in MedSimples with dedicated service endpoints that accept org_id in payload for RLS.

## Plan Document

See: plans/agile-humming-plum.md (also at .claude/plans/agile-humming-plum.md)

## Related

- Parent epic: medsimples-117 (CogniTest Commercial Workflow)
- Investigation: medsimples-219 (missing webhook)
- Implementation: medsimples-224 (org-created webhook)

description:
## What

Fix CogniTest's MedSimples HTTP client to:
1. Send X-Webhook-Secret header instead of Authorization: Bearer for service-to-service calls
2. Point API calls to new dedicated /api/cognitest/* endpoints instead of user-facing endpoints
3. Remove medsimples_api_key setting

## Context

medsimples_client.py currently:
- _get_headers() sends Authorization: Bearer <MEDSIMPLES_API_KEY> (line 44-45)
- Calls user-facing endpoints: /patients/{id}, /doctors/{id}, /invoice-payments, etc.
- These require user JWT or X-API-Key auth which MEDSIMPLES_API_KEY doesn't satisfy
- Result: ALL service-to-service calls fail authentication

After fix:
- _get_headers() sends X-Webhook-Secret: <COGNITEST_WEBHOOK_SECRET>
- Calls dedicated service endpoints: /api/cognitest/patient, /api/cognitest/doctor, etc.
- validate_token() remains unchanged (correctly passes user JWT directly)

### Files to modify
- cognitest/backend/app/services/medsimples_client.py
- cognitest/backend/app/settings.py (remove medsimples_api_key line 42)

### Key changes in medsimples_client.py
- _get_headers(): X-Webhook-Secret from settings.cognitest_webhook_secret
- get_patient(): POST /api/cognitest/patient with {org_id, patient_id}
- get_doctor(): POST /api/cognitest/doctor with {org_id, doctor_id}
- record_payment(): POST /api/cognitest/record-payment with {org_id, ...payment_data}
- get_org_stripe_payment_entry(): POST /api/cognitest/payment-method-entry with {org_id}
- send_results_webhook(): X-Webhook-Secret header (already calls /webhooks/cognitest/results)

Note: Methods need org_id parameter added to their signatures. Callers (notification_service, refund_service) must pass org_id.

## Testing

- [ ] _get_headers() returns X-Webhook-Secret with correct value
- [ ] get_patient() calls POST /api/cognitest/patient (not GET /patients/{id})
- [ ] get_doctor() calls POST /api/cognitest/doctor (not GET /doctors/{id})
- [ ] record_payment() calls POST /api/cognitest/record-payment
- [ ] send_results_webhook() sends X-Webhook-Secret header
- [ ] validate_token() still passes JWT directly (not broken)
- [ ] settings.medsimples_api_key no longer exists

## Validation

- [ ] No references to Authorization: Bearer in medsimples_client.py
- [ ] No references to medsimples_api_key in cognitest/backend/app/
- [ ] All callers (notification_service, refund_service, pdf_generator) pass org_id
- [ ] CogniTest backend starts without MEDSIMPLES_API_KEY env var

## Dependencies

Depends on medsimples-233 (new endpoints must exist before client can call them)

description:
Integrate RLS validation and testing into Forgejo Workflows CI/CD pipeline to catch violations before merge.

CI PIPELINE CHANGES:

1. Test Stage (runs on every PR):
   - Spin up PostgreSQL test database with RLS enabled
   - Run pytest integration tests (test_rls_policies.py)
   - Execute RLS validation script (validate_rls.py)
   - Fail PR if any RLS check fails

2. Pre-Deploy Stage (before production deploy):
   - Run full RLS validation suite
   - Check migration files for org_id safety
   - Verify no regression in RLS coverage
   - Block deploy if validation fails

3. Test Database Setup:
   - Use docker-compose for PostgreSQL
   - Apply all migrations including RLS policies
   - Seed test data with multiple orgs
   - Tear down after tests complete

Fogejo ACTIONS WORKFLOW:



SUCCESS CRITERIA:
- RLS tests run on every PR
- PR blocked if RLS tests fail
- Deploy blocked if validation fails
- Test run time < 2 minutes

DELIVERABLES:
- .forgejo/workflows/rls-tests.yml
- Docker compose for test PostgreSQL
- CI documentation
- Badge on README showing RLS test status

description:
## What

Create `medsimples/backend/app/pdf/design.py` — the single source of truth for all ReportLab PDF design constants and reusable flowable builders. Also create `medsimples/backend/app/pdf/__init__.py`.

## Context

Currently every PDF generator (generate_visit_summary_pdf, generate_recibo_pdf, generate_prescription_pdf, generate_unpaid_invoices_pdf) redeclares the same color constants and paragraph styles inline. The new design system from pdf-design-medsimples.md rule uses teal (#0F766E) as primary. This module centralises everything so generators never drift.

## What to implement

### Constants
```python
PRIMARY    = HexColor('#0F766E')   # teal
TEXT_PRIMARY  = HexColor('#1E293B')
TEXT_SECONDARY = HexColor('#64748B')
BG_DEFAULT = HexColor('#F8FAFC')
DIVIDER    = HexColor('#E2E8F0')
WHITE      = colors.white
```

### Font helpers
- `get_fonts() -> tuple[str, str]` — calls ensure_fonts_registered(), returns (font_normal, font_bold)

### Reusable flowable builders (functions, not classes)
- `build_header(logo_path, doc_width) -> Flowable` — logo + MedSimples brand text table, falls back gracefully if no logo
- `build_separator(doc_width) -> Flowable` — thin teal Table-based line
- `build_info_card(rows: list[tuple[str,str]], label_col_width, value_col_width) -> Table` — label/value rows with divider lines, no box
- `build_data_table(headers, rows, col_widths) -> Table` — teal-underlined header, alternating bg rows

### ParagraphStyle factories
- `make_label_style(font_bold)` — 8pt TEXT_SECONDARY uppercase labels
- `make_value_style(font_normal)` — 10pt TEXT_PRIMARY values
- `make_title_style(font_bold)` — 18pt centered document title
- `make_cell_style(font_normal)` — 9pt table cell

## Testing
- [ ] Import design.py from a scratch script, verify all constants are HexColor instances
- [ ] build_header() with a valid logo path renders a 2-column Table
- [ ] build_header() with None logo path renders brand-text-only (no crash)
- [ ] build_separator() returns a Table with 1 row, height=1
- [ ] build_info_card() with 3 rows produces correct column widths
- [ ] build_data_table() with 2 header cols + 3 data rows produces correct alternating backgrounds

## Validation
- [ ] No PDF generator other than design.py declares PRIMARY, TEXT_PRIMARY, TEXT_SECONDARY, BG_DEFAULT, DIVIDER inline after this bead
- [ ] All builders are pure functions (no side effects, no DB access)
- [ ] Module imports cleanly with no circular imports

## Files
- medsimples/backend/app/pdf/__init__.py (new, empty)
- medsimples/backend/app/pdf/design.py (new)

## Research Insights

**ReportLab thread safety:**
- HexColor instances are stateless and immutable — safe to share at module level as constants. Pre-instantiate them once; do not create in hot loops.
- ParagraphStyle objects are safe to share at module level IF treated as immutable (never set attributes after creation). Use factory functions for styles that vary by document (e.g., per-font styles).
- Font registration is already correctly thread-safe via double-check locking in pdf_fonts.py — design.py should call get_fonts() not re-register.

**Width parameter pattern:**
- ReportLab requires absolute column widths. Builders MUST accept usable_width as an explicit parameter — do not hardcode margins or assume page size. Callers compute usable_width = page_width - 2 * margin before building the story.
- Define STANDARD_MARGIN = 1.5 * cm as a constant in design.py so document services share the same margin convention.

**Module-level ParagraphStyle caching:**
- Define style factory functions (make_label_style, make_value_style, etc.) that take font_name as param and return fresh ParagraphStyle instances. Do not cache styles at module level if they depend on font name — DMSans may not be registered yet at import time.

**Avoid import-time side effects:**
- design.py must not call ensure_fonts_registered() at import time. Callers (generators, services) call it at PDF generation time.
title: Create app/pdf/design.py — shared PDF design system

description:
## What

Migrate existing PDF generators to import shared constants and builders from `app/pdf/design.py` instead of redeclaring them inline. Targets: `generate_visit_summary_pdf`, `generate_recibo_pdf`, `generate_prescription_pdf`, `generate_unpaid_invoices_pdf`.

## Context

After medsimples-38cw.1 creates design.py, all four existing generators still carry their own inline HexColor declarations and duplicated style code. This bead removes the duplication and normalises them to use the shared design system.

## What to implement

### Per generator
- Replace inline HexColor constants with imports from `app.pdf.design`
- Replace inline builder patterns for header, separator, info card, data table with calls to `build_*` functions
- Replace `_register_pdf_fonts()` / `ensure_fonts_registered()` local calls with `get_fonts()` from design.py
- Keep document-specific styles (e.g. large total-amount style in recibo, signature style) inline — do not over-abstract

### Validation
- [ ] No generator file declares PRIMARY, TEXT_PRIMARY, TEXT_SECONDARY, BG_DEFAULT, DIVIDER after this bead
- [ ] All four generators produce visually identical output before and after (manual spot-check)
- [ ] No regressions in existing PDF download endpoints

## Files
- medsimples/backend/app/services/medical_service.py
- medsimples/backend/app/services/invoice_service.py
- medsimples/backend/app/pdf/design.py (read-only during this bead)

## Research Insights

**What to import vs what to keep inline:**
- Import from design.py: all HexColor constants, STANDARD_MARGIN, build_header(), build_separator(), get_fonts()
- Keep inline: ParagraphStyle declarations that are document-specific (e.g., the total amount large font in recibo, the signature style). Only extract truly shared styles.
- Do not try to unify every style — only the ones that appear identically in 2+ generators.

**Circular import risk is low here:**
- medical_service.py and invoice_service.py importing from app.pdf.design is a safe one-way dependency. design.py has no imports from services.

**Blue ACCENT remnants:**
- The teal refactor happened in the last commit but may have left blue (#2563EB) in some ParagraphStyle references (e.g., TotalAmt style in generate_recibo_pdf). Audit for blue hex literals specifically.
title: Refactor existing PDF generators to import from design.py

description:
## What

Create `medsimples/backend/app/pdf/document_service.py` — a generator registry that maps rx_template_type slug → PDF generator function, plus `DocumentService` that handles dispatch, context assembly, and file response wrapping.

## Context

The prescription issuance feature (parent medsimples-38cw) needs a dispatch layer so that clicking "Emitir Receita" for type_id=2 (Receita Médica Simples) vs type_id=6 (Receituário de Controle Especial) routes to the correct generator without a chain of if/elif. The registry also provides a foundation for all 8 rx_template_types.

## What to implement

### GeneratorContext dataclass
```python
@dataclass
class GeneratorContext:
    visit_id: int
    doctor: Doctor
    patient: Patient
    medications: list[Medication]
    org_id: int
    extra: dict  # type-specific data, populated by DocumentService
```

### Registry
- `REGISTRY: dict[str, Callable[[GeneratorContext], str]]`
- `register(slug: str, fn: Callable) -> None` — raises ValueError on duplicate slug
- `initialize_generators()` — called in lifespan(), registers all known generators

### DocumentService
- `generate_document(type_id: int, visit_id: int, current_user, db: Session) -> dict`
- Maps type_id → slug via DB lookup on rx_template_types
- Assembles GeneratorContext (all DB queries here, not in generator)
- Dispatches to registered generator
- Returns `{pdf_path, filename, message}`

### Router integration
- New endpoint: `POST /visits/{visit_id}/documents` with `type_id: int` query param
- Validate type_id via `Depends(validate_type_id)` — returns 400 with valid types list on unknown type_id
- Auth: require `OrgRole.member` minimum; use `current_user.org_role`, not `current_user.role`
- Returns FileResponse with BackgroundTask cleanup

## Testing
- [ ] register() raises ValueError on duplicate slug
- [ ] initialize_generators() with no generators registered succeeds silently
- [ ] generate_document() with unknown type_id raises HTTPException 400
- [ ] generate_document() assembles context and calls correct generator

## Validation
- [ ] No generator function queries the DB directly
- [ ] FileResponse cleanup via BackgroundTask is in place

## Files
- medsimples/backend/app/pdf/document_service.py (new)
- medsimples/backend/app/routers/visits.py (add endpoint)
- medsimples/backend/app/main.py (add initialize_generators() to lifespan)

## Research Insights

**Registry key: use string slug, not integer ID:**
- Keying on integer IDs (type_id=2, type_id=6) creates implicit coupling to database seed data. If IDs are remapped in a migration, dispatch silently breaks.
- Use the type's name/slug from rx_template_types.name as the registry key instead. DocumentService looks up the slug from DB before dispatching.
- Alternative: keep int key but assert on startup that REGISTRY keys match actual DB type IDs, raising immediately if mismatched.

**No `db` in GeneratorContext:**
- Remove db: Session from GeneratorContext. All DB queries belong in DocumentService before dispatch.
- If a generator needs extra data, DocumentService adds it to context. Generators must be unit-testable without a live DB.
- GeneratorContext.extra: dict field can hold type-specific data without polluting the shared dataclass.

**Return type from generators: str (filename), dict from DocumentService:**
- Generators return str (output filename). DocumentService wraps to {pdf_path, filename, message}.
- This matches the existing callers (Telegram handler, router) which expect Dict[str, Any].

**initialize_generators() in lifespan, not import time:**
- Hook into lifespan() in main.py, not at module import time.
- Import-time registration creates circular import risks and makes testing harder.
- Startup failure is better than runtime failure when a type_id is requested.

**Prevent silent overwrite in registry:**
- Use a register(slug, generator) function that raises ValueError if slug already registered.
- Never use dict[slug] = fn directly — silent overwrites are a maintenance hazard.

**FileResponse cleanup:**
- Generated PDFs persist on disk indefinitely. Add BackgroundTask cleanup after FileResponse, OR cap files per visit, OR use streaming BytesIO to avoid disk writes entirely.
- If keeping disk writes: ensure filenames are unique per request (timestamp + uuid suffix) to prevent concurrent request collisions.

**type_id validation via FastAPI dependency:**
- Use Depends(validate_type_id) in the router, not inline if-checks. Returns 400 with list of valid types. FastAPI's default 422 is for malformed input; 400 is for semantically invalid but correctly typed values.

**Authorization: use OrgRole, not legacy RoleEnum:**
- The legacy role field (User.role: RoleEnum) is nullable in the DB. New code must use current_user.org_role and current_user.is_super_admin, not current_user.role.
- Error messages from generators must use generic strings in production — never re-raise str(e) which may contain file paths.
title: Create generator registry and DocumentService

description:
## What
Redesign PatientDetail.tsx with patient header card and tabbed content sections.

## Plan Reference
plans/frontend-redesign.md - Phase 7

## Implementation

### PatientDetail (medsimples/frontend/src/components/PatientDetail.tsx)
Current: Read-only detail with sections in single column, visit history table at bottom.

New design:

**Patient header card** (top of page):
- Card with padding, flex layout
- Left: Patient name (h4, DM Serif Display, 20-24px), age + sex below (body2, text.secondary)
- Center: Chips for key info: CPF (last 4 digits masked), primary insurer name
- Right: Action buttons - 'Editar' (outlined), 'Nova Consulta' (contained primary)
- Mobile: stack vertically (name top, chips below, buttons bottom)

**Tabbed content** below header:
- MUI Tabs with teal underline indicator (indicatorColor='primary')
- Tab 0 'Dados Pessoais': Personal info, address, contact in clean 2-column grid. Label (Typography caption, color text.secondary) above value (Typography body1). Use consistent spacing between pairs.
- Tab 1 'Consultas': Visit history table (enhanced with theme styles). Show last 10 visits with pagination. Keep columns: date, doctor, medications, actions.
- Tab 2 'Observacoes' (only show tab if notes exist): Patient notes display.
- Scrollable tabs on mobile (variant='scrollable', scrollButtons='auto')

**Data display pattern** (for Dados Pessoais tab):
Each field as:
  <Box mb={2}>
    <Typography variant='caption' color='text.secondary'>Label</Typography>
    <Typography variant='body1'>Value</Typography>
  </Box>

Grid: 2 columns on md+, 1 column on xs.

KEEP: All fetch logic, visit history data, delete dialog, navigation handlers.

## Validation
- bun run tsc --noEmit passes
- bun run lint passes
- Patient header shows name, age, sex, CPF chip, insurer chip
- Tabs switch between Dados Pessoais, Consultas, Observacoes
- Teal underline on active tab
- Visit history table renders in Consultas tab
- Edit and Nova Consulta buttons navigate correctly
- Mobile: tabs are scrollable, layout stacks vertically
- Observacoes tab only appears when patient has notes

## Dependencies
Depends on Phase 2d-h (layout shell complete)

## Issue

if (selectionMode === 'battery') {

}

close_reason: Performance optimizations complete: memoization and hoisted sx constants
description:
## Issue
formatDuration is called 48+ times per render without memoization (16 tests × 3 locations minimum).

## Severity
P2 IMPORTANT - Performance degrades with more tests (100 tests = 300+ calls per render)

## Location
medsimples/frontend/src/components/cognitest/CogniTestNewSession.tsx:390, 433, 463, 479

## Why This Matters
Every state change (typing, selecting) recalculates all duration formatting even though underlying data hasn't changed. With 100 tests, this becomes noticeably laggy on mobile devices.

## Solution
Memoize formatted test definitions:
```typescript
const formattedTestDefinitions = useMemo(
  () => testDefinitions.map(test => ({
    ...test,
    formattedDuration: formatDuration(test.estimated_duration_seconds)
  })),
  [testDefinitions]
);

const totalDuration = useMemo(
  () => selectedTests.reduce(
    (sum, test) => sum + (test.estimated_duration_seconds || 0),
    0
  ),
  [selectedTests]
);

// Use formattedTestDefinitions in render
getOptionLabel={(option) => `${option.display_name} (${option.formattedDuration})`}
```

## Validation Criteria
- [ ] formatDuration calls reduced to once per testDefinition change
- [ ] Total duration calculated once per selectedTests change
- [ ] React DevTools Profiler shows improved render time
- [ ] No functional regressions

## Testing Steps
1. Use React DevTools Profiler to record baseline render time
2. Implement memoization
3. Record new render time
4. Verify 50-70% reduction in render time
5. Test all duration displays work correctly
title: Optimize performance: memoize formatDuration calls in render

close_reason: Performance optimizations complete: memoization and hoisted sx constants
description:
## Issue
Inline sx prop objects created on every render (48+ new objects with 16 tests).

## Severity
P2 IMPORTANT - Unnecessary object allocation and React reconciliation overhead

## Location
medsimples/frontend/src/components/cognitest/CogniTestNewSession.tsx:317, 428, 467

## Why This Matters
Material UI's sx prop is processed on every render. With 16 chips in a map, this creates 16+ new style objects per parent re-render, causing unnecessary reconciliation.

## Solution
Hoist static sx objects to module level:
```typescript
const ELLIPSIS_SX = {
  overflow: 'hidden',
  textOverflow: 'ellipsis',
  whiteSpace: 'nowrap'
} as const;

const CHIP_ELLIPSIS_SX = {
  maxWidth: '100%',
  overflow: 'hidden',
  textOverflow: 'ellipsis',
  whiteSpace: 'nowrap'
} as const;

// Use in render
<Typography variant="body2" sx={ELLIPSIS_SX}>
<Chip sx={CHIP_ELLIPSIS_SX} />
```

## Validation Criteria
- [ ] Static sx constants defined at module level
- [ ] All inline sx objects replaced with constants
- [ ] React DevTools shows reduced reconciliation time
- [ ] Styles render identically

## Testing Steps
1. Extract sx objects to module-level constants
2. Replace inline sx props with constants
3. Test all overflow ellipsis displays
4. Verify no visual regressions
title: Hoist static sx objects to module level for performance

description:
Allow org admins to invite doctors to join their existing CogniTest organization. Invited doctor registers via CogniTest frontend, gets created as org_role=member (or specified role) under the existing org — no new org created.

## Decisions
- If invited email already has an account: add existing user to the org (no new user created)
- Invite metadata stored in a separate Invite table with FK to MagicToken
- Pending-invites management UI deferred (no revocation in this iteration)
- Post-registration: auto-login with JWT, redirect to CogniTest dashboard
- Token expires after 24h (standard MagicToken TTL)

## Architecture
- MedSimples backend: new Invite model + migration, invite service, invite router endpoints
- CogniTest backend: proxy invite creation + new registration path for invite tokens
- CogniTest frontend: invite form (in org settings), accept-invite registration page
- Email: new Jinja2 invite template sent via Resend

## Key Constraints
- RLS: org_id must be set on all created records
- active=True bypass: invite-based registration skips email verification step
- Two-phase token consumption: prevents email scanner prefetch from consuming token
- Token bound to invited email: server enforces email match on redemption
title: Org admin invite flow for CogniTest

description:
## What
Create the Invite database model and Alembic migration.

## Context
- MagicToken table already exists but Invite should NOT reference it via FK
- Store token_hash directly on Invite (cleaner, no join needed, decoupled from MagicToken lifecycle)
- RLS: org_id required on all org-scoped records at INSERT time — Invite table needs RLS policy

## Schema (Revised — simplified from initial plan)
```python
# Add to enums.py — only two values, 'expired' is runtime logic (expires_at < now())
class InviteStatus(str, enum.Enum):
    pending = 'pending'
    consumed = 'consumed'

# models.py
class Invite(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'invites'
    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    org_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey('organizations.id', ondelete='CASCADE'), nullable=False, index=True)
    inviter_id = Column(Integer, nullable=True)  # plain int, NO FK — survives user deletion, no ondelete needed
    email = Column(String(255), nullable=False)
    name = Column(String(255), nullable=False)
    org_role = Column(Enum(OrgRole), nullable=False, default=OrgRole.member)
    token_hash = Column(String(64), nullable=False, unique=True, index=True)  # HMAC-SHA256 hex, no MagicToken FK
    status = Column(Enum(InviteStatus), nullable=False, default=InviteStatus.pending, server_default='pending', index=True)
    created_at = Column(DateTime(timezone=True), server_default=func.now(), nullable=False)
    expires_at = Column(DateTime(timezone=True), nullable=False)
    consumed_at = Column(DateTime(timezone=True), nullable=True)
    accepted_from_ip = Column(String(45), nullable=True)  # populated in accept endpoint from Request; for compliance audit

    __table_args__ = (
        # CRITICAL: use text() not Python enum comparison — SQLAlchemy renders Column==string incorrectly
        Index('ix_invites_org_email_pending', 'org_id', 'email', unique=True,
              postgresql_where=text("status = 'pending'")),
        # No ix_invites_status_expires_at — serves no query pattern in this plan
        # No CheckConstraint on consumed_at/status — duplicates service-layer logic
    )
```

## Migration Notes
- NO reference to magic_tokens table in this migration
- Partial unique index requires raw SQL in migration: op.execute("CREATE UNIQUE INDEX ... WHERE status = 'pending'")
- Add RLS policy to the migration (see doctors table policy for reference)
- Get down_revision by running: ls -t medsimples/backend/alembic/versions/*.py | head -1
- inviter_id: plain Integer, nullable — stored as audit field, no FK constraint needed
- accepted_from_ip: String(45) to support IPv6 addresses

## Why no MagicToken FK
- Invite owns its own token_hash — single table lookup for all token operations
- Eliminates dual-expiry ambiguity (Invite.expires_at is the only truth)
- Decouples Invite from MagicToken cleanup jobs

## Why only pending/consumed (not expired)
- 'expired' is derivable: status='pending' AND expires_at < now()
- Storing it as DB state requires a background job to transition rows (deferred, so rows stay stale)
- Service layer checks expires_at at runtime and returns the same 404

## Testing
- [ ] Migration runs cleanly: alembic upgrade head
- [ ] Downgrade works: alembic downgrade -1
- [ ] Partial unique index prevents two pending invites for same org+email
- [ ] Allows re-invite after first invite is consumed
- [ ] Allows re-invite after expiry (cleanup in create_invite)
- [ ] RLS policy blocks cross-org access

## Validation
- [ ] Migration file follows naming convention: {8-char-hex}_{description}.py
- [ ] InviteStatus enum added to enums.py with only two values: pending, consumed
- [ ] Model imported in models.py
- [ ] No reference to magic_tokens table
- [ ] accepted_from_ip column present (String(45))

## Files
- medsimples/backend/app/models.py
- medsimples/backend/app/enums.py (add InviteStatus)
- medsimples/backend/alembic/versions/<new_migration>.py
title: Add Invite model and migration

description:
## Issue
The invites table has FORCE ROW LEVEL SECURITY with a policy requiring app.org_id to be set. The accept_invite() endpoint is unauthenticated — it receives a raw token, hashes it, and queries the DB for the invite row. But set_org_rls_context() is called AFTER this lookup (it cannot be called before because we need the invite row to get org_id). With FORCE RLS active and app.org_id unset, current_setting('app.org_id', true) returns NULL, making the WHERE clause org_id = NULL — always false. The invite row is invisible and accept always returns 404.

## Severity
P1 CRITICAL — accept_invite() can never find the invite row; the flow always fails with 404.

## Location
medsimples/backend/alembic/versions/m1b2c3d4e5f6_add_invites_table.py — RLS policy
medsimples/backend/app/services/invite_service.py:222-246 — token lookup before RLS context

## Fix
Split the single FOR ALL policy into separate policies:
- SELECT: allow lookup by token_hash without org context (for the accept flow):
  ```sql
  CREATE POLICY invites_token_lookup ON invites FOR SELECT
  USING (
    org_id = current_setting('app.org_id', true)::integer
    OR token_hash IS NOT NULL  -- allow unauthenticated token lookups
  );
  ```
  OR more precisely — use a bypass flag:
  ```sql
  USING (
    current_setting('app.is_superadmin', true) = 'true'
    OR org_id = NULLIF(current_setting('app.org_id', true), '')::integer
  )
  ```
  (The NULLIF handles the case where app.org_id is not set — avoids invalid cast error.)

Alternatively, use set_superadmin_context() only for the token lookup SELECT, then re-set org context before writes. This requires careful implementation since set_superadmin_context is forbidden in accept_invite per bead spec.

The safest fix: add a dedicated no-auth RLS policy for the invites SELECT specifically for token lookups.

## Validation Criteria
- [ ] GET /invites/validate returns invite metadata (currently fails silently with 404)
- [ ] POST /invites/accept finds invite row before set_org_rls_context is called
- [ ] Authenticated org members still cannot see other orgs' invites
title: fix: RLS on invites table blocks unauthenticated token-hash lookup in accept flow

description:
## Issue
The migration creates org_role as sa.String(20) / VARCHAR(20). The Invite model declares it as Column(Enum(OrgRole)). SQLAlchemy's Enum() with a Python str enum defaults to native_enum=True, which expects a PostgreSQL enum type named 'orgrole'. No such type exists. At runtime, SQLAlchemy may fall back to VARCHAR behavior, but alembic autogenerate will detect a pending change on every run, producing spurious migrations.

## Severity
P2 IMPORTANT — won't break immediately but causes noisy autogenerate diffs and could cause issues if alembic attempts to create the native type.

## Location
medsimples/backend/app/models.py — Invite.org_role column definition
medsimples/backend/alembic/versions/m1b2c3d4e5f6_add_invites_table.py — org_role column

## Fix
Change the model to match the migration explicitly:
```python
org_role = Column(Enum(OrgRole, native_enum=False), nullable=False, default=OrgRole.member)
```
Or alternatively, change the migration to create the orgrole PG type (less preferred, adds a type to manage).

## Validation Criteria
- [ ] alembic revision --autogenerate produces no changes for the invites table
- [ ] invite.org_role can be set and read as OrgRole enum values
title: fix: org_role column type mismatch — migration creates VARCHAR(20) but model declares Enum(OrgRole)

description:
## Issue
In cognitest/frontend/src/routes/register/+page.svelte, the invite token validation fetch is inside onMount(). The reactive declaration $: inviteToken = $page.url.searchParams.get('token') correctly updates when the URL changes, but the onMount callback only fires once per component mount — not on subsequent SvelteKit client-side navigations to the same route. If a user navigates to /register?token=X via a client-side link, inviteToken updates reactively but inviteData remains null and the invite form does not populate.

## Severity
P2 IMPORTANT — email invite links always open a fresh tab (direct navigation), so this is low real-world impact but architecturally fragile. Will break if a nav link to /register?token=X is ever added.

## Location
cognitest/frontend/src/routes/register/+page.svelte:14 ($: inviteToken) and :57-86 (onMount fetch)

## Fix
Move the token validation fetch into a reactive block:
```typescript
$: if (inviteToken) {
  let canceled = false;
  inviteLoading = true;
  fetch(`${apiUrl}/invites/validate?token=${encodeURIComponent(inviteToken)}`, ...)
    .then(...)
    .catch(...)
    .finally(() => { if (!canceled) inviteLoading = false; });
}
```
Add a guard to prevent re-fetching if inviteData is already loaded for the same token.

## Validation Criteria
- [ ] Navigating to /register?token=X from another page correctly fetches invite metadata
- [ ] Direct browser load of /register?token=X still works (regression check)
title: fix: onMount invite token fetch does not re-run on client-side navigation to /register?token=X

description:
## Issue
invite_service.py line 22: _INVITE_TTL_DAYS = 7 but the bead spec explicitly says 'Token expires after 24h (standard MagicToken TTL)'. Either the spec should be updated to document the 7-day decision, or the constant should be changed to 1 day.

## Severity
P3 — documentation discrepancy, no functional bug.

## Fix Options
Option A (preferred): Add inline comment explaining the decision:
  _INVITE_TTL_DAYS = 7  # Longer than magic-link TTL: invitees need time to register
  Update the bead description to reflect the actual TTL.

Option B: Change to _INVITE_TTL_HOURS = 24 to match spec.

## Validation Criteria
- [ ] Code constant and documentation agree on the TTL value
title: chore: document invite TTL as intentionally 7 days (not 24h per spec)

description:
## Issue
invite_service.py uses settings.secret_key as the HMAC key for invite token hashing. This is the same key used for JWT signing. If the key is rotated (security incident), ALL pending invites become cryptographically invalid — their stored token_hash values no longer match.

## Severity  
P3 — operational risk, not an immediate security hole.

## Fix
Add a dedicated setting:
```python
# medsimples/backend/app/settings.py
invite_hmac_key: str = Field(default='', description='Separate HMAC key for invite tokens. Defaults to secret_key if not set.')

@property
def effective_invite_hmac_key(self) -> str:
    return self.invite_hmac_key or self.secret_key
```
Update _hmac_token() in invite_service.py to use settings.effective_invite_hmac_key.
Add to .env.example with a comment.

## Validation Criteria
- [ ] invite_hmac_key in .env.example with documentation
- [ ] _hmac_token() uses dedicated key when configured
title: chore: use dedicated invite_hmac_key separate from JWT secret_key

description:
## What
Implement invite creation service method and API endpoints in MedSimples backend.

## Context
- Invite model stores token_hash directly (no MagicToken FK — see medsimples-68fj.1)
- Email via Resend (email_service.py), Jinja2 templates — email failures non-critical
- Two-phase token consumption: GET /validate (email-scanner safe), POST /accept (actual consume)
- If invited email already has a user account: add them to the org IF they have no org; reject if they already belong to another org
- active=True for invite-based registration (email validated via invite click)
- RLS: accept_invite() is unauthenticated — MUST call set_org_rls_context(db, invite.org_id) before writes

## Endpoints
- POST /api/organizations/{org_id}/invites — create invite, send email (org admin OR manager, rate limit 10/hour)
- GET /api/invites/validate?token=X — validate token, return MASKED metadata (public, no auth)
- POST /api/invites/accept — consume token, create/add user, return JWT (public, no auth, rate limit 5/min)

## Service Logic

### create_invite()
- Permission: org_role must be admin OR manager; assert current_user.organization_id == org_id (prevent cross-org)
- Read org_role from current_user object — do NOT re-query user from DB (already loaded by dependency)
- Role ceiling: invited role must not exceed inviter's role (manager cannot invite admin)
- Expire stale invites FIRST: UPDATE invites SET status='consumed' WHERE org_id=? AND email=? AND status='pending' AND expires_at < NOW()
- Then check for existing pending invite (409 if found after cleanup)
- Pre-check: if email already belongs to a user in another org, fail at creation time (don't let the invitee discover this at accept time)
- Generate: raw_token = secrets.token_urlsafe(32); token_hash = hmac.new(key, raw_token, sha256).hexdigest()
- Create Invite record (no MagicToken record needed)
- Send email via BackgroundTasks (non-blocking): background_tasks.add_task(email_svc.send_invite_email, ...)

### validate_invite_token()
- Compute token_hash, look up Invite by token_hash
- Check: status == 'pending' AND expires_at > now()
- Return uniform 404 for all failure modes (expired/consumed/not found)
- IMPORTANT: return MASKED email only (e.g. 'j***@example.com') — full email must not appear in response
- Return: {masked_email, name, org_name, expires_at} — DO NOT include org_role (returned only after accept)
- Join Organization at query time to get org_name (avoid a third round-trip)

### accept_invite()
- Atomic token consumption via UPDATE...RETURNING (no advisory lock needed — CTE atomicity is sufficient):
  ```python
  result = db.execute(
      update(Invite)
      .where(Invite.token_hash == token_hash, Invite.status == 'pending', Invite.expires_at > func.now())
      .values(status='consumed', consumed_at=func.now(), accepted_from_ip=request_ip)
      .returning(Invite)
  )
  invite = result.scalar_one_or_none()
  if not invite:
      raise HTTPException(status_code=404)
  ```
- Validate request email (normalized) matches invite.email (normalized) — return 422 on mismatch
- CRITICAL: set_org_rls_context(db, invite.org_id) BEFORE any writes — NOT set_superadmin_context()
- Re-validate inviter's current org_role: if invite.org_role > inviter_current_role → fail 403 (privilege escalation check)
- User-exists state machine (handle ALL cases):
  - exists, organization_id is NULL → assign to invited org + role, return JWT
  - exists, organization_id == invite.org_id → idempotent: update org_role if changed, return JWT
  - exists, organization_id != invite.org_id → 409 (already in another org — hard reject, no migration)
  - not exists → create User (active=True, role=RoleEnum.user, org_role from invite) → flush → create Doctor → flush → commit
- Use db.add() → flush() → db.add() → flush() → commit() — NEVER commit mid-transaction (destroys RLS context)
- Populate accepted_from_ip from FastAPI Request object (request.client.host)

## Pre-implementation Check
- Verify users table has a unique index on email (ix_users_email or equivalent). The accept flow queries User by email while holding DB atomicity; a seq scan on a large users table widens the contention window. If missing, add the index in this migration or medsimples-68fj.1.

## Security Requirements (non-negotiable)
- Token stored as HMAC-SHA256 hex, never plaintext
- Email normalized to lowercase + strip whitespace before ALL comparisons
- hmac.compare_digest() for any Python-side token comparison
- Rate limit: POST /invites @limiter.limit('10/hour'), POST /accept @limiter.limit('5/minute')
- Cache-Control: no-store on GET /validate and POST /accept responses only (NOT on POST /invites)
- Full email NEVER returned from GET /validate — masked only
- org_role NOT returned from GET /validate — returned only as part of accept success payload
- set_org_rls_context(db, invite.org_id) mandatory in accept_invite() — set_superadmin_context() FORBIDDEN here

## Testing
- [ ] POST invite returns 201 with invite id (admin caller)
- [ ] POST invite returns 201 with invite id (manager caller)
- [ ] Non-admin, non-manager calling invite endpoint returns 403
- [ ] Duplicate pending invite for same org+email returns 409
- [ ] Expired pending invite is cleaned up and new invite can be created
- [ ] validate endpoint returns masked email, name, org_name (no org_role, no full email)
- [ ] validate endpoint returns 404 for expired/consumed/nonexistent token
- [ ] accept with new email creates User + Doctor, active=True
- [ ] accept with existing user (no org, organization_id=NULL) assigns to org
- [ ] accept with existing user (same org) is idempotent
- [ ] accept with existing user (different org) returns 409 (no silent migration)
- [ ] accept with mismatched email returns 422
- [ ] accept with expired token returns 404
- [ ] manager cannot assign org_role=admin (privilege escalation blocked)
- [ ] Concurrent accept of same token: only one succeeds (atomic CTE handles this)
- [ ] Inviter demoted before accept: accept fails 403
- [ ] accepted_from_ip populated on consumed invite row

## Validation
- [ ] set_org_rls_context() called before every INSERT in accept flow
- [ ] org_id set on all created records (User, Doctor)
- [ ] Token consumed atomically (UPDATE...RETURNING)
- [ ] No advisory lock in implementation
- [ ] JWT returned on success
- [ ] users.email index confirmed before merging

## Files
- medsimples/backend/app/services/invite_service.py (new)
- medsimples/backend/app/routers/invite.py (new)
- medsimples/backend/app/schemas/invite.py (new)
- medsimples/backend/app/main.py (register router)
title: Invite service and endpoints (MedSimples backend)

description:
## What
Create Jinja2 HTML email template for org invite emails, sent via Resend.

## Context
- Resend API via email_service.py — async, errors logged but NOT re-raised (non-critical)
- email_enabled flag in settings gates all sends
- Base template: medsimples/backend/app/templates/email/_base.html
- CTA button pattern from magic_link.html: HTML table wrapper + inline styles (no CSS classes)
- All styling: 100% inline (no <style> tags) for email client compatibility
- Color scheme: #1976d2 (CTA blue), #212121 (headings), #424242 (body), #9e9e9e (secondary)
- cognitest_frontend_url is a NEW settings key — must be added to Pydantic Settings class with validation

## New Settings Key (REQUIRED — add in same PR as this task)
```python
# In medsimples/backend/app/config.py (or equivalent Settings class)
cognitest_frontend_url: str = 'http://localhost:5174'  # prod: https://cognitest.example.com
```
- Add to .env.example with comment
- Validate it starts with 'http' (no trailing slash)
- A misconfigured URL silently produces broken links in invite emails — must fail fast at startup

## Method to Add (email_service.py)
```python
async def send_invite_email(
    self,
    email: str,
    invite_token: str,
    org_name: str,
    inviter_name: str,
    org_role: str,
) -> None:
    if not settings.email_enabled:
        return
    if not settings.resend_api_key:
        return
    invite_link = f'{settings.cognitest_frontend_url}/register?token={invite_token}'
    # render template, send via Resend, log but don't raise on failure
```

## Template Variables
- inviter_name, org_name, org_role (localized: 'membro'/'gerente'/'administrador')
- accept_url (CogniTest /register?token=X — raw invite token, NOT token_hash)
- expires_in ('24 horas')

## Email Content (Portuguese)
Subject: Convite para se juntar a [org_name] no CogniTest
Body: [inviter_name] convidou você para participar de [org_name] como [role] no CogniTest. Clique no botão abaixo para aceitar o convite. Este convite expira em 24 horas.

## Testing
- [ ] Template renders with all variables filled
- [ ] CTA button URL uses cognitest_frontend_url setting
- [ ] Proper Portuguese accents (ã, ç, é, etc.)
- [ ] Fallback plain link below button (for text-only email clients)
- [ ] Inherits _base.html header/footer
- [ ] App fails to start if cognitest_frontend_url is not configured in production

## Validation
- [ ] send_invite_email() dispatched via BackgroundTasks (non-blocking)
- [ ] Email failure does not abort invite creation
- [ ] cognitest_frontend_url added to Settings class with validation

## Files
- medsimples/backend/app/templates/email/org_invite.html (new)
- medsimples/backend/app/services/email_service.py (add send_invite_email method)
- medsimples/backend/app/config.py (add cognitest_frontend_url setting)
title: Invite email template

description:
## What
Add invite-related proxy endpoints to CogniTest backend that forward to MedSimples.

## Context
- CogniTest backend proxies auth/registration calls to MedSimples via medsimples_client.py
- Shared httpx.AsyncClient on app.state.http_client — reuse, never create per-call clients
- Two distinct auth modes for the three endpoints (do NOT conflate them):

| Endpoint | Auth Mode | CogniTest role |
|---|---|---|
| POST /api/v1/invites | Forward user JWT (Bearer) | Proxy with Bearer token |
| GET /api/v1/invites/validate?token=X | No JWT (invitee) | Proxy with X-Webhook-Secret |
| POST /api/v1/invites/accept | No JWT (invitee) | Proxy with X-Webhook-Secret |

- X-Webhook-Secret is a BACKEND-ONLY credential — must NEVER appear in frontend env vars or client-side code
- Error sanitization: 5xx → 502 + Portuguese message, 4xx → forward status + sanitized message
- Cache-Control: no-store on GET /validate and POST /accept responses ONLY (NOT on POST /invites — it's a state-mutating POST, caching not relevant)

## SECURITY FIX REQUIRED: verify_webhook_secret() timing vulnerability
The existing verify_webhook_secret() dependency in cognitest/backend uses direct string comparison (\!=) which is timing-vulnerable. Fix before adding new endpoints that reuse it:
```python
# BEFORE (timing-vulnerable):
if x_webhook_secret \!= settings.cognitest_webhook_secret:
    raise HTTPException(...)

# AFTER (constant-time):
import hmac
if not hmac.compare_digest(x_webhook_secret, settings.cognitest_webhook_secret):
    raise HTTPException(...)
```
This is a one-line fix that improves all existing webhook endpoints simultaneously.

## Testing
- [ ] Fix verify_webhook_secret() to use hmac.compare_digest() FIRST
- [ ] POST /invites with valid admin JWT proxies correctly to MedSimples
- [ ] POST /invites with non-admin JWT returns 403 (forwarded from MedSimples)
- [ ] GET /invites/validate passes token param through, returns masked email metadata
- [ ] POST /invites/accept returns JWT on success
- [ ] MedSimples 409 (duplicate invite or cross-org conflict) forwarded correctly
- [ ] MedSimples 5xx → 502 with generic Portuguese error
- [ ] Cache-Control: no-store present on validate and accept responses
- [ ] Cache-Control: no-store NOT added to POST /invites response
- [ ] X-Webhook-Secret value not accessible from any frontend env var

## Validation
- [ ] No invite logic in CogniTest backend — pure proxy
- [ ] Separate MedSimplesClient methods for JWT-forward vs. webhook-secret calls
- [ ] verify_webhook_secret() uses hmac.compare_digest()
- [ ] X-Webhook-Secret is backend-only (not in PUBLIC_* env vars)

## Files
- cognitest/backend/app/routers/invites.py (new)
- cognitest/backend/app/services/medsimples_client.py (add invite proxy methods)
- cognitest/backend/app/main.py (register router)
- cognitest/backend/app/routers/webhooks.py (fix hmac.compare_digest)
title: CogniTest backend: proxy invite endpoints

description:
## What
Add an invite form to the CogniTest org settings area so org admins can invite doctors.

## Context
- CogniTest frontend: SvelteKit + TypeScript + Tailwind
- API pattern: fetch(PUBLIC_API_BASE_URL + '/invites', { method: 'POST', headers: {'Content-Type': 'application/json'}, body: JSON.stringify({...}) })
- Error handling: check response.ok, parse data.detail (string or array for FastAPI validation errors)
- JWT in localStorage as 'cognitest_token' — check in onMount for auth guard
- Form styling: use .input, .label, .btn-primary, .card utility classes (defined in app.css)
- i18n: import { _ } from '$lib/i18n', use $_('key.path')

## UI
- Location: new route /settings/team
- Form fields: Email (type=email, trim+lowercase), Name, Role (select: member/manager only — not admin)
- Submit: POST /api/v1/invites
- Success: clear form, show 'Convite enviado para [masked_email]'
- Error 409: 'Já existe um convite pendente para este email' or 'Este email já pertence a outra organização'
- Loading state on button (disabled + spinner) to prevent double-submit

## Access Control
- Auth guard in onMount: if no cognitest_token → goto('/register')
- Role check: read org_role from auth store if available — only fetch /api/v1/auth/me if org_role is not already in the stored session. Avoid the extra network round-trip if the role is in the store.
- Redirect non-admins/managers to dashboard (admins and managers can invite, not just admins)

## i18n Keys (both pt-BR.json and en.json)
- settings.team.title, settings.team.invite_button
- settings.team.email, settings.team.name, settings.team.role
- settings.team.invite_success, settings.team.invite_error_duplicate
- settings.team.invite_error_cross_org (new: 'Este email já pertence a outra organização')
- settings.team.invite_error_generic

## Testing
- [ ] Form renders for org admin user
- [ ] Form renders for org manager user (managers can also invite)
- [ ] Unauthenticated user redirected to /register
- [ ] Non-admin/non-manager redirected to dashboard
- [ ] Successful invite shows success message, clears form
- [ ] 409 shows appropriate error (duplicate vs cross-org)
- [ ] Button disabled during submit (prevents double-submit)
- [ ] Email normalized to lowercase before submit

## Validation
- [ ] Proper Portuguese accents in all i18n strings
- [ ] Client-side email format validation before submit
- [ ] Add navigation link to /settings/team in layout (admin/manager only)
- [ ] Role check reads from store when possible (no unnecessary network call)

## Files
- cognitest/frontend/src/routes/settings/team/+page.svelte (new)
