description:
## Issue
Multiple hardcoded hex colors bypass the dark theme:

**Theme overrides (themes/index.ts):**
- MuiTableHead: backgroundColor '#F1F5F9', color '#475569' - unreadable in dark mode
- MuiTableRow hover: backgroundColor '#F8FAFC' - wrong in dark mode
- MuiOutlinedInput notchedOutline: borderColor '#CBD5E1' - invisible in dark mode
- MuiButton containedSecondary: color '#1E293B' - invisible on dark bg

**Sidebar (hardcoded by design, but not documented):**
- SIDEBAR_BG, text colors, active indicator colors

**Header:**
- Frosted glass uses rgba(255,255,255,0.85) only overridden via applyStyles

## Severity
P1 CRITICAL - Dark mode is broken for all table pages and forms.

## Location
medsimples/frontend/src/themes/index.ts:178-208, 147-155, 143-144

## Fix
Replace hardcoded hex in theme component overrides with CSS variables: var(--mui-palette-...) or add theme.applyStyles('dark', {...}) overrides for each.

## Validation Criteria
- [ ] Table headers readable in dark mode
- [ ] Table row hover visible in dark mode
- [ ] Input borders visible in dark mode
- [ ] Secondary buttons text visible in dark mode

## Testing Steps
1. Toggle dark mode via header button
2. Navigate to any list page (Patients, Visits, Invoices)
3. Verify table headers, rows, and input fields are readable
title: Dark mode broken: hardcoded light colors in theme overrides and components

description:
## Problem

CogniTest only supports magic-link login. Doctors who register via invite set a password during registration but have no way to use it to log in. This creates a dead end: they can register but not return.

## Solution

Add a password-based login flow:
1. CogniTest backend: POST /auth/login endpoint proxying to MedSimples POST /token (OAuth2 form encoding), sets httpOnly ct_token cookie on success
2. CogniTest frontend: login form accessible from the app — either on a dedicated /login page or within the existing test-taking entry point
3. Correct post-registration redirect target (goto('/') currently lands on the marketing page, not the app)

## Key Decisions

- MedSimples POST /token accepts OAuth2PasswordRequestForm (form-encoded username+password), not JSON — MedSimplesClient.login() must use data= not json=
- set_auth_cookie() + strip token from body is the established pattern (matches register, accept_invite)
- Rate limit: 5/minute (matches MedSimples /token)
- No password reset in scope — show 'use magic link' as fallback
- Default tab: password login (since magic link is for the public landing page, not the app login)

## Out of Scope

- Password reset / forgot password flow
- Account lockout after N failures (MedSimples handles upstream)
title: Password login for CogniTest

description:
## What

Add a password login endpoint to the CogniTest backend that proxies to MedSimples and sets an httpOnly auth cookie.

## Context

- MedSimples POST /token uses OAuth2PasswordRequestForm (form-encoded), returns {access_token, token_type}
- Established pattern: call set_auth_cookie(response, token), strip token from response body (see register() in auth.py)
- **CRITICAL: MedSimplesClient.login() must follow the request_magic_link()/create_invite() shared-client pattern — NOT register(), which creates a new AsyncClient per call (known resource leak)**
- **CRITICAL: login() must use httpx data= parameter for form encoding and must NOT call _get_headers() which forces Content-Type: application/json**
- Rate limit: 5/minute per IP (two-layer defense with MedSimples's own rate limiting)
- Proxy error handling: HTTPException for 502, generic 422 for any 4xx; sanitize all upstream errors — never forward verbatim
- On MedSimples 4xx: return 422 {"detail": "E-mail ou senha inválidos."}
- On MedSimples connection failure: return 502 {"detail": "Serviço temporariamente indisponível"}
- LoginRequest needs max_length on password (max_length=128) — prevents 10MB payload forwarding to MedSimples
- Also fix JWT error detail leak in dependencies.py line 47: replace detail=f"Invalid token: {str(e)}" with detail="Authentication required"

## Testing

- [ ] POST /auth/login with valid credentials returns 200, sets ct_token cookie, body has no access_token
- [ ] POST /auth/login with wrong password returns 422 with sanitized message (not upstream error)
- [ ] POST /auth/login with missing fields returns 422
- [ ] POST /auth/login exceeding rate limit returns 429
- [ ] POST /auth/login when MedSimples is unreachable returns 502
- [ ] login() uses shared client (verify no 'async with httpx.AsyncClient()' in login method body)

## Validation

- [ ] ct_token cookie is httpOnly, Secure (in prod), SameSite matches existing set_auth_cookie() behavior
- [ ] access_token never appears in response body
- [ ] Upstream error messages never forwarded verbatim
- [ ] Endpoint registered in main.py router
- [ ] LoginRequest.password has max_length=128
- [ ] dependencies.py line 47 no longer leaks JWT exception detail

## Files

- cognitest/backend/app/routers/auth.py
- cognitest/backend/app/services/medsimples_client.py (login() method — follow request_magic_link pattern)
- cognitest/backend/app/schemas/auth.py (LoginRequest with max_length)
- cognitest/backend/app/auth/dependencies.py (fix JWT error leak on line 47)

## Dependencies

None — standalone backend change
title: Add POST /auth/login endpoint to CogniTest backend
Simplify login page: use finally block, remove redundant handleKeyDown

description:
## Issue
The string '/commissions' is hardcoded in 3 places:
- cognitest/frontend/src/routes/login/+page.svelte
- cognitest/frontend/src/routes/register/+page.svelte (twice)

If the default authenticated route ever changes, it requires a find-and-replace across files. Additionally, there is no ?next= query param support — if a user follows a deep link to a protected page while unauthenticated, the auth guard sends them to /login but after login they land on /commissions instead of their original destination.

## Severity
P3 NICE-TO-HAVE — Maintainability and UX improvement.

## Fix
1. Create $lib/constants.ts with:
```typescript
export const DEFAULT_POST_LOGIN_ROUTE = '/commissions';
```
2. Import and use in login/+page.svelte and register/+page.svelte
3. (Optional) Add ?next= support in /(app)/+layout.ts redirect and consume in login page

## Validation Criteria
- [ ] '/commissions' string only defined in one place

description:
## Issue
Login returns 200 and sets the ct_token cookie, but the immediately following GET /auth/me (from the /(app)/+layout.ts auth guard) returns 401. The cookie is not being sent on that first request after navigation.

## Observed in production logs
```
cognitest_login_success email=paypal@dinaventures.com
POST /api/v1/auth/login 200 OK
GET /api/v1/auth/me 401 Unauthorized
```

## Root cause hypothesis
SameSite=Strict was changed to SameSite=Lax (commit 3ffb183) as an attempted fix, and the auth guard redirect was moved outside the try/catch (commit 8ce819a). These may not have fully resolved the issue — the cookie may still not be sent on the first request after the POST->navigation sequence.

## What to investigate
- Confirm whether the Set-Cookie response header is present on the /auth/login 200 response
- Confirm whether the cookie is present in the browser after login (DevTools -> Application -> Cookies)
- Check if the /auth/me request includes the Cookie header (DevTools -> Network)
- Check cookie domain/path — in production the app is served from a single origin; confirm ct_token path='/' and no domain restriction that would block it
- Check if goto('/commissions') triggers a full page reload or client-side navigation — a full reload would clear in-flight cookies on some browsers

## Validation Criteria
- [ ] After successful login, /auth/me returns 200 (not 401)
- [ ] User lands on /commissions without being redirected back to /login
- [ ] ct_token cookie visible in DevTools after login
title: Login succeeds but /auth/me returns 401 immediately after — cookie not sent on first post-login request

description:
## What

## Context

- There is no dedicated dashboard — the only authenticated app routes are /(app)/commissions and /settings/team
- The login form should live at /login (new route) so it's bookmarkable and shareable
- Auth guard pattern: call GET /auth/me with credentials: 'include'; redirect to /login on 401
- On successful login: the ct_token cookie is set by the backend; the frontend should redirect to /commissions
- Show 'Prefira receber um link? Use o link mágico' hint linking to / for users who have no password
- **Keep the login page simple: ~100 lines, 2 fields (email + password), one error string. Do NOT add password strength meter, fieldErrors dict, multi-step form, or per-field validation — only 2 fields, validate non-empty before submit**
- Error cases: 3 cases only — rate-limited (429), invalid credentials (any 4xx), network error. Do not distinguish between 401/403/422.

## Testing

description:
## Issue
POST /auth/login returns 200 and logs 'cognitest_login_success' even when MedSimples returns a 200 with no access_token in the body. No cookie is set. The frontend sees success, calls goto('/commissions'), the layout guard calls /auth/me, gets 401, and loops back to /login with no error surfaced.

## Severity
P1 CRITICAL — Silent success on missing token makes login appear to work while not actually logging the user in.

## Location
cognitest/backend/app/routers/auth.py lines 99-105

## Fix
```python
token = body.get('access_token')
if not token:
    logger.error('login_proxy_missing_token', upstream_status=status_code)
    raise HTTPException(status_code=502, detail='Serviço temporariamente indisponível', headers=_NO_STORE)
set_auth_cookie(response, token)
```

## Validation Criteria
- [ ] POST /auth/login with mocked MedSimples 200 response missing access_token returns 502
- [ ] login_proxy_missing_token is logged
- [ ] No cognitest_login_success log on token-less response
title: Fix silent success when MedSimples returns 200 with no access_token

description:
## Issue
The router-level Limiter in auth.py uses get_remote_address which reads request.client.host (always the load balancer IP behind a proxy). The app-level limiter correctly uses a custom get_real_ip that walks X-Forwarded-For. Result: all users behind the same proxy share one rate limit bucket, and an attacker with a spoofed X-Forwarded-For bypasses per-IP limiting entirely.

## Severity
P1 CRITICAL — Rate limiting is effectively non-functional in production (reverse proxy deployment).

## Location
cognitest/backend/app/routers/auth.py — the local Limiter instance:
```python
limiter = Limiter(key_func=get_remote_address)  # WRONG in proxy deployments
```

## Fix
Replace with the same get_real_ip function used by app.state.limiter in main.py, or use the app-level limiter instead of a separate router-level instance.

## Validation Criteria
- [ ] Rate limit applies per real client IP, not per proxy IP
- [ ] X-Forwarded-For spoofing from untrusted proxies is rejected
- [ ] get_real_ip from main.py (or equivalent) used in the login rate limiter
title: Fix rate limiter key_func: uses get_remote_address instead of get_real_ip
Fix auth guard: use isRedirect() instead of fragile Response/status duck-type check

description:
## Issue
handleSubmit in login/+page.svelte has no re-entrancy guard at its entry point. The form's on:submit and the Enter key handler (handleKeyDown) can both call handleSubmit concurrently in a narrow timing window (e.g. user focuses submit button and hits Space + Enter simultaneously). The loading flag check in handleKeyDown is correct but handleSubmit itself has no guard.

## Severity
P2 — Can cause duplicate login requests in edge cases.

## Location
cognitest/frontend/src/routes/login/+page.svelte — handleSubmit()

## Fix
```typescript
async function handleSubmit(): Promise<void> {
    if (loading) return;  // add this as first line
    // ...
}
```

Also: the handleKeyDown function is redundant — the form's on:submit already handles Enter natively. Remove handleKeyDown entirely and the on:keydown handler from the form.

## Validation Criteria
- [ ] if (loading) return; added as first line of handleSubmit
- [ ] handleKeyDown function removed (or kept with .catch() attached)
- [ ] Form submits correctly via Enter key (native form behavior)
title: Add missing guard at top of handleSubmit against re-entrant calls

description:
## Issue
The login endpoint in routers/auth.py has several redundant patterns:
1. Cache-Control header set twice (via _NO_STORE on each HTTPException AND manually on success path) — set once at top of function instead
2. except Exception on ms_response.json() should be except ValueError (json.JSONDecodeError is a subclass)
3. Bare 'as e' binding in auth/dependencies.py catch block is unused after the JWT leak fix — remove it
4. body.pop('access_token', None); return body is simpler than the dict comprehension

## Severity
P3 NICE-TO-HAVE — Code clarity and consistency.

## Location
- cognitest/backend/app/routers/auth.py (login endpoint)
- cognitest/backend/app/auth/dependencies.py (unused 'as e')

## Fix
```python
# auth.py: set Cache-Control once at top
response.headers['Cache-Control'] = 'no-store'
# Remove headers=_NO_STORE from each HTTPException raise

# auth.py: narrow exception type
except ValueError:  # not except Exception

# auth.py: simpler token strip
body.pop('access_token', None)
return body

# dependencies.py: remove unused binding
except (JWTError, ValueError):  # not 'as e'
```

## Validation Criteria
- [ ] No behavior change
- [ ] Cache-Control set once at function start
- [ ] except ValueError instead of except Exception on json parse
- [ ] Dict comprehension replaced with body.pop()
title: Simplify login endpoint: consolidate repeated patterns

description:
Address all issues found by react-doctor v0.0.15 scan: 8 errors and 177 warnings across 78/120 source files.

## Scan Results
- 8 errors (fetch in useEffect, derived state in useEffect)
- 177 warnings (giant components, excess useState, index keys, unused files, recharts splitting, autoFocus, event handler simulation)
- 32,960 lines of frontend code, 120 source files

## Tech Stack
React 18 + TypeScript + Vite + MUI 6.5.0. Custom apiCache.ts for data fetching (no react-query/SWR). Context API with useReducer in providers. React.lazy already used for route-level code splitting.

## Scope
medsimples/medsimples/frontend/src/

## Key Constraints
- Keep custom apiCache.ts — it is the established pattern; do NOT introduce react-query/SWR
- Respect MUI 6.5.0+ (never 6.1.0)
- Move fetches to service layer or extract custom hooks, not a new library
- AbortController for async cleanup (not hasFetchedRef)
- Promise.allSettled for independent parallel fetches
title: React frontend quality: fix react-doctor errors and warnings

description:
## What
Extract fetch logic from useEffect into custom hooks that use cachedAxiosGet from apiCache.ts. Affects 7 files.

## Files to Fix
- src/components/PatientForm.tsx:115
- src/components/pdf-editor/PDFCanvasOverlay.tsx:44
- src/components/PatientsList.tsx:160
- src/components/VisitList.tsx:232
- src/components/VisitDetail.tsx:246
- src/components/PatientDetail.tsx:149
- src/components/InvoiceForm.tsx:246

## Context
The codebase uses a custom apiCache.ts (Map-based, TTL, promise deduplication). Do NOT introduce react-query or SWR. The correct fix is to extract fetch logic into custom hooks (e.g., usePatient, useVisits) that encapsulate the cachedAxiosGet call and manage their own loading/error state. Use AbortController for cleanup (not hasFetchedRef). Use Promise.allSettled for independent parallel fetches.

Pattern established in: src/utils/apiCache.ts

## Implementation Pattern
```typescript
function usePatient(patientId: string) {
  const [patient, setPatient] = useState<Patient | null>(null);
  const [loading, setLoading] = useState(true);
  useEffect(() => {
    const controller = new AbortController();
    cachedAxiosGet(`/api/patients/${patientId}`)
      .then(res => { if (\!controller.signal.aborted) setPatient(res.data); })
      .finally(() => { if (\!controller.signal.aborted) setLoading(false); });
    return () => controller.abort();
  }, [patientId]);
  return { patient, loading };
}
```

## Testing
- [ ] Each component loads data correctly after refactor
- [ ] No console warnings about state updates on unmounted components
- [ ] AbortController cancels in-flight requests on unmount
- [ ] Repeated navigation doesn't cause duplicate fetches (cache works)
- [ ] Loading states display correctly

## Validation
- [ ] react-doctor re-scan (`sandbox-exec -f /tmp/no-network.sb react-doctor medsimples/frontend/src --no-lint -y`) shows 0 'fetch in useEffect' errors
- [ ] No regression in data loading behavior in affected components

## Files
- src/components/PatientForm.tsx
- src/components/pdf-editor/PDFCanvasOverlay.tsx
- src/components/PatientsList.tsx
- src/components/VisitList.tsx
- src/components/VisitDetail.tsx
- src/components/PatientDetail.tsx
- src/components/InvoiceForm.tsx
- src/hooks/ (new custom hooks extracted here)

## Dependencies
None
title: Fix: move fetch() out of useEffect into custom hooks

description:
## What
Refactor components with 8+ useState calls for related state into useReducer. Prioritize the most complex components; not all 31 flagged components need this treatment.

## Context
The codebase already has established useReducer patterns in LayoutContext.tsx and UserContext.tsx (split context with dispatch). Use the same pattern:
- Define a typed state interface
- Define action union types
- Create reducer function
- Use `const [state, dispatch] = useReducer(reducer, initialState)`

Key pattern from institutional learnings: use functional updater spread for partial updates. Group state by concern (formData, uiState, loadingState) rather than one giant reducer.

This task should be done AFTER medsimples-ijoe.1 (fetch extraction) since extracting fetches will reduce the state footprint first.

## Testing

description:
## What
Refactor 23 useEffect blocks that contain multiple setState calls. Either (a) combine into a single state object update, (b) migrate to useReducer dispatch, or (c) restructure so each effect has a single concern.

## Context
Multiple setState in one effect causes cascading re-renders (one per setState call). Better approaches:
1. Single object state: `setState({ field1, field2, field3 })` — one re-render
2. useReducer dispatch — batch related updates
3. For data-loading effects: extract to custom hook (covered by medsimples-ijoe.1)

Many of these overlap with the fetch extraction task — once fetches are in custom hooks, the remaining setState calls in effects will decrease significantly.

This task should be done AFTER medsimples-ijoe.1 and medsimples-ijoe.10.

## Testing

- [ ] TypeScript check passes

description:
## What
Break up 32 components that exceed ~300 lines into smaller, focused subcomponents. Prioritize the most egregious cases first.

## Priority Targets
| Component | Lines | Primary sections to extract |
|-----------|-------|-----------------------------|
| InvoiceDetail.tsx | ~1500 | Header, LineItems, PaymentSection, NFSe panel, Status bar |
| VisitForm.tsx | ~900 | PatientSection, PrescriptionSection, DiagnosisSection, AttachmentsSection |
| PatientForm.tsx | ~953 | DemographicsSection, AddressSection, InsuranceSection, ContactSection |
| InvoiceForm.tsx | large | ItemsTable, TotalsSection, PatientPicker, VisitPicker |
| VisitDetail.tsx | large | VisitHeader, MedicationsTab, DocumentsTab, InvoiceTab |
| FiscalSettings.tsx | large | NfseConfig, CertificateSection, InvoiceDefaults |
| exam-results/ExamResultDetail.tsx | large | Header, ResultsGrid, ScoresSection |
| pdf-editor/PDFCanvasOverlay.tsx | large | Canvas, FieldOverlay, DragHandles |

## Context
Existing pattern: MedSimplesLayout.tsx uses React.lazy for route-level components. Subcomponents within a file do NOT need lazy loading — they're in the same bundle chunk. Extract to separate files in a subdirectory pattern, e.g., `components/invoice-detail/` with `InvoiceDetailHeader.tsx`, `InvoiceDetailLineItems.tsx`, etc.

This task is best done LAST — after fetch extraction (ijoe.1), useReducer refactor (ijoe.10), and setState cleanup (ijoe.11) have reduced each component's complexity. This avoids splitting components that will change significantly anyway.

Do NOT split components that are flagged as unused (covered by ijoe.4).

description:
## Issue
useVisitData.ts exports useVisitData() but VisitDetail.tsx never imports it. VisitDetail still uses inline fetchVisitData + hasFetchedRef pattern which has no AbortController — wrong patient data can render when navigating between visits.

## Severity
P1 CRITICAL — hasFetchedRef has no AbortController: if user navigates from visit/1 to visit/2 while fetch is in-flight, the id=1 response dispatches into the id=2 view (wrong patient data shown). 100 lines of dead code in hooks/useVisitData.ts.

## Location
- medsimples/frontend/src/components/VisitDetail.tsx:392–451 (inline fetch / hasFetchedRef)
- medsimples/frontend/src/hooks/useVisitData.ts (entire file — zero consumers)

## Why This Matters
hasFetchedRef has no AbortController. Navigation between visits can show the wrong patient's data. The hook that fixes this exists but is unused.

## Fix Options
A (preferred): Wire VisitDetail.tsx to import and use useVisitData(id). Remove inline fetchVisitData and hasFetchedRef. The hook already uses AbortController + Promise.allSettled correctly.
B: Delete hooks/useVisitData.ts and add AbortController to the inline fetch in VisitDetail.

## Validation Criteria
- [ ] VisitDetail.tsx imports useVisitData (or inline fetch has AbortController)
- [ ] hasFetchedRef pattern removed
- [ ] hooks/useVisitData.ts has at least one consumer OR is deleted
- [ ] bun run tsc --noEmit passes
- [ ] Navigating rapidly between visits does not show stale data
title: Fix: wire VisitDetail to use useVisitData hook (or delete hook)

description:
## Issue
VisitDetailAction union has 24 individual SET_field cases (SET_PATIENT, SET_VISIT, SET_INVOICE, SET_MEDICATIONS, SET_SPECIALTY, SET_INSURER, SET_DATE, SET_NOTES, etc.). Each dispatches a single scalar payload — ~180 lines of boilerplate that adds no semantic value over a simple setState.

## Severity
P2 IMPORTANT — Maintainability cost. Adding a new form field requires 4 edits: state type, action union, reducer case, dispatch call.

## Location
medsimples/frontend/src/components/VisitDetail.tsx — VisitDetailState type, VisitDetailAction union, reducer switch

## Why This Matters
- Compound actions already exist (CANCEL_EDIT, INIT_FORM_AND_MEDICATIONS), proving the pattern works
- 24 single-field actions provide no isolation benefit; all affect the same state object
- Cognitive overhead: a reviewer must scan all 24 cases to understand state shape

## Proposed Fix
Consolidate scalar setters into 3–5 compound actions:
- LOAD_VISIT_DATA: { visit, patient, invoice, medications, specialty, insurer }
- UPDATE_FORM_FIELD: { field: keyof VisitFormState, value: unknown }
- SET_UI_STATE: { editingMode?, saving?, showDeleteConfirm?, ... }
Keep CANCEL_EDIT and INIT_FORM_AND_MEDICATIONS as-is.

## Validation Criteria
- [ ] Reducer has ≤8 action types
- [ ] bun run tsc --noEmit passes
- [ ] bun run lint passes
- [ ] VisitDetail renders; edit and save flows work manually
title: Refactor VisitDetail reducer: collapse 24 SET_X actions into compound actions

description:
## Issue
CSRF protection calls localStorage.getItem('csrf_token') but the token is never stored there by the auth flow. The result is always null, making CSRF validation silently a no-op on the frontend side.

## Severity
P2 IMPORTANT — Security control that appears active but is bypassed; could contribute to CSRF vulnerability if combined with a state-changing GET endpoint.

## Location
medsimples/frontend/src — search for localStorage.getItem('csrf_token') and where csrf_token should be written post-login

## Proposed Fix
1. Identify where the backend sends the CSRF token (Set-Cookie header, response body, etc.)
2. Store it correctly (sessionStorage, httpOnly cookie, or Redux state)
3. Read from the correct location in the axios interceptor/header attachment
4. Or remove the dead code if CSRF is fully cookie-based and SameSite=Strict

## Validation Criteria
- [ ] CSRF token storage and retrieval use the same mechanism
- [ ] Network requests include a valid CSRF token or CSRF is demonstrably not needed (SameSite cookie)
- [ ] No localStorage.getItem('csrf_token') calls that return null
title: Fix: CSRF token always null (localStorage.getItem never populated)

description:
## Issue
useVisitData fetches visit data in two separate useEffect calls that fire at different times: first the visit record, then the related patient/invoice. Between the two effects there is a window where visit is populated but patient/invoice are still null, which could cause child components to render with inconsistent state.

## Severity
P2 IMPORTANT — Could cause brief rendering of empty/wrong data, confusing error states, or null dereferences in components that assume all data arrives together.

## Location
medsimples/frontend/src/hooks/useVisitData.ts

## Proposed Fix
Combine into a single fetch: use Promise.all([fetchVisit(), fetchPatient(visitId), fetchInvoice(visitId)]) so all data lands atomically. Use a single loading/error state for the whole batch.

## Validation Criteria
- [ ] useVisitData issues at most one state update that sets all fields together
- [ ] Loading state is true until ALL sub-fetches complete
- [ ] No intermediate state where visit is populated and patient is null
title: Fix: useVisitData two-phase fetch exposes partial state window
Fix: medication search in VisitDetail has no abort on unmount

description:
## Issue
InvoiceDetailNFSePanel is 529 lines with a 30+ prop interface. Callers must thread every piece of NFS-e state and every handler through props. This makes the component hard to test and tightly couples InvoiceDetail to NFS-e implementation details.

## Severity
P2 IMPORTANT — Prop-drilling antipattern that negates the refactoring benefit of extracting the subcomponent.

## Location
medsimples/frontend/src/components/invoice-detail/InvoiceDetailNFSePanel.tsx

## Proposed Fix
Option A (preferred): Consume the parent reducer's dispatch and state slice directly rather than threading all values as props. Create a NfseState sub-slice in InvoiceDetail reducer and dispatch NFSE_* actions.
Option B: Group related props into typed sub-objects: { nfseState: NfseDisplayState, nfseHandlers: NfseHandlers } — reduces prop count from 30 to ~3.
At minimum, props count must drop to ≤10 (or ≤3 grouped sub-objects).

## Validation Criteria
- [ ] InvoiceDetailNFSePanel props count ≤ 10 or grouped into ≤3 sub-objects
- [ ] TypeScript compiles with no errors
- [ ] NFS-e issue, cancel, and PDF flows work manually
title: Refactor InvoiceDetailNFSePanel: decompose 30+ prop interface

description:
## Issue
The login/callback flow uses a returnTo query parameter to redirect users after authentication. This parameter is not validated against an allowlist or origin check, meaning an attacker can craft a link like /login?returnTo=https://evil.com and redirect authenticated users to a malicious site.

## Severity
P2 IMPORTANT — Open redirect vulnerability; OWASP A01 / CWE-601. Commonly used in phishing attacks targeting authenticated users.

## Location
medsimples/frontend/src — search for 'returnTo' in router/auth/callback code

## Proposed Fix
Before using returnTo, validate it is a relative path on the same origin:
```ts
const safeReturnTo = returnTo?.startsWith('/') && \!returnTo.startsWith('//') 
  ? returnTo 
  : '/';
navigate(safeReturnTo);
```
Or use URL API to check same-origin: new URL(returnTo, window.location.origin).origin === window.location.origin.

## Validation Criteria
- [ ] returnTo with external URL (https://evil.com) redirects to '/' instead
- [ ] returnTo with valid internal path (/patients/123) works correctly
- [ ] Unit test covers both cases
title: Fix: open redirect via returnTo param — validate against allowlist

description:
## Issue
Several TypeScript interfaces and types are defined independently in both the new hook files and the new subcomponent files, describing the same domain objects (Visit, Invoice, Medication, Patient). This duplication means type changes must be applied in multiple places and types may silently diverge.

## Severity
P2 IMPORTANT — Type drift risk; a field added to one copy but not the other won't cause a compile error if the objects are structurally compatible.

## Location
medsimples/frontend/src/hooks/ (useVisitData.ts, useInvoiceFormData.ts, usePatientFormData.ts)
medsimples/frontend/src/components/invoice-detail/, visit-detail/, patient-form/

## Proposed Fix
Define canonical types in medsimples/frontend/src/types/ (or existing schema files) and import them in all hooks and subcomponents. Remove local redefinitions.

## Validation Criteria
- [ ] No duplicate interface/type declarations for the same domain object
- [ ] All hooks and subcomponents import from a shared types module
- [ ] bun run tsc --noEmit passes
title: Fix: duplicated TypeScript type definitions across hooks and subcomponents

description:
## Issue
VisitDetailDisplayMode contains a conditional branch that can never be reached because its guard condition is always false given the data flow from VisitDetail. The dead branch makes the component logic harder to follow and may indicate a misunderstanding of the intended behavior.

## Severity
P2 IMPORTANT — Dead code adds cognitive overhead and may indicate a logic bug (the intended branch was supposed to be reachable).

## Location
medsimples/frontend/src/components/visit-detail/VisitDetailDisplayMode.tsx — search for the unreachable conditional

## Proposed Fix
1. Determine whether the condition SHOULD ever be true (design intent)
2. If never true: remove the dead branch entirely
3. If it should be true in some cases: fix the data flow so the condition can be satisfied

## Validation Criteria
- [ ] No dead branches in VisitDetailDisplayMode
- [ ] If behavior changed, manual verification that display mode renders correctly in all visit states
title: Fix: unreachable dead branch in VisitDetailDisplayMode

description:
## Issue
InvoiceDetailLineItems wraps its content in a TableContainer which is itself nested inside another TableContainer from the parent InvoiceDetail. Nested TableContainers both apply overflow:auto scroll containers, resulting in a double scrollbar — one for the inner table and one for the outer container.

## Severity
P2 IMPORTANT — Visual bug; users see two simultaneous scrollbars on the invoice line items table.

## Location
medsimples/frontend/src/components/invoice-detail/InvoiceDetailLineItems.tsx — outer TableContainer wrapping the table
medsimples/frontend/src/components/InvoiceDetail.tsx — parent TableContainer

## Proposed Fix
Remove one TableContainer. If InvoiceDetailLineItems is always used inside InvoiceDetail's TableContainer, remove the inner one. If InvoiceDetailLineItems must be self-contained, remove the outer one in InvoiceDetail.

## Validation Criteria
- [ ] Invoice detail page shows exactly one scrollbar on the line items area
- [ ] Table still scrolls horizontally on narrow screens
title: Fix: nested TableContainer in InvoiceDetailLineItems causes double scrollbar

description:
## Issue
VisitDetailDisplayMode renders a Quill editor in read-only mode but still passes an onChange handler to it. React-quill fires onChange even when readOnly=true on internal changes (e.g., programmatic content updates), which can trigger unintended state updates or console warnings.

## Severity
P2 IMPORTANT — Subtle state corruption risk; read-only components should never mutate state on onChange.

## Location
medsimples/frontend/src/components/visit-detail/VisitDetailDisplayMode.tsx — ReactQuill or StyledReactQuill usage with onChange prop

## Proposed Fix
Remove the onChange prop when readOnly=true. If the component conditionally renders edit/display modes, use a ternary:
```tsx
{isEditing ? (
  <StyledReactQuill value={notes} onChange={setNotes} />
) : (
  <StyledReactQuill value={notes} readOnly />
)}
```

## Validation Criteria
- [ ] No onChange passed to Quill when readOnly=true
- [ ] Visit notes still display correctly in view mode
- [ ] No console warnings from Quill in display mode
title: Fix: onChange on readOnly Quill editor in VisitDetailDisplayMode

description:
## What
Replace array index used as React key with stable unique identifiers across 15 occurrences in 12 files.

## Context
Index keys cause bugs when lists are reordered or filtered — React reuses DOM nodes incorrectly. Use a stable unique identifier from the data (e.g., item.id, item.slug). When no natural ID exists (static/derived lists), construct a composite key from stable values.

Institutional learning: keys must be unique across the ENTIRE list, not just within sub-groups (e.g., dial codes '1' shared by US/Canada caused duplicates).

## Testing
- [ ] Each list renders correctly with new keys
- [ ] Reordering or filtering a list does not corrupt component state
- [ ] No duplicate key warnings in console

## Validation
- [ ] react-doctor re-scan (`sandbox-exec -f /tmp/no-network.sb react-doctor medsimples/frontend/src --no-lint -y`) shows 0 'array index as key' warnings
- [ ] No regressions in list-heavy pages (InvoiceDetail, VisitForm, InsurerPieChart)

## Files
- src/components/dashboard/OverdueChart.tsx
- src/components/InvoiceDetail.tsx
- src/components/InvoiceReports.tsx
- src/components/exam-results/ExamResultDetail.tsx
- src/components/layout/BreadCrumbs.tsx
- src/components/VisitForm.tsx

- src/components/common/AddressSuggestionBanner.tsx
- src/components/dashboard/InsurerPieChart.tsx
- src/components/cognitest/CogniTestResults.tsx
- src/components/InvoiceForm.tsx
- src/components/FiscalSettings.tsx
- src/components/shared/MedicationSelector.tsx
- src/components/TemplateForm.tsx

## Dependencies
None
title: Fix: replace array index keys with stable IDs

description:
## Issue
autoRefreshCount is stored in the VisitDetail useReducer state, which means every increment triggers a full component re-render. This value is only used as a trigger to re-fire a useEffect — it has no display role. Using state for this wastes a render cycle per refresh.

## Severity
P3 NICE-TO-HAVE — Minor performance optimization; each auto-refresh causes an extra render.

## Location
medsimples/frontend/src/components/VisitDetail.tsx — autoRefreshCount in VisitDetailState, SET_AUTO_REFRESH_COUNT reducer case

## Proposed Fix
Replace with useRef:
```ts
const autoRefreshRef = useRef(0);
// To trigger refresh:
autoRefreshRef.current += 1;
// In useEffect dependency:
// useEffect([..., autoRefreshRef.current]) won't work — keep a separate refresh state boolean instead
```
Actually the cleanest fix: replace with a refreshToken state (boolean that flips) or use a dedicated useCallback to refetch explicitly rather than using a counter.

## Validation Criteria
- [ ] Auto-refresh does not trigger an extra render per cycle
- [ ] Visit data still refreshes on schedule
title: Chore: autoRefreshCount in VisitDetail reducer should be useRef not state

description:
## Issue
StyledReactQuill uses React.lazy to defer loading the heavy react-quill JavaScript bundle. However, the Quill CSS stylesheet is imported at the top of the file with a static import statement, which is always eagerly evaluated by the module bundler regardless of the lazy() wrapper. This means the CSS adds to the initial bundle even though the component itself is deferred.

## Severity
P3 NICE-TO-HAVE — Partial optimization; the JS benefit of lazy loading is real but the CSS still contributes to initial render cost.

## Location
medsimples/frontend/src/components/shared/StyledReactQuill.tsx — CSS import at top of file

## Proposed Fix
Move the CSS import inside the dynamically imported module (inside the lazy callback), or use a CSS-in-JS approach, or accept the trade-off and document it with a comment explaining why eager CSS is intentional.

## Validation Criteria
- [ ] Either CSS is deferred alongside JS, or a code comment explains the intentional eager load
- [ ] Quill editor still renders and styles correctly
title: Chore: Quill CSS imported eagerly despite lazy JS in StyledReactQuill

## Issue
useCepLookup (CEP = Brazilian postal code lookup) has error messages written without proper Portuguese accents. Per CLAUDE.md: 'Portuguese text MUST use proper accents (não, será, convênio, incluídos, observações, etc.)'.

## Severity
P3 NICE-TO-HAVE — Style/convention violation per project rules.

## Location
medsimples/frontend/src/hooks/useCepLookup.ts (or wherever CEP lookup error messages are defined)

## Proposed Fix
Review all string literals in useCepLookup and related error messages. Apply correct accents:
- 'Nao encontrado' → 'Não encontrado'
- 'CEP invalido' → 'CEP inválido'
- etc.

## Validation Criteria

- [ ] bun run tsc --noEmit passes
title: Fix: useCepLookup missing Portuguese accents in error messages

description:
## Issue
InvoiceDetail uses window.innerWidth directly inside the render function to conditionally render layout. This is a side effect during render and does not respond to window resize events — the layout is calculated once on initial render and never updates if the user resizes their browser.

## Severity
P3 NICE-TO-HAVE — SSR incompatibility risk; does not update on resize; should use React's preferred API.

## Location
medsimples/frontend/src/components/InvoiceDetail.tsx — window.innerWidth reference in render

## Proposed Fix
Replace with MUI's useMediaQuery hook:
```ts
const isMobile = useMediaQuery(theme.breakpoints.down('sm'));
```
This is reactive to resize events and SSR-safe.

## Validation Criteria
- [ ] No window.innerWidth in InvoiceDetail render
- [ ] Layout responds correctly to browser resize
- [ ] bun run tsc --noEmit passes
title: Fix: window.innerWidth in InvoiceDetail render — replace with useMediaQuery

description:
## What
Refactor 4 useEffect hooks that simulate event handlers into actual event handlers (onClick, onChange, onSubmit).

## Context
A useEffect that runs based on a state flag (e.g., `useEffect(() => { if (shouldSubmit) doSubmit(); }, [shouldSubmit])`) is simulating an event handler. This pattern adds unnecessary state, delays execution by one render, and makes control flow hard to follow.

The fix is to move the logic directly into the triggering event handler (e.g., the onClick of a Save button or onSubmit of a form), removing the flag state entirely.

## Testing
- [ ] Form submission works correctly in PatientForm, OrganizationDetail, FiscalSettings, InsurerDetail
- [ ] No double-submission or missed submission bugs
- [ ] Loading/error states still work correctly

## Validation
- [ ] react-doctor re-scan (`sandbox-exec -f /tmp/no-network.sb react-doctor medsimples/frontend/src --no-lint -y`) shows 0 'useEffect simulating event handler' warnings
- [ ] Manual QA on each affected form: create, edit, save flows work correctly

## Files
- src/components/PatientForm.tsx
- src/components/OrganizationDetail.tsx
- src/components/FiscalSettings.tsx
- src/components/InsurerDetail.tsx

## Dependencies
None
title: Fix: move useEffect event handlers to actual event handlers

description:
## What
Remove or fix autoFocus attribute usage that causes usability issues for sighted and non-sighted users.

## Context
autoFocus can disorient screen reader users by immediately shifting focus without user intent. It also causes issues on mobile. The fix depends on context:
- In dialogs/modals: MUI Dialog has built-in focus management — remove autoFocus from fields inside dialogs
- On standalone form pages (LoginPage, RegistrationForm): autoFocus on the first field is common and acceptable UX. Keep if intentional, or disable if it causes issues.
- Review each case individually rather than bulk-removing all instances.

## Testing
- [ ] Keyboard navigation still works correctly on affected pages
- [ ] Screen reader (VoiceOver on macOS) does not jump unexpectedly
- [ ] Login form still usable and feels natural
- [ ] MUI Dialog focus management works in InvoiceDetail and ApiKeys

## Validation
- [ ] react-doctor re-scan (`sandbox-exec -f /tmp/no-network.sb react-doctor medsimples/frontend/src --no-lint -y`) shows 0 or justified reduction in 'autoFocus' warnings
- [ ] No regression in form usability

## Files
- src/components/InvoiceDetail.tsx
- src/components/ApiKeys.tsx
- src/components/LoginPage.tsx
- src/components/FiscalSettings.tsx
- src/components/RegistrationForm.tsx

## Dependencies
None
title: Fix: autoFocus accessibility issues (6 occurrences)

description:
## What
Extract the default empty array prop in PDFCanvasOverlay.tsx:153 to a module-level constant to prevent new array reference creation on every render.

## Context
In React, `defaultValue=[]` or `prop = []` as an inline default creates a new array reference on each render. This causes child components that receive the prop to re-render unnecessarily, and can break memo/useEffect dependency comparisons.

Fix:
```typescript
// BAD — new array every render
function PDFCanvasOverlay({ fields = [] }: Props) { ... }

// GOOD — stable reference
const EMPTY_FIELDS: Field[] = [];
function PDFCanvasOverlay({ fields = EMPTY_FIELDS }: Props) { ... }
```

## Testing
- [ ] PDF canvas overlay renders correctly with no fields passed
- [ ] PDF canvas overlay renders correctly with fields passed
- [ ] No unnecessary re-renders (verify with React DevTools if available)

## Validation
- [ ] react-doctor re-scan (`sandbox-exec -f /tmp/no-network.sb react-doctor medsimples/frontend/src --no-lint -y`) shows 0 'default prop array reference' warnings
- [ ] PDF editor functionality unaffected

## Files
- src/components/pdf-editor/PDFCanvasOverlay.tsx

## Dependencies
None
title: Fix: stabilize default prop [] in PDFCanvasOverlay

description:
## What
Wrap the 6 recharts imports in dashboard components with React.lazy() to enable code splitting and reduce initial bundle size.

## Context
recharts is a heavy library. Dashboard chart components are only rendered when the user navigates to the dashboard, making them ideal candidates for lazy loading. MedSimplesLayout.tsx already uses React.lazy + Suspense for route-level splitting — extend the same pattern to these chart subcomponents.

Since these are used inside other components (not as top-level routes), lazy load at the import site:
```typescript
const RevenueChart = React.lazy(() => import('./dashboard/RevenueChart'));
// Wrap usage in <Suspense fallback={<CircularProgress />}>
```

The existing PageLoadingFallback component can be reused as the Suspense fallback.

## Testing
- [ ] Dashboard loads and charts render correctly
- [ ] Network tab shows recharts split into a separate chunk
- [ ] Bundle size decreases measurably (check bun run build output)
- [ ] Suspense fallback shows correctly during chart load

## Validation
- [ ] react-doctor re-scan (`sandbox-exec -f /tmp/no-network.sb react-doctor medsimples/frontend/src --no-lint -y`) shows 0 'heavy library not code-split' warnings
- [ ] bun run build succeeds with recharts in a separate chunk
- [ ] Dashboard page still fully functional

## Files
- src/components/dashboard/OverdueChart.tsx
- src/components/dashboard/VisitsByDayChart.tsx
- src/components/dashboard/RevenueChart.tsx
- src/components/dashboard/InsurerPieChart.tsx
- src/components/dashboard/CollectionRateChart.tsx
- src/components/shared/StyledReactQuill.tsx
- src/components/dashboard/Dashboard.tsx (parent component wrapping lazy imports in Suspense)

## Dependencies
None

description:
## Issue
Auto-refresh adds 20+ LOC complexity for minimal UX gain. NFSe processes in <5s.

## Fix
Remove autoRefreshing state, autoRefreshCount, and entire useEffect. Keep manual refresh button.

description:
## Issue
VisitForm and InvoiceForm use Promise.all for parallel fetches, causing entire page to fail if any single request fails.

## Severity
P2 IMPORTANT - Poor user experience, page breaks even when most data loaded successfully

## Location
- medsimples/frontend/src/components/VisitForm.tsx:151-162
- medsimples/frontend/src/components/InvoiceForm.tsx:180-184

## Why This Matters
If fetching doctors fails but patient/insurers succeed, user sees generic error and no form appears. Should show partial data with warning.

## Current Code (VisitForm)
```typescript
const fetches: Promise<any>[] = [
  cachedAxiosGet(`/api/patients/${patientId}`),
  cachedAxiosGet("/api/doctors"),
  cachedAxiosGet("/api/insurers/basic"),
];
const [patientResponse, doctorsResponse, insurersResponse] = await Promise.all(fetches);
```

## Fix Required
```typescript
const results = await Promise.allSettled(fetches);
const patientResponse = results[0].status === 'fulfilled' ? results[0].value : null;
// Handle partial failures with warnings
```

## Validation Criteria
- [ ] Form displays even if doctors API fails
- [ ] User sees specific warnings for failed requests
- [ ] Patient data always required
- [ ] Graceful degradation for non-critical data

## Testing Steps
1. Mock doctors API to fail
2. Load visit form
3. Should show patient data with warning
title: P2: Promise.all fails fast without error isolation

description:

## Validation

- [ ] No polling when tab inactive

description:
## Problem

CogniTest DB models use UUID() columns for doctor_id and organization_id (commission.py), but MedSimples integer PKs are stored as strings like '8' and '36' in the JWT. This causes commission queries to fail — the commissions page shows 'Request failed' after login.

## Root cause
- cognitest/backend/app/models/commission.py: doctor_id = Column(UUID()), organization_id = Column(UUID())
- MedSimples user IDs are integers, not UUIDs
- DoctorInfo.user_id and org_id are now strings (e.g. '8', '36') after the fix in be60847

## What needs to be decided
- Should CogniTest store integer doctor_id/org_id to match MedSimples? (requires migration)
- Or should MedSimples migrate to UUID PKs? (medsimples-d7o8 epic covers this)
- Or should CogniTest use a separate UUID for its own records and map to MS integer IDs?

## Likely correct fix
Wait for medsimples-d7o8 (UUID v7 migration) to complete, then CogniTest Commission model UUIDs will align naturally. In the meantime, commissions feature is broken for password-login users.
title: CogniTest Commission model uses UUID for doctor_id/org_id but MedSimples sends integer IDs

## What

Add a display_name field to the TestDefinition model and database schema that stores the translated test name for the current locale.

## Context

- Frontend must use i18n to translate name_key → display name
