description:
## What

Create and configure the medtest test database on PostgreSQL server 10.10.0.43:6432 for CogniTest end-to-end testing. This database will be isolated from production data.

## Context

**Database Server:** 10.10.0.43:6432
**Target Database:** medtest
**Migrations:** 26 Alembic migrations in cognitest/backend/alembic/versions/
**Access:** Uses ~/.pgpass (ensure chmod 600)
**Production DB:** cognitest database exists at same host (DO NOT TOUCH)

**Key Constraints:**
- Production database is at 10.10.0.43:6432/cognitest (read-only for reference)
- MedSimples production is at 10.10.0.43:6432/med (DO NOT MODIFY without approval)
- CogniTest uses String(36) for UUID fields, not native PostgreSQL UUID
- Session status stored as String(20), not native ENUM

**Migration Chain:** Linear sequence from 001_initial_schema through 026. Key migrations:
- 001: Base tables (organizations, patients, test_definitions, test_sessions, test_results)
- 002-013: Test definition seeds
- 014b: cognitest_payments table
- 016: pricing tables
- 019: notification_logs table

## Testing

- [ ] Connect to PostgreSQL server: psql -h 10.10.0.43 -p 6432 -U rbm postgres
- [ ] Check if medtest database exists: \l medtest
- [ ] Create if missing: CREATE DATABASE medtest OWNER rbm;
- [ ] Connect to medtest: \c medtest
- [ ] Run migrations from cognitest/backend: uv run alembic upgrade head
- [ ] Verify migration count: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM alembic_version; (should be 1 row)
- [ ] Verify core tables exist: \dt (should show ~20 tables including test_sessions, cognitest_payments, notification_logs)
- [ ] Verify test definitions seeded: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM test_definitions; (should be 13)
- [ ] Check test codes: SELECT code FROM test_definitions ORDER BY code;
- [ ] Verify default batteries: SELECT * FROM test_batteries;

## Validation

- [ ] All 26 migrations applied successfully
- [ ] Core tables present: organizations, patients, test_sessions, test_results, cognitest_payments, notification_logs
- [ ] 13 test definitions seeded with snake_case codes (digit_span, reaction_time, etc.)
- [ ] Default test batteries exist
- [ ] No errors in migration output
- [ ] Database isolated from production cognitest and med databases

## Dependencies

None - this is the first step
title: Set up medtest database for CogniTest

description:
## What

Create cognitest/backend/.env.test configuration file pointing to medtest database and configure all required secrets for end-to-end testing.

## Context

**Environment Files Pattern:**
- Production: cognitest/backend/.env (if exists)
- Example: cognitest/backend/.env.example (reference template)
- Test: cognitest/backend/.env.test (create this)

**Backend Ports:** CogniTest backend runs on 8001 (MedSimples uses 8000)
**Frontend Ports:** CogniTest frontend runs on 5174 (MedSimples uses 5173)

**Required Environment Variables:**
```
DATABASE_URL=postgresql://rbm@10.10.0.43:6432/medtest
DEBUG=true
ENV=development
BASE_URL=http://localhost:8001
FRONTEND_URL=http://localhost:5174
SECRET_KEY=[shared with MedSimples for JWT validation]
MEDSIMPLES_API_URL=http://localhost:8000/api
MEDSIMPLES_FRONTEND_URL=http://localhost:5173
STRIPE_SECRET_KEY=sk_test_...
STRIPE_PUBLISHABLE_KEY=pk_test_...
STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET=whsec_...
KAPSO_API_KEY=[from Kapso account]
KAPSO_BASE_URL=https://api.kapso.io/v1
COGNITEST_WEBHOOK_SECRET=[shared secret for service-to-service auth]
DEV_ACCESS_KEY=[optional - bypasses session access_key validation]
```

**Service-to-Service Auth:** COGNITEST_WEBHOOK_SECRET must match between CogniTest and MedSimples for X-Webhook-Secret header validation using hmac.compare_digest.

**Kapso API Key:** Required for WhatsApp template sending. Check if templates are Meta-approved (1-3 day lead time).

**Stripe Keys:** Test mode keys (sk_test_*, pk_test_*). Webhook secret from Stripe CLI or dashboard.

**DEV_ACCESS_KEY:** If set, bypasses session.access_key validation in all patient-facing endpoints. Useful for testing but insecure.

## Testing

- [ ] Copy from .env.example: cp cognitest/backend/.env.example cognitest/backend/.env.test
- [ ] Update DATABASE_URL to point to medtest database
- [ ] Verify SECRET_KEY matches MedSimples (needed for JWT validation)
- [ ] Verify MEDSIMPLES_API_URL points to local MedSimples backend (port 8000)
- [ ] Add KAPSO_API_KEY from Kapso account settings
- [ ] Add Stripe test keys (sk_test_*, pk_test_*, whsec_*)
- [ ] Set COGNITEST_WEBHOOK_SECRET (generate with: python -c 'import secrets; print(secrets.token_urlsafe(32))')
- [ ] Optional: Set DEV_ACCESS_KEY for easier testing
- [ ] Test database connection: psql $DATABASE_URL -c 'SELECT 1;'
- [ ] Verify file permissions: ls -la cognitest/backend/.env.test (should not be world-readable)

## Validation

- [ ] .env.test file created with all required variables
- [ ] DATABASE_URL successfully connects to medtest
- [ ] SECRET_KEY matches MedSimples for JWT compatibility
- [ ] KAPSO_API_KEY is valid (check with curl or API test)
- [ ] Stripe keys are test mode (sk_test_*, pk_test_*)
- [ ] Service URLs point to localhost with correct ports
- [ ] File not committed to git (in .gitignore)

## Dependencies

Depends on: medsimples-m07c.1 (medtest database must exist)
title: Configure CogniTest backend .env.test for medtest database

description:
## What

Create a test patient in the MedSimples medtest database with valid Brazilian phone number for WhatsApp testing. Ensure patient is in an organization with cognitest module enabled.

## Context

**Database:** MedSimples medtest database (if separate from cognitest medtest)
**Production Note:** This is a PRODUCTION database write operation - requires explicit approval

**MedSimples Module System:**
- Organizations have `enabled_modules` (PostgreSQL array column)
- CogniTest requires 'cognitest' in organization.enabled_modules
- Check with: SELECT id, name, enabled_modules FROM organizations;
- Enable with: UPDATE organizations SET enabled_modules = array_append(enabled_modules, 'cognitest') WHERE id = X;

**Brazilian Phone Validation:**
- Format: E.164 (+5511999999999)
- Area codes requiring 9th digit: 11-19, 21, 22, 24, 27, 28
- Example valid number: +5511991430052
- Country code stored separately: whatsapp_country='55', whatsapp='11991430052'

**Patient Fields Required:**
- name (String)
- cpf (String 14, format: XXX.XXX.XXX-XX)
- whatsapp (String, local number without country code)
- whatsapp_country (String 5, default '55' for Brazil)
- organization_id (FK to organizations)

**CPF Format:** Use fake but valid format: XXX.XXX.XXX-XX. Does not need to pass checksum validation for testing.

**Institutional Learning:** Only 1 doctor in production has phone data (id=3, already E.164). Zero patients have phone data currently. This test patient will likely be the first.

## Testing

- [ ] Connect to MedSimples database: psql -h 10.10.0.43 -p 6432 -U rbm med
- [ ] Find organization with cognitest module: SELECT id, name, enabled_modules FROM organizations WHERE 'cognitest' = ANY(enabled_modules);
- [ ] If none exist, enable for test org: UPDATE organizations SET enabled_modules = array_append(enabled_modules, 'cognitest') WHERE id = [ORG_ID];
- [ ] Generate fake CPF: 123.456.789-00 (or use online generator)
- [ ] Create test patient with INSERT:
  ```sql
  INSERT INTO patients (name, cpf, whatsapp_country, whatsapp, organization_id)
  VALUES ('Paciente Teste CogniTest', '123.456.789-00', '55', '11991234567', [ORG_ID])
  RETURNING id, name, whatsapp_country, whatsapp;
  ```
- [ ] Document patient ID and phone number
- [ ] Verify organization module: SELECT enabled_modules FROM organizations WHERE id = [ORG_ID];
- [ ] Test E.164 construction: SELECT '+' || whatsapp_country || whatsapp FROM patients WHERE id = [PATIENT_ID];

## Validation

- [ ] Test patient created with valid ID
- [ ] Patient has valid Brazilian phone number (area code 11 with 9)
- [ ] whatsapp_country = '55'
- [ ] Patient belongs to organization with 'cognitest' in enabled_modules
- [ ] E.164 phone format validates: +5511991234567
- [ ] Patient details documented (ID, name, phone) for use in later beads

## Dependencies

Depends on: medsimples-m07c.1 (database must exist)
Note: May require user approval for production database write
title: Create test patient in MedSimples medtest database

description:
## What

Set up ngrok tunnel to expose CogniTest backend webhook endpoint and configure Kapso to send delivery status updates to the tunnel URL.

## Context

**Webhook Endpoint:** POST /api/v1/webhooks/kapso (cognitest/backend/app/routers/webhooks.py:52)
**Webhook Handler:** Currently logs delivery status but does NOT update notification_logs table (noted as "Future" enhancement)
**No Auth:** Kapso webhook endpoint has no authentication (unlike /organization-created which uses webhook secret)

**ngrok Setup:**
- Install: brew install ngrok (or download from ngrok.com)
- Auth: ngrok config add-authtoken [YOUR_TOKEN]
- Start tunnel: ngrok http 8001 (CogniTest backend port)
- Copy HTTPS URL: https://[random].ngrok.io

**Kapso Webhook Configuration:**
- Dashboard: https://app.kapso.io (or API endpoint)
- Webhook URL format: https://[random].ngrok.io/api/v1/webhooks/kapso
- Events: message.sent, message.delivered, message.read, message.failed

**Webhook Payload Example:**
```json
{
  "event": "message.delivered",
  "message_id": "kapso_msg_123",
  "timestamp": "2026-02-15T12:00:00Z",
  "status": "delivered"
}
```

**Known Limitation:** The webhook handler logs events but doesn't update notification_logs.status. For E2E test, we'll validate webhook delivery through logs, not database updates.

**Alternative to ngrok:** localhost.run, serveo.net, or Kapso's test webhook feature (if available)

## Testing

- [ ] Install ngrok: brew install ngrok (or verify installed)
- [ ] Start ngrok tunnel: ngrok http 8001
- [ ] Copy HTTPS forwarding URL (e.g., https://abc123.ngrok.io)
- [ ] Verify tunnel: curl https://[NGROK_URL]/api/health (should return CogniTest health check)
- [ ] Log into Kapso dashboard at https://app.kapso.io
- [ ] Navigate to Webhooks settings
- [ ] Add webhook URL: https://[NGROK_URL]/api/v1/webhooks/kapso
- [ ] Enable events: message.sent, message.delivered, message.read, message.failed
- [ ] Send test webhook from Kapso dashboard (if available)
- [ ] Monitor CogniTest backend logs for incoming webhook
- [ ] Verify webhook received with: grep "Kapso webhook" cognitest/backend/logs/* (or check stdout)

## Validation

- [ ] ngrok tunnel running and stable
- [ ] HTTPS URL accessible from internet
- [ ] Kapso webhook configured with correct URL
- [ ] Test webhook delivery confirmed in logs
- [ ] Webhook endpoint responds with 200 OK
- [ ] ngrok session documented (URL expires on restart)
- [ ] ngrok process running in separate terminal/tmux session

## Dependencies

Depends on: medsimples-m07c.2 (backend .env.test configured)
Note: Must complete before sending WhatsApp messages in bead .7
title: Configure Kapso webhook for test environment

description:
## What

Start CogniTest backend and frontend services with .env.test configuration. Verify all health endpoints, database connectivity, and MedSimples integration before proceeding with testing.

## Context

**Service Ports:**
- CogniTest Backend: 8001 (uvicorn)
- CogniTest Frontend: 5174 (SvelteKit/Vite)
- MedSimples Backend: 8000 (must be running for integration)
- MedSimples Frontend: 5173 (optional for E2E test)

**Backend Startup:**
```bash
cd cognitest/backend
uv sync  # Install dependencies
uv run uvicorn app.main:app --reload --port 8001 --env-file .env.test
```

**Frontend Startup:**
```bash
cd cognitest/frontend
bun install  # If not already done
bun run dev  # Starts on port 5174
```

**Health Endpoints:**
- CogniTest: GET http://localhost:8001/api/health
- MedSimples: GET http://localhost:8000/api/health

**MedSimples Integration Points:**
- app/services/medsimples_client.py:30+ (MedSimplesClient)
- Endpoints: /token/validate, /cognitest/patient, /cognitest/doctor
- Auth: X-Webhook-Secret header with COGNITEST_WEBHOOK_SECRET

**Critical Services:**
- PostgreSQL: 10.10.0.43:6432/medtest (connection pooling via SQLAlchemy)
- Kapso API: https://api.kapso.io/v1 (check KAPSO_API_KEY valid)
- Stripe API: https://api.stripe.com (test mode with sk_test_*)

**Common Startup Issues:**
- Missing .env.test file (should error immediately)
- Database connection failure (check DATABASE_URL)
- MedSimples not running (integration endpoints will fail)
- KAPSO_API_KEY invalid (WhatsApp sends will fail)

## Testing

- [ ] Start MedSimples backend first: cd medsimples && uv run uvicorn backend.app.main:app --reload --port 8000
- [ ] Verify MedSimples running: curl http://localhost:8000/api/health
- [ ] Start CogniTest backend: cd cognitest/backend && uv run uvicorn app.main:app --reload --port 8001 --env-file .env.test
- [ ] Check backend logs for startup errors (DATABASE_URL, migrations, Alembic)
- [ ] Verify CogniTest health: curl http://localhost:8001/api/health
- [ ] Check database connection: curl http://localhost:8001/api/health should show db:true
- [ ] Start CogniTest frontend: cd cognitest/frontend && bun run dev
- [ ] Open frontend in browser: http://localhost:5174
- [ ] Check browser console for API connection errors
- [ ] Test MedSimples integration: curl -X POST http://localhost:8001/api/v1/auth/register (should proxy to MedSimples)
- [ ] Monitor logs for any warnings or errors

## Validation

- [ ] CogniTest backend running on port 8001 without errors
- [ ] CogniTest frontend running on port 5174
- [ ] MedSimples backend running on port 8000
- [ ] Health endpoints return 200 OK for both services
- [ ] Database connection successful (check logs)
- [ ] No import errors or missing dependencies
- [ ] Frontend loads without console errors
- [ ] CORS configured correctly (CogniTest accepts requests from localhost:5174)
- [ ] All services running in separate terminal sessions or tmux panes

## Dependencies

Depends on: medsimples-m07c.2 (.env.test configured), medsimples-m07c.4 (ngrok tunnel running)
title: Start CogniTest services with test configuration

description:
## What

Create a CogniTest session for the test patient through the MedSimples UI or API. Configure session with selected cognitive tests, capture session ID and access_key for WhatsApp testing.

## Context

**Session Creation Endpoint:** POST /api/v1/sessions (cognitest/backend/app/routers/sessions.py:121)

**Session Request Schema:**
```json
{
  "patient_id": "uuid",  // From bead .3
  "battery_id": "uuid (optional)",  // Or use test_ids
  "test_ids": ["uuid", ...],  // Custom test selection
  "clinician_user_id": "uuid (optional)",  // Doctor ID
  "requires_payment": true,  // Set to true for E2E test
  "test_code": "string (optional)"  // For free credit check
}
```

**Session Flow:**
1. Check free credit eligibility if test_code provided
2. Create custom TestBattery if test_ids provided without battery_id
3. Create TestSession with status AWAITING_PAYMENT (if requires_payment=true) or PENDING
4. Auto-send WhatsApp notification (cognitest_test_assignment) if clinician_user_id set
5. Return session with session_url and access_key

**WhatsApp Auto-Trigger:** Only happens if clinician_user_id is provided (routers/sessions.py:204). This calls NotificationService.send_test_assignment_notification_for_session() which:
- Fetches patient data from MedSimples API
- Fetches doctor data from MedSimples API
- Sends template cognitest_test_assignment with 7 variables + payment URL button

**Available Tests (13):** digit_span, reaction_time, trail_making, finger_tapping, go_nogo, n_back, stroop, spatial_span, word_list_learning, ess, gds, snap_iv, mmse

**Session Access:** session_url = {FRONTEND_URL}/test/{session_id}?key={access_key}
**Payment URL:** {FRONTEND_URL}/pay/{session_id}?key={access_key}

**Test Definition IDs:** Query medtest database:
```sql
SELECT id, code, display_name FROM test_definitions WHERE code IN ('digit_span', 'reaction_time') ORDER BY code;
```

**Organization Linking:** CogniTest organization must have medsimples_org_id set. Check with:
```sql
SELECT id, medsimples_org_id FROM organizations WHERE medsimples_org_id IS NOT NULL;
```

## Testing

- [ ] Query test definition IDs: psql -h 10.10.0.43 -p 6432 -U rbm medtest -c "SELECT id, code FROM test_definitions WHERE code IN ('digit_span', 'reaction_time');"
- [ ] Get patient UUID from bead .3 documentation
- [ ] Get organization from patient: SELECT organization_id FROM patients WHERE id = '[PATIENT_ID]';
- [ ] Verify org has medsimples_org_id: SELECT medsimples_org_id FROM organizations WHERE id = '[ORG_ID]';
- [ ] Create session via API:
  ```bash
  curl -X POST http://localhost:8001/api/v1/sessions \
    -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
    -d '{
      "patient_id": "[PATIENT_UUID]",
      "test_ids": ["[DIGIT_SPAN_ID]", "[REACTION_TIME_ID]"],
      "clinician_user_id": "[DOCTOR_UUID]",
      "requires_payment": true
    }'
  ```
- [ ] Capture response with session_id and access_key
- [ ] Check backend logs for WhatsApp notification trigger
- [ ] Verify session created: psql -c "SELECT id, status, access_key FROM test_sessions WHERE id = '[SESSION_ID]';"
- [ ] Verify session status is AWAITING_PAYMENT
- [ ] Check notification log: SELECT * FROM notification_logs WHERE test_session_id = '[SESSION_ID]';
- [ ] Document: session_id, access_key, payment_url, session_url

## Validation

- [ ] Session created successfully with status AWAITING_PAYMENT
- [ ] access_key is 32-character alphanumeric token
- [ ] Custom TestBattery created with 2 tests
- [ ] WhatsApp notification logged in notification_logs table
- [ ] Notification status is SENT (if Kapso succeeded) or FAILED (if error)
- [ ] Patient should receive WhatsApp message with payment link (verify in bead .7)
- [ ] Session accessible at /test/{session_id}?key={access_key} (returns payment required)
- [ ] Payment URL accessible at /pay/{session_id}?key={access_key}

## Dependencies

Depends on: medsimples-m07c.3 (test patient created), medsimples-m07c.5 (services running)
title: Create CogniTest session for test patient

description:
## What

Execute the complete WhatsApp flow end-to-end: receive test assignment message, complete payment via PIX, receive confirmation, access test via link, complete consent, execute all configured tests (DigitSpan, ReactionTime), and verify scoring.

## Context

**WhatsApp Flow (Expected):**
1. Patient receives cognitest_test_assignment template with payment link
2. Patient clicks payment link → /pay/{session_id}?key={access_key}
3. Patient completes Stripe PIX payment
4. Stripe webhook fires → payment status PAID, session status PENDING
5. (Payment confirmation WhatsApp NOT auto-triggered yet - known gap)
6. Patient accesses test link → /test/{session_id}?key={access_key}
7. Consent form → Test overview → Individual tests → Completion

**Payment Flow:**
- Stripe Payment Element with PIX support
- Webhook: POST /api/v1/payments/webhooks/stripe
- Events: payment_intent.succeeded, payment_intent.payment_failed
- On success: Payment → PAID, Session AWAITING_PAYMENT → PENDING
- Auto-commission created via CommissionService

**Consent Flow (LGPD):**
- POST /api/v1/sessions/{id}/consent
- Requires 3 checkboxes: data_collection, data_sharing, data_deletion_rights
- Frontend: ConsentForm.svelte component

**Test Execution:**
- GET /api/v1/tests/{session_id}/next → returns next test from battery
- POST /api/v1/tests/{session_id}/start → creates TestResult, status IN_PROGRESS
- POST /api/v1/tests/{session_id}/response → incremental trial data
- POST /api/v1/tests/{session_id}/complete → triggers scoring, stores results

**Test Components:**
- DigitSpan: cognitest/frontend/src/lib/components/tests/DigitSpan.svelte
- ReactionTime: cognitest/frontend/src/lib/components/tests/ReactionTime.svelte

**Scoring:**
- DigitSpanScorer: app/scoring/digit_span_scorer.py
- ReactionTimeScorer: app/scoring/reaction_time_scorer.py
- Returns: {primary_score, secondary_scores, valid, validity_flags}

**Session States:** AWAITING_PAYMENT → (payment) → PENDING → (start) → IN_PROGRESS → (complete) → COMPLETED

**Kapso Webhook Monitoring:** Watch ngrok tunnel and backend logs for delivery status updates (sent/delivered/read/failed)

**Known Issues:**
- cognitest_payment_confirmed NOT auto-sent after Stripe webhook (manual trigger needed)
- cognitest_test_completed_doctor NOT auto-sent on session completion
- Kapso webhook doesn't update notification_logs.status

## Testing

- [ ] Monitor WhatsApp on test patient phone for incoming message
- [ ] Verify message contains: greeting, patient name, doctor title/name, test names, payment link
- [ ] Click "Continuar" button in WhatsApp → should open payment page
- [ ] Verify payment page loads at /pay/{session_id}?key={access_key}
- [ ] Complete Stripe PIX payment flow
- [ ] Monitor backend logs for Stripe webhook: payment_intent.succeeded
- [ ] Verify payment status: SELECT status FROM cognitest_payments WHERE session_id = '[SESSION_ID]';
- [ ] Verify session status: SELECT status FROM test_sessions WHERE id = '[SESSION_ID]';
- [ ] (MANUAL) Send payment confirmation WhatsApp if needed:
  ```bash
  curl -X POST http://localhost:8001/api/v1/notifications/payment-confirmed \
    -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
    -d '{"session_id": "[SESSION_ID]", "phone": "+5511991234567", "country_code": "55"}'
  ```
- [ ] Access test link from WhatsApp or manually: http://localhost:5174/test/[SESSION_ID]?key=[ACCESS_KEY]
- [ ] Complete LGPD consent form (3 checkboxes)
- [ ] Verify consent stored: SELECT * FROM consents WHERE session_id = '[SESSION_ID]';
- [ ] Start session, verify status → IN_PROGRESS
- [ ] Execute DigitSpan test: follow on-screen instructions, complete all trials
- [ ] Verify DigitSpan result: SELECT primary_score FROM test_results WHERE session_id = '[SESSION_ID]' AND test_definition_id = '[DIGIT_SPAN_ID]';
- [ ] Execute ReactionTime test: follow on-screen instructions
- [ ] Verify ReactionTime result stored
- [ ] Complete session, verify status → COMPLETED
- [ ] Monitor Kapso webhook deliveries via ngrok logs
- [ ] Check notification_logs: SELECT * FROM notification_logs WHERE test_session_id = '[SESSION_ID]' ORDER BY sent_at;

## Validation

- [ ] WhatsApp message received with correct template variables
- [ ] Payment link accessible and functional
- [ ] Stripe payment succeeds (check Stripe dashboard test mode)
- [ ] Session transitions from AWAITING_PAYMENT → PENDING after payment
- [ ] Commission created: SELECT * FROM commissions WHERE cognitest_payment_id = '[PAYMENT_ID]';
- [ ] Test link accessible with access_key
- [ ] Consent form submitted and stored
- [ ] Session transitions PENDING → IN_PROGRESS → COMPLETED
- [ ] Both tests executed successfully
- [ ] Scoring algorithms ran (primary_score populated)
- [ ] raw_responses JSON stored for both tests
- [ ] Kapso webhooks received (check for sent/delivered/read events)
- [ ] Response times under 2 seconds for webhook endpoints
- [ ] No errors in backend logs during flow

## Dependencies

Depends on: medsimples-m07c.6 (session created), medsimples-m07c.4 (webhook configured)
title: Execute WhatsApp test flow end-to-end

description:
## What

Validate that test results are correctly stored, scores calculated accurately, and all database tables updated as expected. Verify scoring algorithm correctness and data integrity.

## Context

**Database Tables to Verify:**
- test_sessions: status=COMPLETED, started_at, completed_at timestamps
- test_results: primary_score, secondary_scores, raw_responses JSON
- consents: all 3 LGPD checkboxes stored
- cognitest_payments: status=PAID, amount, stripe_receipt_url
- notification_logs: all sent messages with kapso_message_id
- commissions: auto-created from payment with correct calculations

**Scoring Validation:**
- DigitSpanScorer: primary_score = max(forward_span, backward_span)
- ReactionTimeScorer: primary_score = median reaction time
- secondary_scores: JSON with additional metrics
- valid: boolean flag
- validity_flags: array of issues (empty if valid)

**Raw Responses Format:**
```json
[
  {"timestamp": 1234567890, "type": "digit_span_trial", "data": {...}},
  {"timestamp": 1234567891, "type": "digit_span_trial", "data": {...}}
]
```

**Commission Calculation:**
- Platform fees: Stripe 3.99% + IOF 3.5% = 7.49% total
- Commission rate: 25% default (from commission_rates table)
- Commission = (gross_amount - fees) * commission_rate
- Status: PENDING (awaits approval/payout)

**Data Integrity Checks:**
- Session has exactly 2 test_results (DigitSpan, ReactionTime)
- Each test_result.status = COMPLETED
- raw_responses is valid JSON array
- primary_score is numeric and reasonable (not NULL, not negative)
- completed_at > started_at for session and each test
- Payment amount matches expected pricing

**Institutional Learning:** CogniTest uses String(36) for UUIDs, String(20) for session status (not native PostgreSQL types)

## Testing

- [ ] Query session: 
  ```sql
  SELECT id, status, started_at, completed_at, access_key 
  FROM test_sessions 
  WHERE id = '[SESSION_ID]';
  ```
- [ ] Verify status = 'COMPLETED' and timestamps are set
- [ ] Query test results:
  ```sql
  SELECT tr.id, td.code, tr.status, tr.primary_score, tr.secondary_scores, 
         LENGTH(tr.raw_responses::text) as response_size, tr.valid, tr.validity_flags
  FROM test_results tr
  JOIN test_definitions td ON tr.test_definition_id = td.id
  WHERE tr.session_id = '[SESSION_ID]'
  ORDER BY tr.completed_at;
  ```
- [ ] Verify 2 results exist (digit_span, reaction_time)
- [ ] Verify primary_score is populated and reasonable
- [ ] Check raw_responses size > 100 bytes (has trial data)
- [ ] Validate raw_responses JSON:
  ```sql
  SELECT raw_responses::json FROM test_results WHERE session_id = '[SESSION_ID]' LIMIT 1;
  ```
- [ ] Query consent:
  ```sql
  SELECT data_collection, data_sharing, data_deletion_rights, consented_at
  FROM consents
  WHERE session_id = '[SESSION_ID]';
  ```
- [ ] Verify all 3 boolean fields are TRUE
- [ ] Query payment:
  ```sql
  SELECT status, amount, currency, stripe_payment_intent_id, paid_at, stripe_receipt_url
  FROM cognitest_payments
  WHERE session_id = '[SESSION_ID]';
  ```
- [ ] Verify status = 'PAID', amount > 0, stripe_payment_intent_id starts with 'pi_'
- [ ] Query commission:
  ```sql
  SELECT c.amount, c.rate_applied, c.status, c.platform_fees, cp.gross_amount
  FROM commissions c
  JOIN cognitest_payments cp ON c.cognitest_payment_id = cp.id
  WHERE cp.session_id = '[SESSION_ID]';
  ```
- [ ] Verify commission calculation: commission.amount ≈ (gross - platform_fees) * rate_applied
- [ ] Query notification logs:
  ```sql
  SELECT notification_type, status, kapso_message_id, sent_at, template_used
  FROM notification_logs
  WHERE test_session_id = '[SESSION_ID]'
  ORDER BY sent_at;
  ```
- [ ] Count notifications sent (should be at least 1: test_assignment)
- [ ] Check for kapso_message_id (indicates successful send)

## Validation

- [ ] Session status = COMPLETED with valid timestamps
- [ ] 2 test_results exist with status = COMPLETED
- [ ] Both tests have primary_score populated
- [ ] raw_responses contains valid JSON trial arrays
- [ ] Scoring appears reasonable (not 0, not absurdly high)
- [ ] Consent record exists with all 3 checkboxes TRUE
- [ ] Payment status = PAID with Stripe payment_intent_id
- [ ] Commission created with correct calculation (7.49% fees, 25% rate)
- [ ] At least 1 notification logged (test_assignment)
- [ ] No NULL values in critical fields
- [ ] Timestamps are logical (completed_at > started_at)
- [ ] All foreign keys resolve correctly

## Dependencies

Depends on: medsimples-m07c.7 (WhatsApp flow executed)
title: Validate test results and scoring

description:
## What

Document all findings from the end-to-end test: bugs discovered, performance issues, UX problems, edge cases not handled. Capture screenshots/logs, clean up test data if needed, and create follow-up beads for any issues.

## Context

**Documentation Locations:**
- Test summary: Add as comment to this epic bead (medsimples-m07c)
- Screenshots: cognitest/docs/testing/ (create directory)
- Logs: Extract relevant sections from backend logs
- Follow-up beads: Create in .beads/ for bugs/improvements

**Common Issues to Check:**
- WhatsApp template variables not rendering correctly
- Phone normalization issues (E.164 format, 9th digit)
- Payment flow errors (Stripe PIX, webhook delays)
- Consent form UX (unclear language, checkbox issues)
- Test instructions unclear or missing translations
- Scoring algorithm errors (verify with manual calculation)
- Session state transitions not smooth
- Access key validation too strict or too lenient
- Performance bottlenecks (slow API calls, database queries)
- CORS issues between frontend/backend
- Mobile responsiveness (WhatsApp links often open on mobile)

**Known Gaps to Document:**
- Only cognitest_test_assignment auto-triggers (others require manual intervention)
- Kapso webhook doesn't update notification_logs.status
- No automatic payment_confirmed WhatsApp after Stripe success
- No automatic test_completed_doctor notification
- No automatic results_ready_patient notification

**Learnings to Capture:**
- Use LEARNED/DECISION/PATTERN/INVESTIGATION/FACT prefixes
- Document template approval status (Meta takes 1-3 days)
- Note actual phone normalization behavior vs expected
- Capture webhook delivery times (Kapso SLA)
- Document Stripe webhook reliability
- Record actual test execution times vs estimated

**Cleanup Considerations:**
- Test patient can remain in database (useful for future tests)
- Test session can remain for reference
- Payment should NOT be refunded (test mode, no real charge)
- Notification logs valuable for audit
- ngrok tunnel can be closed
- Services can be stopped

## Testing

- [ ] Review all backend logs from session creation through completion
- [ ] Extract any ERROR or WARNING lines
- [ ] Take screenshots of:
  - [ ] WhatsApp message received
  - [ ] Payment page
  - [ ] Consent form
  - [ ] Test instructions
  - [ ] Test execution screens
  - [ ] Completion screen
- [ ] Document timing:
  - [ ] Time to receive WhatsApp after session creation
  - [ ] Payment webhook latency (Stripe event → backend processing)
  - [ ] Kapso webhook delivery time
  - [ ] Total test execution time
- [ ] Check for race conditions or timing issues
- [ ] Verify mobile responsiveness (if tested on mobile)
- [ ] Review UX pain points noted during testing
- [ ] List any manual interventions required (payment_confirmed WhatsApp, etc.)
- [ ] Close ngrok tunnel: Ctrl+C in ngrok terminal
- [ ] Stop services: Ctrl+C in backend/frontend terminals
- [ ] Optional: Clean up test data:
  ```sql
  -- Review before running:
  SELECT * FROM test_sessions WHERE id = '[SESSION_ID]';
  -- DELETE FROM test_sessions WHERE id = '[SESSION_ID]'; -- Cascades to test_results, consents
  ```

## Validation

- [ ] Test summary documented as bead comment with LEARNED prefix
- [ ] All bugs filed as separate beads with priority/type set
- [ ] Screenshots saved to cognitest/docs/testing/
- [ ] Performance metrics captured (API response times, webhook latencies)
- [ ] Known gaps documented for future work
- [ ] Manual workarounds documented (e.g., how to trigger payment_confirmed)
- [ ] Institutional learnings logged to knowledge.jsonl via bd comment commands:
  ```bash
  bd comment medsimples-m07c "LEARNED: [key insight from E2E test]"
  bd comment medsimples-m07c "INVESTIGATION: [root cause of any issue found]"
  bd comment medsimples-m07c "PATTERN: [recommended pattern discovered]"
  ```
- [ ] Follow-up beads created for:
  - [ ] Auto-trigger payment_confirmed WhatsApp
  - [ ] Auto-trigger test_completed_doctor notification
  - [ ] Implement Kapso webhook notification_logs.status updates
  - [ ] Any bugs or UX issues discovered
- [ ] Services stopped cleanly
- [ ] ngrok tunnel closed
- [ ] Ready to close epic as completed

## Dependencies

Depends on: medsimples-m07c.8 (results validated)
title: Document test findings and cleanup

description:
## Issue
BreadCrumbs.tsx is a single 117-line function with a large switch-like if/else chain for route matching. Hard to maintain as routes grow.

## Location
medsimples/frontend/src/components/layout/BreadCrumbs.tsx

## Fix
Extract route config into a data structure, use route matching utilities instead of if/else chain.

## Validation
- [ ] All breadcrumb routes still render correctly
- [ ] Adding new route requires only data, not new if/else
title: BreadCrumbs: monolithic 117-line function needs decomposition

description:
## What

PATCH /api/v1/sessions/{session_id} does not validate the access_key, allowing any caller who knows a session UUID to set status=completed and bypass payment.

## Context

sessions.py:279 update_session accepts status changes without calling validate_access_key(). The access_key is visible in session_url returned by the API (sessions.py:213), and session UUIDs may be guessable via enumeration if they're sequential (they are UUIDs so probably not, but the auth gap remains).

## Fix

1. Either require authenticated doctor (get_current_doctor) on update_session, OR
2. Call validate_access_key(session, key) where key is required in the request body
3. Determine which is appropriate — if this endpoint is called by the test-taker (patient), use access_key. If by the doctor, use JWT auth.

## Files

- cognitest/backend/app/routers/sessions.py (update_session)
title: Security: update_session missing access key validation — payment bypass

description:
CogniTest backend currently has no repository layer. Routers and services perform db.query() directly, violating the separation of concerns enforced in MedSimples.

CURRENT STATE:
- 69 direct db operations in router files (sessions, tests, results, payments, credits, commissions, notifications)
- 76 direct db operations in service files
- No repositories/ directory exists
- Routers create and mutate records directly (e.g. sessions.py:195, credits.py:40, tests.py:208)

TARGET STATE (match MedSimples):
- routers/ -> delegate to services only, no db access
- services/ -> delegate persistence to repositories, contain business logic
- repositories/ -> all db.query, db.add, db.commit calls; extend BaseRepository
- BaseRepository with generic CRUD (create, get_by_id, get_all, update, delete)

SCOPE: cognitest/backend/app/
- Create repositories/base_repository.py (adapt from medsimples pattern)
- Create per-model repositories: session, test_definition, test_battery, test_result, payment, commission, commission_rate, credits, notification_log, organization, patient, pricing
- Refactor services to use repositories instead of direct db access
- Refactor routers to use services only

REFERENCE: medsimples/backend/app/repositories/base_repository.py and the DoctorService/DoctorRepository pair as the canonical pattern to follow.

NOTE: CogniTest does not use PostgreSQL RLS, so expire_on_commit and RLS context setup are NOT needed in the repository layer.
title: Refactor CogniTest backend to layered architecture with repository pattern

description:

Simplify InvoicePaymentRepository.create() - remove relationship pre-loading

description:
## Overview

Modernize both FastAPI backends to align with current FastAPI 0.131.0 conventions and eliminate legacy patterns. This work improves IDE support, enables Pydantic v2 Rust-backed JSON serialization, and enforces consistent patterns across MedSimples and CogniTest.

## Scope

**MedSimples backend** (medsimples/backend/): 126 endpoints, 21 routers
**CogniTest backend** (cognitest/backend/): 37 endpoints, 9 routers

## Changes

1. Version bumps: fastapi, uvicorn, pydantic in both pyproject.toml files
2. Return type annotations on all 163 route handlers (replaces response_model= decorator param)
3. Union[X, Y] -> X | Y syntax modernization (2 files in medsimples)
4. List[X] -> list[X] and Optional[X] -> X | None cleanup
5. CogniTest auth.py JSONResponse pattern replacement with proper Pydantic schemas

## Key constraints from research

- RLS context is lost after db.commit() - this affects re-queries. POST responses that reload relationships are already handled by the repository pattern; return type annotations do not change this behavior.
- Pydantic response_model= and return type annotations are functionally equivalent in FastAPI 0.115+. Switching is safe.
- JSONResponse in cognitest/auth.py proxies MedSimples magic-link endpoint - errors must still return correct HTTP status codes (502, 400) with Portuguese messages.
title: Modernize FastAPI backends: version bumps, return type annotations, and response pattern standardization

close_reason: Version pins updated and uv sync confirmed clean
description:
## What

Update version pins in both pyproject.toml files to current releases:

- fastapi: medsimples >=0.115.0 -> >=0.131.0, cognitest >=0.109.0 -> >=0.131.0
- uvicorn: cognitest >=0.27.0 -> >=0.30.0
- pydantic: cognitest >=2.0 -> >=2.11.4 (match medsimples specificity)

## Context

FastAPI 0.130.0 introduced Pydantic v2 Rust-backed JSON serialization for all response_model= endpoints — this is the main perf win and activates automatically after the version bump. CogniTest is 6 minor versions behind medsimples.

## Testing

- [ ] uv sync in medsimples/ - resolves without conflicts
- [ ] uv sync in cognitest/backend/ - resolves without conflicts
- [ ] Both backends start successfully (uv run uvicorn ... --port 800X)
- [ ] No import errors at startup

## Validation

- [ ] medsimples/pyproject.toml has fastapi>=0.131.0

description:
## Issue
cognitest/backend/app/routers/auth.py line 49 raises HTTPException with detail=str(e) where e is a MedSimplesAPIError constructed from raw response.text. Under DB failure conditions in MedSimples, this can leak psycopg2 error messages including table names, constraint names, and submitted email values to unauthenticated callers.

## Severity
P2 - Only triggered on DB errors in MedSimples (not normal operation), but the endpoint is unauthenticated and rate-limited only at 2/hour.

## Location
cognitest/backend/app/routers/auth.py:49
medsimples/backend/app/services/cognitest_service.py:154

## Fix
1. In cognitest/routers/auth.py: parse the JSON detail from MedSimplesAPIError and return only a sanitized Portuguese message, never raw str(e)
2. In medsimples/services/cognitest_service.py line 154: replace str(e) in the ValidationError message with a generic message; log the real error

## Validation
- [ ] DB failure in MedSimples during registration returns generic Portuguese error to cognitest caller
- [ ] Full error details are logged server-side, not returned to client
title: Sanitize MedSimplesAPIError detail before forwarding in cognitest register endpoint

description:
## Issue
cognitest/backend/app/routers/auth.py lines 73-82: status 422 and 429 responses from MedSimples are forwarded verbatim to the unauthenticated caller. The docstring claims errors are sanitized but 422/429 bodies are not. If MedSimples ever enriches its 422 detail, that content flows to callers without filtering.

## Severity
P3 - Defense-in-depth. Not exploitable today; MedSimples 422 bodies contain only field names and validation types.

## Location
cognitest/backend/app/routers/auth.py:73-82

## Fix
Replace the pass-through with fixed Portuguese messages:
  status 422 -> JSONResponse(422, {'detail': 'Dados inválidos'}, headers={'Cache-Control': 'no-store'})
  status 429 -> JSONResponse(429, {'detail': 'Limite de tentativas atingido'}, headers={'Cache-Control': 'no-store'})
Only status 200 should pass through verbatim.

## Validation
- [ ] 422 from MedSimples returns fixed Portuguese string to cognitest caller, not raw MedSimples body
- [ ] 429 from MedSimples returns fixed Portuguese string
- [ ] 200 from MedSimples still passes through verbatim
title: Replace verbatim 422/429 pass-through in magic-link proxy with fixed safe responses

close_reason: Replaced custom ThemeContext with MUI ThemeProvider wrapper. Removed ColorChangeThemePopper, ChangeThemeFab. Updated all consumers to use useTheme(). No reload hack. TSC and lint pass.
description:
## What
Replace the custom ThemeContext with MUI 6's built-in color scheme management. Eliminate the page reload hack.

## Plan Reference
plans/frontend-redesign.md - Phase 1, section 1d

## Implementation

### File: medsimples/frontend/src/context/ThemeContext.tsx
The current file has:
- ThemeStateContext (stores full MUI theme object)
- ThemeDispatchContext (dispatch to change theme)
- useThemeState() hook
- Four theme variants (default, secondary, success, dark)
- window.location.reload() on theme change

Replace with:
- REMOVE ThemeStateContext and ThemeDispatchContext
- REMOVE the four-variant theme logic
- Import the single theme from themes/index.ts
- Provide just a ThemeProvider wrapper that passes the single theme
- For mode switching: MUI 6's useColorScheme() hook does everything automatically (reads/writes localStorage, no reload needed)

### Update consumers
All files that import useThemeState():
- Sidebar.tsx uses it for theme.palette.mode checks and theme.breakpoints -> replace with useTheme() from @mui/material/styles
- MedSimplesLayout.tsx uses it for ThemeProvider -> simplify to import theme directly from themes/index.ts
- Any other file using useThemeState() for palette.mode -> replace with useTheme() and theme.applyStyles('dark', {...}) pattern

CRITICAL: Replace ALL instances of theme.palette.mode === 'dark' ? X : Y with theme.applyStyles('dark', {...}) in styled/sx props. The old pattern causes CSS variable flicker.

### Update App.tsx provider hierarchy
Current: ThemeProvider (custom) wraps everything
New: MUI's ThemeProvider with the single theme. Remove custom theme provider from hierarchy if no longer needed.

## Validation
- bun run tsc --noEmit passes
- bun run lint passes  
- No references to old theme variants (default, secondary, success) remain
- No window.location.reload() calls for theme switching
- useThemeState() either removed or simplified to just return useTheme()
- Grep for 'palette.mode ===' returns zero results (all converted to applyStyles)
- App loads without errors
- Theme colors apply correctly (teal primary, slate text)

## Dependencies
Depends on Phase 1c (new theme file)
title: Phase 1d: Replace ThemeContext with MUI 6 useColorScheme

description:
Pre-existing critical security gaps found during dashboard plan review. Multiple endpoints are unauthenticated and unscoped:

1. list_sessions (sessions.py:223) — no auth, returns all sessions across all orgs. Partially addressed in 0ekz.2 for the dashboard case, but the general endpoint needs full fix.
2. update_session (sessions.py:279) — no auth, no access key check. Any caller who knows a session UUID can flip status to 'completed', bypassing payment. Combined with unauth list_sessions = full IDOR.
3. GET /results/session/{session_id} and /pdf — no auth, exposes full cognitive test results and patient PDFs with only a session UUID.
4. POST /credits/organization/{id}/add — no auth, admin endpoint for granting credits is publicly accessible (anyone can grant themselves unlimited credits).
5. GET /credits/organization/{id} — no auth, credit balance queryable for any org.

These are pre-existing and not introduced by the dashboard feature, but become more exploitable once the dashboard ships and session UUIDs become indirectly discoverable.

Priority order: update_session (data integrity) → credits add (financial) → results endpoints (patient data privacy) → credits read → list_sessions general case.
title: Security: add auth to list_sessions, update_session, results, credits endpoints

description: The 'substituted' value was in the Python NFSeStatusEnum but never added to the PostgreSQL nfsestatusenum via migration. Setting original.status='substituted' in substitute_nfse() caused a DB error caught by the broad except Exception handler, silently failing. The SEFIN API call had already succeeded, so the new issuance was created but original.status and invoice.invoice_number were never updated. Fix: migration adding enum value + data cleanup, explicit flush after success branch, improved get_issuance_status to prefer non-substituted issuances.
title: Fix NFSE substitution: missing 'substituted' enum value and stale data

Migrating CogniTest to reportlab would require rewriting HTML/CSS-based PDF templates to Python code. The maintenance overhead and risk outweigh the benefits of unification. Each project already uses its chosen library correctly.
description: MedSimples uses reportlab (pure Python, no system deps) for PDFs. CogniTest uses weasyprint (HTML+CSS to PDF, requires libpango/libcairo system libs). Evaluate whether to: (1) migrate MedSimples to weasyprint for better HTML/CSS templating, or (2) migrate CogniTest to reportlab to eliminate system deps. Consider: template maintainability, output quality, deployment complexity, and developer experience.
title: Evaluate unifying PDF generation across MedSimples and CogniTest

description:
Enable doctors to self-register on MedSimples/CogniTest via two entry points on the Meu Teste Médico landing page:

1. **Email flow**: Doctor fills name + email → receives magic link email → clicks → completes onboarding form (specialty, CRM, clinic) → account activated
2. **WhatsApp flow**: Doctor clicks 'Cadastre-se pelo WhatsApp' → wa.me pre-filled link → sends message → Kapso bot triggers a WhatsApp Flow (native form) collecting name/specialty/CRM → backend sends magic link via WhatsApp → doctor clicks → account activated

Magic links are DB-stored tokens (not JWT) for LGPD revocability, single-use, 48h TTL.
Registration creates: Organization + User (active=False) + Doctor records atomically.
After activation: call CogniTest webhook to seed free credits (currently broken per medsimples-219).
CogniTest module enabled on new org by default.
title: Doctor self-registration via magic link (email + WhatsApp)

description:
## What
Two new endpoints in MedSimples backend auth router:

1. `POST /api/auth/magic-link` — accept name + email (+ optional phone/source), generate token, trigger notification
2. `GET /api/auth/magic-link/verify?token=<raw>` — render confirmation page (two-phase flow to prevent Outlook SafeLinks consuming token)
3. `POST /api/auth/magic-link/verify` — consume token, create Org + User + Doctor atomically, return JWT

## Context

### Token security (CRITICAL)
- Token generation: `secrets.token_urlsafe(32)` — 256-bit entropy, URL-safe
- Storage: **HMAC-SHA256**, NOT bcrypt — bcrypt is a DoS vector on high-entropy tokens (no cost parameter benefit)
  ```python
  import hmac, hashlib
  token_hash = hmac.new(settings.secret_key.encode(), raw_token.encode(), hashlib.sha256).hexdigest()
  ```
- Lookup: store token_hash, look up by hash on verify — never store or return raw token after generation

### Two-phase verify flow (prevents Outlook SafeLinks token consumption)
- `GET /verify?token=` → renders a confirmation page ('Click to complete registration')
- `POST /verify` (with token in body) → atomically consumes token and creates entities
- This is the standard pattern; single-step GET causes ~30% link failures in corporate email environments

### Atomic `UPDATE ... RETURNING` for single-use token enforcement (prevents TOCTOU race)
```python
# In UserService.consume_magic_token():
result = db.execute(
    update(MagicToken)
    .where(MagicToken.token_hash == token_hash)
    .where(MagicToken.used_at.is_(None))
    .where(MagicToken.expires_at > func.now())
    .values(used_at=func.now())
    .returning(MagicToken)
)
token = result.scalars().first()
if not token:
    raise HTTPException(400, 'Invalid or expired token')
# Then create Org → User → Doctor
```
This eliminates the race between check and use.

### Rate limiting strategy
- Per-email: **silent 200 + background task** (prevents email enumeration timing side-channel)
  - Rate check happens BEFORE sending; if over limit, return 200 but do nothing
  - Limit: 5 requests/hour per email address
- Per-IP: **429** at 20 requests/hour (anti-spam)
- Use SlowAPI decorator on the endpoint

### PostgreSQL advisory lock for duplicate org prevention
```python
# Prevent concurrent registrations for same email
db.execute(text('SELECT pg_advisory_xact_lock(:key)'), {'key': hash(email) % 2**31})
```

### Atomic creation order (from medsimples-124 pattern)
```python
org = Organization(name=clinic_name, enabled_modules=['cognitest'])
db.add(org); db.flush()  # get org.id for RLS

# Set RLS context for subsequent inserts
db.execute(text("SELECT set_config('app.org_id', :id, true)"), {'id': str(org.id)})

user = User(org_id=org.id, email=email, active=True, ...)
db.add(user); db.flush()

doctor = Doctor(organization_id=org.id, user_id=user.id, ...)
db.add(doctor); db.flush()

db.commit()
```

### CogniTest webhook (fixes medsimples-219)
- After commit: call CogniTest `POST /webhooks/organization-created` with `X-Webhook-Secret` header
- Use httpx singleton from `app.state.http_client` (not per-call `async with httpx.AsyncClient()`)
- Wrap in try/except — webhook failure MUST NOT fail the registration transaction
- Use `pybreaker` circuit breaker if repeated failures occur

### httpx anti-pattern to fix
The existing `medsimples_client.py` creates a new `httpx.AsyncClient` per call — this is a known anti-pattern.
Fix: create singleton in FastAPI `lifespan` on `app.state.http_client`, share across calls.

## Files
- `medsimples/backend/app/routers/auth.py` — add three endpoints
- `medsimples/backend/app/services/user_service.py` — add `request_magic_link()`, `consume_magic_token()`
- `medsimples/backend/app/schemas/auth.py` — add `MagicLinkRequest`, `MagicLinkVerifyResponse`
- `medsimples/backend/app/settings.py` — add `COGNITEST_BACKEND_URL`, `SECRET_KEY` if not present
- `medsimples/backend/app/main.py` — add lifespan to create httpx singleton on `app.state`

## Testing
- [ ] POST with valid email returns 200 (does NOT reveal if email exists — always 200)
- [ ] POST same email 6x in 1h → 5th+ are silently dropped (still 200)
- [ ] POST from same IP 21x in 1h → 429
- [ ] GET /verify with valid unused token → confirmation page rendered, token NOT consumed
- [ ] POST /verify with valid unused token → creates records, returns JWT
- [ ] POST /verify twice with same token → second returns 400
- [ ] POST /verify with expired token → 400
- [ ] POST /verify with invalid token → 400 (same error message as expired — no oracle)
- [ ] Created User has active=True, org_id set
- [ ] Created Doctor has organization_id set
- [ ] CogniTest webhook called after creation; failure doesn't abort registration
- [ ] cognitest in enabled_modules
- [ ] Concurrent POST /verify with same token (race condition) → exactly one succeeds

## Validation
- [ ] No plaintext tokens ever logged or returned after generation
- [ ] token_hash stored as HMAC-SHA256 hex string
- [ ] RLS context (app.org_id) set before User/Doctor inserts
- [ ] Two-phase verify: GET does not consume token
title: MedSimples: registration endpoints (request + verify)

description:
## What
Add email sending capability to MedSimples backend using Resend, with a magic link registration email template.

## Context
- **Use Resend** (not aiosmtplib, not python-anymail). Resend has the simplest setup, best deliverability for Brazilian ISPs (Gmail, Outlook, UOL, Terra, IG), and satisfies LGPD via a signed DPA with SCCs.
- Resend Python SDK is synchronous (requests-based). Use `asyncio.to_thread(_send_sync, params)` as the async wrapper — same pattern as KapsoClient.
- **Use FastAPI BackgroundTasks** for sending (not inline await) so the HTTP response returns immediately regardless of delivery outcome — prevents email enumeration via timing side-channel.
- **Template design**: table-based HTML layout with fully inline CSS — required for Outlook and legacy Brazilian webmail (UOL, Terra, IG). Use `_base.html` Jinja2 parent template.
- Token expiry to state in email: **15 minutes for login links, 24 hours for registration/activation**. The 48h original plan is too long per security research.
- Include `List-Unsubscribe` header on every email (required by Gmail/Yahoo bulk sender rules since Feb 2024).
- Do NOT include patient PII (CPF, test scores, medical data) in email bodies — LGPD minimization.
- Add suppression list: store unsubscribed emails in DB, check before calling send_magic_link.

## DNS / Deliverability checklist (must complete before first production send)
- SPF: `TXT @ "v=spf1 include:amazonses.com ~all"` (Resend sends via SES)
- DKIM: `CNAME resend._domainkey → resend._domainkey.resend.dev` (auto-rotated via CNAME)
- DMARC: `TXT _dmarc "v=DMARC1; p=quarantine; rua=mailto:dmarc@domain"`

## Files
- `medsimples/backend/app/services/email_service.py` — EmailService with `send_magic_link(email, token, purpose='registration')`
- `medsimples/backend/app/templates/email/_base.html` — Jinja2 base template (table layout, inline CSS)
- `medsimples/backend/app/templates/email/magic_link.html` — PT-BR magic link template
- `medsimples/backend/app/settings.py` — add: `resend_api_key`, `email_from`, `email_enabled`
- `medsimples/backend/.env.example` — document new vars

## Testing
- [ ] `send_magic_link()` sends email with correct link via Resend API
- [ ] Link contains raw token (not hash) with correct `/cadastro?token=` URL
- [ ] Email renders with PT-BR text including proper accents (avaliação, médico, etc.)
- [ ] Graceful failure when `resend_api_key` not set: logs warning, returns without raising
- [ ] BackgroundTasks: HTTP response returns before email delivery completes
- [ ] `List-Unsubscribe` header present in every send

## Validation
- [ ] Raw token never logged
- [ ] FROM_EMAIL and resend_api_key configurable via env (not hardcoded)
- [ ] email_enabled=False skips send (for dev/test environments)
title: MedSimples: email sending service + magic link template

description:
## What
Add a `MagicToken` table to MedSimples backend to store revocable, single-use registration tokens.

## Context
- Prefer DB tokens over JWT for LGPD compliance (can be revoked/expired on demand)
- **HMAC-SHA256, NOT bcrypt** — bcrypt is for low-entropy passwords; a 32-byte token_urlsafe has 256-bit entropy making bcrypt's slowness pure DoS risk with no security gain. Use `hmac.new(settings.secret_key.encode(), token.encode(), 'sha256').hexdigest()`
- Index on `token_hash` for fast O(1) lookups — inherently timing-safe
- Add `invalidated_at` column to support token rotation (supersede old tokens when new one is requested)
- Add `ip_address` for LGPD audit trail (Marco Civil da Internet requires 6-month retention)
- RLS: table does NOT need org_id (pre-registration, no org yet)
- **LGPD**: do NOT store `name` here — accept it only at verify step to minimize PII in token table. Store only email + hash + audit fields.
- Rows must be deleted on use (same transaction as registration) and via scheduled cleanup for expired rows (purge after 24h grace period)

## Schema
```python
class MagicToken(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'magic_tokens'
    id: UUID (pk)
    token_hash: str(64)   # HMAC-SHA256 hex, NOT bcrypt
    email: str(255)
    phone: str(50) | None  # E.164 format if via WhatsApp
    phone_country: str(5)  # default '55'
    source: str(20)        # 'email' | 'whatsapp'
    expires_at: DateTime(timezone=True)  # now + 24h (not 48h — see research)
    used_at: DateTime | None
    invalidated_at: DateTime | None       # set when superseded by new token
    invalidation_reason: str(50) | None   # 'superseded'
    ip_address: str(45) | None            # for audit (INET in migration)
    created_at: DateTime
```

## Files
- `medsimples/backend/app/models.py` — add MagicToken model
- `medsimples/backend/alembic/versions/<rev>_add_magic_tokens.py` — migration

## Testing
- [ ] Migration upgrades and downgrades cleanly
- [ ] `token_hash` column has unique index
- [ ] `used_at` NULL + `invalidated_at` NULL = active token
- [ ] Expired token (expires_at < now) detectable via query
- [ ] HMAC token lookup: hash incoming token → query by hash → no application-level string comparison needed

## Validation
- [ ] Model matches migration column types exactly (use INET for ip_address in migration)
- [ ] downgrade() restores prior state
- [ ] Name is NOT stored in this table (LGPD minimization)
title: MedSimples: MagicToken model + migration

description:
## What
Create a WhatsApp Flow (native form inside WhatsApp) that collects doctor registration data: name, specialty, CRM number. On submission, backend sends magic link via WhatsApp.

## Context

### Flow version and structure
- Use **WhatsApp Flows v7.3** with `data_api_version: "3.0"` (dynamic — has data endpoint)
- `routing_model` is **required** for dynamic flows
- **CRITICAL**: `"SUCCESS"` is a **reserved screen ID** in WhatsApp Flows — use `SUCCESS_SCREEN` instead
- Terminal screens require `"terminal": true` AND a Footer component with `complete` action

### Data endpoint encryption
- WhatsApp Flows with data endpoints use **AES-128-GCM + RSA-OAEP** encryption
- Run `node scripts/setup-encryption.js --flow-id <id>` to configure
- Must enable encryption in WABA settings BEFORE running setup-encryption
- Kapso handles key management — handler code receives decrypted request body

### Data endpoint handler rules
- Do NOT use `export` or `module.exports` in handler
- Do NOT include `endpoint_uri` or `data_channel_uri` in handler (Kapso injects these)
- INIT action: return empty form data for pre-fill
- data_exchange action: validate input, call MedSimples API, return SUCCESS_SCREEN
- Completion uses `screen: "SUCCESS_SCREEN"` (NOT "SUCCESS" — reserved)

### CRM validation in handler
- Brazilian CRM format: `CRM/SP 123456` (state abbreviation + number)
- Regex: `/^CRM\/[A-Z]{2}\s?\d{4,6}$/`
- On invalid: return same REGISTER screen with `crm_error` field populated

### Setup sequence
1. `node scripts/create-flow.js --phone-number-id <id> --name "Cadastro Médico"`
2. Enable encryption in WABA settings (Meta Business Manager)
3. `node scripts/setup-encryption.js --flow-id <id>`
4. Write flow JSON to `cognitest/whatsapp/flows/doctor-registration-flow.json`
5. `node scripts/update-flow-json.js --flow-id <id> --json-file ...`
6. Write handler to `cognitest/whatsapp/functions/doctor-registration-handler.js`
7. `node scripts/set-data-endpoint.js --flow-id <id> --code-file ...`
8. `node scripts/deploy-data-endpoint.js --flow-id <id>`
9. `node scripts/register-data-endpoint.js --flow-id <id>`
10. `node scripts/publish-flow.js --flow-id <id>`

### Template for triggering Flow (CTA button)
- Flow is triggered via interactive message with `action.name: "flow"` and `flow_id`
- The bot (bead .5) sends this CTA when doctor first messages the number

## Files
- `cognitest/whatsapp/flows/doctor-registration-flow.json` — Flow JSON
- `cognitest/whatsapp/functions/doctor-registration-handler.js` — data endpoint handler
- `cognitest/whatsapp/README.md` — document flow_id, phone_number_id, setup steps

## Testing
- [ ] `node scripts/send-test-flow.js` confirms all fields render correctly
- [ ] INIT action returns empty form data
- [ ] Invalid CRM format returns inline error on REGISTER screen (no screen change)
- [ ] Valid submission calls MedSimples magic-link endpoint with source='whatsapp'
- [ ] SUCCESS_SCREEN shown after valid submission
- [ ] `node scripts/list-function-logs.js` shows no errors on submit
- [ ] `node scripts/get-encryption-status.js` shows encryption active
- [ ] Flow is in PUBLISHED status (not DRAFT)

## Validation
- [ ] Screen ID `SUCCESS` is NOT used (reserved) — must use `SUCCESS_SCREEN` or another name
- [ ] Terminal screen has `"terminal": true` AND Footer with `complete` action
- [ ] Data endpoint handler does NOT use `export` or `module.exports`
- [ ] `endpoint_uri` and `data_channel_uri` NOT in handler code
- [ ] `"preview shows flow_token is missing"` error = dynamic flow without registered endpoint; fix by completing steps 7-9
title: Kapso: WhatsApp Flow for doctor registration

description:
## What
Configure a phone-number webhook on Kapso and a Kapso workflow that:
1. Listens for `whatsapp.message.received` on the MedSimples phone number
2. When a sender messages in, responds with a CTA Flow button message launching the registration Flow
3. Optionally detects if doctor is already registered to avoid re-triggering Flow

## Context

### Webhook scope (CRITICAL)
- Must be **phone-number scoped**, NOT project-level
- WhatsApp message events (`whatsapp.message.*`) are only available on phone-number webhooks
- Create with: `node scripts/create.js --phone-number-id <id> --url <https://...> --events whatsapp.message.received`
- Use `--payload-version v2` (recommended)
- Verify signature on every delivery (see references/webhooks-overview.md)

### Interactive CTA button to launch Flow
```json
{
  "type": "interactive",
  "interactive": {
    "type": "cta_url",
    "body": {"text": "Cadastre-se para enviar avaliações cognitivas aos seus pacientes."},
    "action": {
      "name": "flow",
      "parameters": {
        "flow_id": "<FLOW_ID>",
        "flow_cta": "Fazer cadastro",
        "mode": "published"
      }
    }
  }
}
```
Send via SDK: `client.messages.sendInteractive({phoneNumberId, to, ...above})`

### 24-hour session window constraint
- Interactive messages (including Flow CTA) require an active 24-hour conversation window
- If doctor hasn't messaged recently, send a template message first to open the window
- Pre-approve a UTILITY template like: 'Olá! Recebemos sua mensagem. Clique abaixo para se cadastrar.'
- Template must be approved by Meta before use

### Already-registered check
- Before sending Flow: call MedSimples `GET /api/auth/check-email?email=` if available
- Alternatively: store registered phone numbers in Kapso database (key-value store in workflow)
- If already registered: reply 'Você já possui um cadastro. Acesse: {login_url}'

### Workflow automation (automate-whatsapp skill)
- Use Kapso workflow for the bot logic — no custom webhook server needed
- Trigger: `whatsapp.message.received` event
- Workflow node: check if first-time sender → send Flow CTA (or template if outside window)
- Confirmation after flow_token received: plain text 'Link enviado! Verifique seu email.'

### Buffer configuration
- Enable buffering on the webhook to coalesce rapid messages: `--buffer-enabled true --buffer-window-seconds 5`
- This prevents duplicate Flow CTAs if doctor sends multiple messages quickly

## Files
- Webhook config documented in `cognitest/whatsapp/README.md` (phone_number_id, webhook_id)
- Workflow definition managed via Kapso dashboard / automate-whatsapp skill
- UTILITY template JSON: `cognitest/whatsapp/templates/registration-cta.json`

## Testing
- [ ] `node scripts/test.js --webhook-id <id>` confirms webhook delivery
- [ ] Sending a WhatsApp message to the number triggers Flow CTA within 5s
- [ ] `node scripts/list.js` shows webhook `active: true` and scope is phone-number
- [ ] Already-registered doctor gets different response (not Flow CTA)
- [ ] Multiple rapid messages → only one Flow CTA sent (buffering works)
- [ ] Signature verification working (reject tampered payloads)

## Validation
- [ ] Webhook is phone-number scoped (NOT project-level)
- [ ] `payload-version v2` set
- [ ] `--buffer-enabled true` to prevent duplicate triggers
- [ ] Template message approved by Meta before go-live
title: Kapso: WhatsApp webhook + bot trigger for registration flow

description:
## What
New React page at `/cadastro` in MedSimples frontend:
1. On load: reads `?token=` from URL, **immediately replaces URL state** to remove token from history
2. Renders confirmation page (two-phase: GET shows 'Confirmar cadastro' button, POST consumes token)
3. If valid: shows onboarding form (specialty, CRM, phone)
4. On submit: stores JWT, redirects to dashboard
5. If invalid/expired: shows error with 'Solicitar novo link' CTA

## Context

### Token URL exposure mitigation
- **Immediately** call `window.history.replaceState({}, '', '/cadastro')` on component mount
- Do this BEFORE the async verification call — prevents token persisting in browser history
- Use `useRef` guard to prevent React 18 StrictMode double-invocation consuming token twice:
```tsx
const verifyAttempted = useRef(false);
useEffect(() => {
  if (verifyAttempted.current) return;
  verifyAttempted.current = true;
  const token = new URLSearchParams(window.location.search).get('token');
  if (token) {
    window.history.replaceState({}, '', '/cadastro');
    verifyToken(token);
  }
}, []);
```

### Two-phase flow (mirrors backend design)
- GET: render confirmation page ('Clique para confirmar seu cadastro')
- User clicks confirm → POST to `/api/auth/magic-link/verify` with token in body
- This prevents Outlook SafeLinks and email security scanners consuming the token via GET prefetch

### CRM input validation
- Brazilian CRM format: `CRM/SP 123456` (two-letter state code + 4-6 digit number)
- Custom `parseCRM()` function for validation + formatting
- Show inline error: 'Formato inválido. Ex: CRM/SP 123456'

### Phone input
- Reuse existing `PhoneInput.tsx` component (already has libphonenumber-js + MUI integration)
- Phone is optional for registration

### Auth flow after verify
1. POST /api/auth/magic-link/verify → returns `{access_token, token_type}`
2. Store token: `localStorage.setItem('token', access_token)`
3. Call `AuthContext.fetchUserData()` to populate user context
4. PATCH /api/doctors/{id}/profile with specialty + CRM + phone
5. Redirect to `/dashboard`

### Error states
- `400 Invalid or expired token` → show 'Link inválido ou expirado' + 'Solicitar novo link' button
- Network error → show 'Erro de conexão. Tente novamente.' + retry button
- Use same error message for invalid and expired (no oracle — don't reveal which)

## Files
- `medsimples/frontend/src/pages/RegistrationPage.tsx` — new page
- `medsimples/frontend/src/App.tsx` (or router) — add `/cadastro` route (public, no auth required)

## Testing
- [ ] Token removed from URL on mount (before async call)
- [ ] React 18 StrictMode: token consumed exactly once (not twice)
- [ ] Confirmation button shown first (two-phase)
- [ ] Valid token → form shown after confirmation
- [ ] Invalid token → error state with resend CTA
- [ ] Expired token → same error message as invalid (no differentiation)
- [ ] Invalid CRM format → inline error before submit
- [ ] Successful submit → JWT in localStorage, redirected to /dashboard
- [ ] Back button during form does not re-consume token

## Validation
- [ ] Route accessible without auth (not behind PrivateRoute)
- [ ] `window.history.replaceState` called before first render with token in URL
- [ ] Uses MUI 6.5.0+ components consistent with rest of app
- [ ] Reuses existing PhoneInput.tsx component
- [ ] PATCH /api/doctors/{id}/profile called for profile fields (not inline in verify)
title: MedSimples frontend: /cadastro magic link registration page

description:
## What
Add a thin proxy endpoint to the CogniTest backend that forwards magic link registration requests to MedSimples, so the CogniTest frontend only ever calls its own backend.

`POST /api/v1/auth/magic-link` → forwards to `{MEDSIMPLES_API_URL}/api/auth/magic-link`

## Context

### httpx singleton (CRITICAL — fix existing anti-pattern)
The existing `medsimples_client.py` creates a new `httpx.AsyncClient` per call — this is a known resource leak and performance anti-pattern.

**Fix**: create singleton in FastAPI lifespan:
```python
# cognitest/backend/app/main.py
from contextlib import asynccontextmanager
import httpx

@asynccontextmanager
async def lifespan(app: FastAPI):
    app.state.http_client = httpx.AsyncClient(timeout=10.0)
    yield
    await app.state.http_client.aclose()

app = FastAPI(lifespan=lifespan)
```

Then update `MedSimplesClient` to accept the shared client:
```python
class MedSimplesClient:
    def __init__(self, http_client: httpx.AsyncClient):
        self.client = http_client
```

### Circuit breaker for upstream protection
Use `pybreaker` to protect against MedSimples being down:
```python
import pybreaker
medsimples_breaker = pybreaker.CircuitBreaker(fail_max=5, reset_timeout=60)

@medsimples_breaker
async def request_magic_link(self, payload: dict) -> dict:
    ...
```
When circuit is open: return 503 with 'Serviço temporariamente indisponível'

### Error sanitization (CRITICAL for security)
- Do NOT pass MedSimples error messages verbatim to the frontend (may leak internal details)
- Map status codes:
  - MedSimples 200 → 200 (pass through)
  - MedSimples 422 → 422 (validation error, safe to pass)
  - MedSimples 429 → 429 (rate limit, safe to pass)
  - MedSimples 4xx other → 400 with generic message
  - MedSimples 5xx → 502 with 'Serviço indisponível'
  - Network error → 502 with 'Serviço indisponível'

### Rate limiting
- Per-IP: 20 requests/hour (SlowAPI) — applied BEFORE forwarding
- This is a secondary defense; MedSimples has its own per-email limiting

### Proxy cache prevention
- Add response headers to prevent caching: `Cache-Control: no-store`
- Magic link endpoints must never be cached by CDN or proxy

## Files
- `cognitest/backend/app/routers/auth.py` — new file with proxy endpoint
- `cognitest/backend/app/main.py` — register auth router, add lifespan for httpx singleton
- `cognitest/backend/app/services/medsimples_client.py` — add `request_magic_link()` method, fix singleton pattern

## Testing
- [ ] Valid request → 200 forwarded from MedSimples
- [ ] Rate limit exceeded (21st request/hour from IP) → 429
- [ ] MedSimples unavailable (connection refused) → 502 with user-friendly message
- [ ] MedSimples 500 → 502 (not raw 500 message)
- [ ] No auth required on this endpoint (public)
- [ ] `Cache-Control: no-store` header present in response
- [ ] httpx client is shared (not recreated per request)

## Validation
- [ ] Does not expose MedSimples internal error messages to frontend
- [ ] Rate limit applied before forwarding to MedSimples
- [ ] `pybreaker` circuit breaker configured
- [ ] httpx singleton created in lifespan, not per-call
title: CogniTest backend: proxy /auth/magic-link to MedSimples

description:
## What
Add two registration entry points to the Meu Teste Médico landing page:

1. **Email CTA**: Form with name + email fields that POSTs to `POST /api/auth/magic-link` (via CogniTest backend proxy). Shows success state 'Link enviado para {email}!'
2. **WhatsApp CTA**: Button that opens `https://wa.me/+55{NUMBER}?text=Quero+me+cadastrar` in a new tab

Both CTAs appear in: hero section + 'Para Médicos' section.

## Context

### SvelteKit form pattern (no library needed)
```svelte
<script lang="ts">
  let name = '';
  let email = '';
  let state: 'idle' | 'loading' | 'success' | 'error' = 'idle';
  let errorMessage = '';

  async function handleSubmit(e: SubmitEvent) {
    e.preventDefault();
    if (!email.trim() || !name.trim()) return;
    state = 'loading';
    try {
      const res = await fetch('/api/auth/magic-link', {
        method: 'POST',
        headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },
        body: JSON.stringify({ name, email, source: 'landing_email' })
      });
      if (!res.ok) throw new Error(await res.text());
      state = 'success';
    } catch {
      state = 'error';
      errorMessage = 'Erro ao enviar link. Tente novamente.';
    }
  }
</script>
```

### Always show success (email enumeration prevention)
- Backend always returns 200 even if email already registered
- Frontend shows 'Link enviado!' regardless — never reveal if email exists
- Do NOT show 'Email already registered' message

### WhatsApp number env var
- Use `PUBLIC_WHATSAPP_NUMBER` env var (SvelteKit public env)
- Import: `import { env } from '$env/dynamic/public'` for runtime OR `import { PUBLIC_WHATSAPP_NUMBER } from '$env/static/public'` for build-time
- If missing: **hide the WhatsApp button entirely** (use `{#if}` guard) — do not show broken link
- wa.me format: `https://wa.me/55{NUMBER}?text=Quero+me+cadastrar` (no + before country code)

### Build configuration
- Add `PUBLIC_WHATSAPP_NUMBER` to `cognitest/frontend/.env.example`
- Add to `cognitest/frontend/build.sh` (inline env var before `bun run build`)
- Note: every `PUBLIC_*` var used in code MUST exist at build time or the build fails

### Form accessibility
- Associate labels with inputs via `for`/`id` attributes
- `<button type="submit" disabled={state === 'loading'}>`
- `aria-live="polite"` region for success/error messages

### Tailwind styling for states
- Loading: spinner + 'Enviando...' text, button opacity-50
- Success: green checkmark icon, hide form fields
- Error: red text below form

## Files
- `cognitest/frontend/src/routes/+page.svelte` — add CTAs to hero + médicos sections
- `cognitest/frontend/.env.example` — add `PUBLIC_WHATSAPP_NUMBER=`
- `cognitest/frontend/build.sh` (or equivalent) — add new PUBLIC_ var

## Testing
- [ ] Email form submits and shows success state ('Link enviado para {email}!')
- [ ] WhatsApp button opens wa.me in new tab with correct pre-filled text
- [ ] Empty name or email → form not submitted (HTML5 required validation)
- [ ] Backend returns error → inline error message shown
- [ ] Submit button disabled while loading
- [ ] PUBLIC_WHATSAPP_NUMBER missing → WhatsApp button hidden (not broken link)
- [ ] Both CTAs present in hero AND 'Para Médicos' sections
- [ ] `bun run build` succeeds with PUBLIC_WHATSAPP_NUMBER set

## Validation
- [ ] No direct MedSimples API calls from frontend (only /api/auth/magic-link on cognitest backend)
- [ ] PUBLIC_WHATSAPP_NUMBER added to build.sh before deploying
- [ ] Success message shown regardless of whether email was already registered
title: CogniTest landing page: registration CTAs (email form + WhatsApp button)

description:
## What
Redesign all 4 list pages with consistent pattern: page header, filter card, enhanced table, empty state.

## Plan Reference
plans/frontend-redesign.md - Phase 5

## Implementation

### Shared: Create table style constants
New file: medsimples/frontend/src/styles/tableStyles.ts
- cellLinkSx: textAlign 'left', border 'none', background 'none', cursor 'pointer', fontWeight 'bold', fontSize '0.875rem', textDecoration 'none', color 'inherit', '&:hover' textDecoration 'underline'
- ellipsisSx: wordBreak 'break-word', overflow 'hidden', textOverflow 'ellipsis', whiteSpace 'nowrap', maxWidth '100%'
- mobileDetailSx: display { xs: 'block', sm: 'none' }, fontSize '0.7rem', color 'text.secondary', mt 0.25, lineHeight 1.1
- actionBoxSx: display 'flex', gap 1, justifyContent 'center'

### Performance fixes (P0 - apply to ALL list pages):
1. Replace useEffect filtering with useMemo:
   BAD: useEffect(() => setFiltered(data.filter(...)), [filters])
   GOOD: const filtered = useMemo(() => data.filter(...), [data, ...filterDeps])
2. Hoist ALL inline sx from .map() loops to module-level constants (use shared tableStyles)
3. Every IconButton in action columns: add aria-label
4. Filter inputs: autoComplete='off', placeholder uses ellipsis char not '...'

### Status chip colors (medical conventions):
- Pago/Confirmado/Ativo = success (green) - never red for non-clinical items
- Pendente/Aguardando = warning (amber)
- Cancelado/Inativo = default (grey)
- Vencido = warning (amber, NOT error/red - red is for clinical emergencies only)

### 5a. PatientsList (medsimples/frontend/src/components/PatientsList.tsx)
- Page header: Box with Typography h4 'Pacientes' + Button 'Novo Paciente' (contained, primary)
- Filter bar: Card wrapping the filter fields with rounded inputs
- Table: uses theme overrides (header bg, hover rows) automatically
- Actions: cleaner icon buttons with aria-labels
- Remove alert() error handling -> use snackbar
- Remove unused useThemeState() if present
- KEEP: All fetch, filter, pagination logic

### 5b. VisitList (medsimples/frontend/src/components/VisitList.tsx)
- Same pattern. Page header + filter card + table.
- Remove unused _currentTheme = useThemeState() subscription
- KEEP: All filter/date picker/search logic

### 5c. InvoiceList (medsimples/frontend/src/components/InvoiceList.tsx)
- Same pattern. Status chips with medical-safe colors.
- Parallel initial fetch: Promise.all([fetchInsurers(), fetchInvoices()])
- Hoist STATUS_CONFIG to module level
- KEEP: All filter/pagination/download logic

### 5d. InsurerList (medsimples/frontend/src/components/InsurerList.tsx)
- Same pattern.
- KEEP: All search/pagination logic

## Validation
- bun run tsc --noEmit passes
- bun run lint passes
- No useEffect used for filtering (grep for setFiltered should return 0)
- No inline sx objects inside .map() callbacks
- All IconButtons have aria-label attributes
- Status chips use correct colors (no red for non-clinical)
- Filter bar wrapped in Card component
- Page headers show title + action button
- Tables have styled headers (grey bg, uppercase, letter-spacing)
- Shared tableStyles.ts file exists and is imported

## Dependencies
Depends on Phase 2d-h (layout shell complete)

description:
Implement monitoring and alerting for RLS policy violations in production to detect multi-tenancy bugs immediately.

MONITORING REQUIREMENTS:

1. Error Detection:
   - Capture psycopg2.errors.InsufficientPrivilege exceptions
   - Log RLS violation details (table, operation, user, org context)
   - Include stack trace and request context

2. Metrics & Dashboards:
   - Count of RLS violations per table/operation
   - RLS violation rate over time
   - Affected users and organizations
   - Dashboard showing RLS health

3. Alerting:
   - Slack/email alert on ANY RLS violation (should be zero)
   - Alert includes: table, user_id, org_id context, stack trace
   - Escalate if >5 violations in 5 minutes
   - Alert threshold: 0 (these should never happen)

4. Context Logging:
   - Log RLS context vars (app.org_id, app.org_role, app.is_superadmin)
   - Include request path, user session, telegram chat_id
   - Capture full SQL statement that failed

IMPLEMENTATION OPTIONS:

Option A: Application-level (Python):
- Add exception handler in backend/app/core/logging.py
- Catch InsufficientPrivilege in error middleware
- Send to structured logging (JSON)
- Use existing logging infrastructure

Option B: Database-level (PostgreSQL):
- Enable PostgreSQL logging for security events
- Configure log_statement = 'ddl' or 'all'
- Use pg_stat_statements for query tracking
- Ship logs to monitoring system

Recommendation: Both (defense in depth)

DELIVERABLES:
- RLS violation exception handler
- Structured logging with RLS context
- Grafana/monitoring dashboard
- Alert configuration (Slack/PagerDuty)
- Runbook for responding to RLS alerts
title: Add production monitoring for RLS policy violations
