description:
## What
Complete rewrite of medsimples/frontend/src/themes/index.ts using MUI 6 colorSchemes API with CSS variables.

## Plan Reference
plans/frontend-redesign.md - Phase 1, section 1c

## Implementation

File: medsimples/frontend/src/themes/index.ts (complete rewrite)

### TypeScript Module Augmentation
Add at top of file (or create theme.d.ts):
declare module '@mui/material/styles' {
  interface Theme { sidebar: { width: number; collapsedWidth: number }; layout: { headerHeight: number } }
  interface ThemeOptions { sidebar?: { width?: number; collapsedWidth?: number }; layout?: { headerHeight?: number } }
}

### Theme Structure
Use MUI 6 createTheme with:
- cssVariables: { colorSchemeSelector: 'data' }
- defaultColorScheme: 'light'
- colorSchemes.light.palette:
  - primary.main: '#0F766E' (teal-700, passes WCAG AA 5.47:1)
  - primary.light: '#14B8A6', primary.dark: '#0D5C56'
  - secondary.main: '#F59E0B' (amber - NOTE: fails WCAG AA for white text, use dark text on amber buttons)
  - success.main: '#16A34A', warning.main: '#EAB308', error.main: '#DC2626', info.main: '#0EA5E9'
  - background.default: '#F8FAFC', background.paper: '#FFFFFF'
  - text.primary: '#1E293B' (slate-800), text.secondary: '#64748B' (slate-500)
  - divider: '#E2E8F0' (slate-200)
- colorSchemes.dark.palette:
  - primary.main: '#2DD4BF' (teal-400, lighter for dark bg)
  - background.default: '#0F172A', background.paper: '#1E293B'
  - text.primary: '#F1F5F9', text.secondary: '#94A3B8'

### Typography
- Default fontFamily: '"DM Sans Variable", "DM Sans", -apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, sans-serif'
- Heading fontFamily (h1-h4): '"DM Serif Display", Georgia, "Times New Roman", serif'
- h1: 2.25rem weight 400, h2: 1.875rem/400, h3: 1.5rem/400, h4: 1.25rem/400 (serif is naturally heavy)
- h5: 1.125rem/600 (sans), h6: 1rem/600 (sans)
- body1: 0.9375rem/400/lineHeight 1.6, body2: 0.875rem/400/1.6
- button: fontWeight 600, textTransform 'none'

### Shape
borderRadius: 12

### Component Overrides (MUI 6 API)
- MuiButton: defaultProps disableElevation. styleOverrides root: textTransform 'none', borderRadius 8, fontWeight 600, padding '8px 20px'. containedPrimary hover: boxShadow '0 4px 12px rgba(15,118,110,0.3)'. Secondary variant: color '#1E293B' (dark text for amber accessibility).
- MuiOutlinedInput: borderRadius 8, notchedOutline borderColor '#CBD5E1' (slate-300), hover border '#0F766E', focused border '#0F766E' width 2
- MuiCard: defaultProps elevation 0. root: borderRadius 16, border '1px solid #E2E8F0', boxShadow '0 1px 3px rgba(0,0,0,0.04), 0 1px 2px rgba(0,0,0,0.06)'
- MuiPaper: root borderRadius 12
- MuiTableHead: root .MuiTableCell-head: backgroundColor '#F1F5F9', fontWeight 600, fontSize '0.8125rem', color '#475569', textTransform 'uppercase', letterSpacing '0.05em'
- MuiTableRow: root lastChild td borderBottom 0, hover backgroundColor '#F8FAFC'
- MuiTableCell: root padding '12px 16px', borderColor '#F1F5F9', fontVariantNumeric 'tabular-nums'
- MuiChip: root borderRadius 8, fontWeight 500
- MuiTextField: defaultProps variant 'outlined', size 'small'
- MuiCssBaseline: styleOverrides body scrollbarWidth 'thin'. ':root': '--sidebar-width': '260px', '--sidebar-collapsed-width': '72px', '--header-height': '64px'

### Custom Properties
sidebar: { width: 260, collapsedWidth: 72 }
layout: { headerHeight: 64 }

### Export
Export default: single theme object (NOT a map of themes). MUI 6 colorSchemes handles light/dark internally.

## Validation
- bun run tsc --noEmit passes (module augmentation works)
- bun run lint passes
- Theme exports a single Theme object, not an object with keys
- All color values are correct hex
- Component overrides use correct MUI 6 API structure
- No references to old theme variants (secondary, success themes)

## Dependencies
Depends on Phase 1a-b (fonts installed)
title: Phase 1c: Rewrite MUI theme with colorSchemes API

description:
## Issue
PatientDetail uses hasFetchedRef to prevent double-fetch in StrictMode, but this does NOT prevent stale data when navigating between patients. When React Router navigates from /app/pacientes/5 to /app/pacientes/10 without unmounting, the old fetch can complete and set wrong patient data.

## Severity
P1 CRITICAL - In a medical app, showing wrong patient data is a patient safety concern.

## Location
medsimples/frontend/src/components/PatientDetail.tsx:291-341

## Why This Matters
Doctors may see patient 5's data under patient 10's heading for a flash. In a medical context, data integrity is paramount.

## Fix
Replace hasFetchedRef with AbortController scoped to each effect invocation. Pass signal to axios. Check aborted before setState. This also handles StrictMode naturally.

## Validation Criteria
- [ ] Rapidly clicking between two patients never shows stale data
- [ ] StrictMode double-mount does not cause double API calls
- [ ] Breadcrumb context receives correct patient data

## Testing Steps
1. Open patient 5, then immediately click patient 10 before data loads
2. Verify patient 10 data appears (never patient 5)
3. Run in StrictMode and verify no console errors
title: PatientDetail: stale fetch race when navigating between patients

description: When CogniTest refunds a payment, the linked MedSimples invoice is never updated. Both process_refund() and handle_charge_refunded() call _mark_medsimples_invoice_refunded() which currently only logs a skip message. MedSimples needs a DELETE or PATCH endpoint to void/delete the invoice_payment record created by record_payment(). Until then, a refunded CogniTest session shows the MedSimples invoice as paid — financial inconsistency. See refund_service.py:156 and :246. The stub method also needs to be made async and receive org_id (required by MedSimplesClient for RLS scoping).
title: Implement MedSimples invoice refund sync

description: Pages like visits and invoices load slowly from Brazilian IPs due to network latency. API responses are fast (92-250ms) but no compression, no caching headers, and short frontend cache (2s) make the experience sluggish. Target: 3-5s page loads down to ~500-800ms.
title: Improve performance for high-latency connections (Brazil)

description:
## What

Add GZIPMiddleware to FastAPI to compress HTTP responses. No compression currently exists anywhere in the stack.

## Context

- Server responses are fast (92-250ms) but transfer uncompressed over high-latency connections (Brazil)
- JSON API payloads for visits/invoices can reach 50-200KB uncompressed
- GZip achieves 70-85% compression on structured JSON (repeated keys, string values)
- Expected: 100KB response -> 15-30KB compressed

### Research Insights

**Middleware Placement (CRITICAL):**
- Must be the **outermost** middleware (first `add_middleware` call, before RequestContextMiddleware)
- In Starlette, middleware added first wraps all subsequent middleware
- This ensures compression happens AFTER all other middleware has processed the response
- LoggingMiddleware does not log response sizes, so placement won't affect logging

**Configuration:**
- Use `minimum_size=500` (not 1000) — many single-record API responses (patient detail, visit detail) fall in 500-1000 byte range and still benefit
- Use default `compresslevel=9` (maximum compression) — CPU overhead is negligible for this app's scale
- FastAPI's GZIPMiddleware automatically skips binary content types (images, PDFs, zip files)
- Middleware automatically adds `Vary: Accept-Encoding` header (required for correct caching)

**BREACH Attack Consideration:**
- GZip on authenticated responses with user-controlled input (search params) can theoretically enable BREACH attacks
- Mitigated by: existing CSRF protection, rate limiting on search endpoints, HTTPS
- Risk is LOW for this app — BREACH requires attacker to inject content AND observe response sizes
- Accept the risk; monitor if threat model changes

**Skip Brotli for now:**
- GZip is sufficient for dynamic JSON APIs
- Brotli adds complexity with marginal benefit (15-25% smaller than GZip but slower compression)
- Reconsider only if adding CDN with pre-compression at build time

**No SSE/streaming concerns:**
- App does not use Server-Sent Events or streaming responses for data endpoints
- PDF downloads are already binary (not compressed by GZip middleware)

## Implementation

File: `medsimples/backend/app/main.py`

Add as the FIRST middleware (before RequestContextMiddleware at line 86):
```python
from starlette.middleware.gzip import GZipMiddleware
app.add_middleware(GZipMiddleware, minimum_size=500)
```

Note: Import from `starlette.middleware.gzip` (FastAPI re-exports it but starlette is the source).

## Testing

- [ ] Verify `Content-Encoding: gzip` header on JSON API responses > 500 bytes
- [ ] Verify `Vary: Accept-Encoding` header present
- [ ] Verify binary responses (PDF downloads) are NOT compressed
- [ ] Verify response body is correctly decompressed by browser
- [ ] Compare response sizes before/after with curl: `curl -H "Accept-Encoding: gzip" -sD - URL | head -20`
- [ ] Check no interaction issues with existing CORS, security headers, or rate limiter

## Validation

- JSON API responses should show 60-80% size reduction
- No increase in error rates after deployment
- CPU usage should not noticeably increase
title: Add GZip compression middleware to FastAPI

description:
## Issue
_REFERENCE_DATA_PREFIXES uses paths without trailing slashes ('/api/doctors', '/api/insurers', '/api/procedures'). The startswith() check could match unintended routes like '/api/doctors_backup' if one were added.

## Severity
P3 NICE-TO-HAVE - No false matches exist today, but defensive coding prevents future issues.

## Location
medsimples/backend/app/main.py:130

## Fix
Either add trailing slashes or check for exact match or path+/:
```python
_REFERENCE_DATA_PREFIXES = ('/api/doctors/', '/api/insurers/', '/api/procedures/')
# Also match exact path without trailing slash
elif request.method == 'GET' and any(
    path == p.rstrip('/') or path.startswith(p) for p in _REFERENCE_DATA_PREFIXES
):
```

## Validation Criteria
- [ ] /api/doctors and /api/doctors/ still get reference cache headers
- [ ] /api/doctors/123 still gets reference cache headers
- [ ] Hypothetical /api/doctors_backup would NOT get reference cache headers
title: Add trailing slash to _REFERENCE_DATA_PREFIXES to prevent false matches

description:
## Issue
The add_security_headers middleware calls urlparse() on every request to build the CSP header with CogniTest origin. This is a constant value that should be computed once at startup.

## Severity
P3 NICE-TO-HAVE - Minor performance improvement, reduces per-request overhead.

## Location
medsimples/backend/app/main.py:146-151

## Fix
Move to module level:
```python
# Module level, after settings import
_cognitest_csp_origin = ''
if settings.cognitest_backend_url:
    _parsed = urlparse(settings.cognitest_backend_url)
    _cognitest_csp_origin = f' {_parsed.scheme}://{_parsed.netloc}'

# In middleware, just use _cognitest_csp_origin directly
```

## Validation Criteria
- [ ] CSP header still includes CogniTest origin when configured
- [ ] No urlparse import inside the middleware function
- [ ] urlparse called only once at startup
title: Hoist urlparse() for CogniTest CSP to module level

close_reason: Documented as known limitation - edge case masked by page navigation
description:
## Issue
If a GET request is in-flight when a mutation (POST/PUT/DELETE) completes and invalidates the cache, the GET's response is already stale but will be used by the caller. A generation counter could detect this.

## Severity
P3 NICE-TO-HAVE - Edge case that only manifests under specific timing. Current UX mostly masks this since pages re-fetch on navigation.

## Location
medsimples/frontend/src/utils/apiCache.ts

## Why This Matters
Rare timing issue where stale data briefly appears after a mutation. Usually resolved on next navigation.

## Possible Fix
Add a generation counter that increments on invalidation. GET callers check the counter after response returns and discard if it changed. This is complex and may not be worth implementing unless users report stale data issues.

## Validation Criteria
- [ ] Document as known limitation if not implementing
- [ ] If implementing: verify stale responses are discarded after mutations
title: In-flight GET during mutation may return stale data

description:
## Issue
Some frontend files use raw axios.get/post/put/delete instead of the cachedAxios* wrappers from apiCache.ts. This means those calls bypass cache invalidation logic and CSRF token injection.

## Severity
P3 NICE-TO-HAVE - Inconsistency that reduces the effectiveness of the caching layer and may miss CSRF tokens on some mutations.

## Location
Various frontend files - needs audit

## Why This Matters
- Raw axios.post calls skip CSRF token injection from addCsrfToHeaders
- Raw axios.get calls don't benefit from caching
- Raw mutations don't trigger cache invalidation

## Fix
Audit all frontend files for direct axios usage and migrate to cachedAxios* wrappers where appropriate. Some calls (like auth token requests) may intentionally bypass caching.

## Validation Criteria
- [ ] Audit complete - all direct axios calls documented
- [ ] Intentional bypasses annotated with comments
- [ ] Unintentional bypasses migrated to cached wrappers
title: Audit and migrate raw axios calls to cachedAxios wrappers

description:
## What

Add Cache-Control headers to HTTP responses based on route type. Currently no caching headers exist anywhere — every page load re-fetches all resources from scratch.

## Context

- Vite builds produce content-hashed filenames (e.g., `index-abc123.js`) in `/assets/` — safe for aggressive caching
- API endpoints are JWT-authenticated and org-scoped via RLS
- Multi-tenant medical app under LGPD — data isolation is critical
- Users in Brazil experience high latency (~150ms RTT per round-trip)

### Research Insights

**SECURITY (CRITICAL): Never use `public` on authenticated API endpoints**
- Original plan had `Cache-Control: public, max-age=300` for doctors/insurers — this is UNSAFE
- `public` allows shared caches (CDN, proxy, ISP transparent caches) to store and serve responses
- Risk: User A (Org 123) fetches /api/doctors, shared cache stores response, User B (Org 456) gets Org 123's doctors
- Brazilian ISPs sometimes use transparent proxy caches, amplifying this risk
- LGPD Art. 46 violation — unauthorized disclosure of personal data
- **FIX: Use `private` for ALL org-scoped API endpoints** (browser cache only, no shared caches)

**Static Assets (`/assets/*`):**
- `Cache-Control: public, max-age=31536000, immutable` — safe because content-hashed
- `immutable` supported in Firefox, Edge, Safari; Chrome ignores it but has equivalent behavior
- Eliminates 800-1500ms of JS/CSS re-download on return visits

**SPA Entry Point (`index.html`, `/`):**
- `Cache-Control: no-store, no-cache, must-revalidate` — MUST NOT be cached
- If cached, users won't receive code updates when content-hashed asset filenames change

**API Reference Data (doctors, insurers, procedures):**
- `Cache-Control: private, max-age=300, stale-while-revalidate=600`
- `private` prevents shared cache storage (multi-tenant safety)
- `stale-while-revalidate=600` lets browser serve stale data instantly while revalidating in background — hides latency
- `Vary: Authorization` as defense-in-depth (ensures browser cache separates by auth header)

**API Transactional Data (visits, invoices, patients):**
- `Cache-Control: private, no-cache, must-revalidate`
- Forces browser to revalidate before serving — ensures freshness for multi-user editing
- Combined with ETag support (future enhancement) this saves bandwidth via 304 responses

**Unauthenticated Endpoints (health, csrf-token):**
- `Cache-Control: public, max-age=60` — safe, no sensitive data

## Implementation

File: `medsimples/backend/app/main.py`

Extend the existing `add_security_headers` middleware (line 129) to add Cache-Control headers based on path:

```python
# Static assets (Vite content-hashed)
if request.url.path.startswith("/assets/"):
    response.headers["Cache-Control"] = "public, max-age=31536000, immutable"

# SPA entry point — never cache
elif request.url.path == "/" or (
    request.method == "GET" and not request.url.path.startswith("/api/")
    and "." not in request.url.path.split("/")[-1]
):
    response.headers["Cache-Control"] = "no-store, no-cache, must-revalidate"

# Unauthenticated endpoints
elif request.url.path in ("/api/health", "/api/csrf-token"):
    response.headers["Cache-Control"] = "public, max-age=60"

# Reference data endpoints (stable, org-scoped)
elif request.method == "GET" and any(
    request.url.path.startswith(p) for p in ["/api/doctors", "/api/insurers", "/api/procedures"]
):
    response.headers["Cache-Control"] = "private, max-age=300, stale-while-revalidate=600"
    response.headers["Vary"] = "Authorization"

# All other API GET endpoints (transactional data)
elif request.method == "GET" and request.url.path.startswith("/api/"):
    response.headers["Cache-Control"] = "private, no-cache, must-revalidate"
    response.headers["Vary"] = "Authorization"
```

## Testing

- [ ] Verify `/assets/*` responses have `Cache-Control: public, max-age=31536000, immutable`
- [ ] Verify SPA routes (/, /patients, /visits) serve index.html with `no-store`
- [ ] Verify `/api/doctors` has `Cache-Control: private, max-age=300, stale-while-revalidate=600`
- [ ] Verify `/api/visits` has `Cache-Control: private, no-cache, must-revalidate`
- [ ] Verify ALL API responses have `Vary: Authorization` (except unauthenticated)
- [ ] Verify NO API endpoint returns `Cache-Control: public` (except health/csrf)
- [ ] Test multi-tenant isolation: two different org users should NOT see each other's cached data
- [ ] Verify browser DevTools Network tab shows `(disk cache)` for `/assets/*` on reload

## Validation

- Return visits should skip JS/CSS download entirely (served from disk cache)
- Reference data endpoints should show `stale-while-revalidate` behavior in Network tab
- No cross-tenant data leakage in cache
title: Add Cache-Control headers for static assets and API responses

description:
## What

Enhance the frontend apiCache to use endpoint-aware cache durations and add mutation-based cache invalidation. Current 2s TTL is effectively useless — only deduplicates within the same render cycle.

## Context

- Current implementation: in-memory Map with 2s TTL, request deduplication
- File: `medsimples/frontend/src/utils/apiCache.ts`
- Users navigate between pages (visits list -> visit detail -> back) and re-fetch everything
- Medical records app with multi-user editing — stale data is a concern for transactional data
- No SWR or React Query used; custom cache is lightweight and sufficient for now

### Research Insights

**Endpoint-Aware TTLs (not flat 30s):**
- Reference data (doctors, insurers, procedures): 5 minutes — changes rarely
- Transactional data (visits, invoices): 10-15 seconds — multi-user editing risk
- Patient data: 30 seconds — moderate update frequency
- A flat 30s for invoices/visits risks showing stale data in multi-user scenarios

**Cache Invalidation on Mutations (CRITICAL for correctness):**
- Current `cachedAxiosPost/Put/Delete` do NOT clear GET cache
- After creating a visit, the visits list cache still holds old data for remaining TTL
- Must invalidate related cache entries after POST/PUT/DELETE
- Pattern: strip trailing ID from URL, clear all matching cache entries

**Don't Cache Failed Requests:**
- Current impl caches the Promise including rejections
- A 500 error gets cached and returned to subsequent callers within TTL
- Fix: remove cache entry on promise rejection, keep only on success

**Cache Key Reliability:**
- `JSON.stringify(config)` is unreliable — object property order not guaranteed
- Use only URL + sorted query params for cache key
- Ignore non-cacheable config properties (headers, transformers)

**Memory Leak Prevention:**
- With longer TTLs and many unique URLs, Map could grow unbounded
- Add max cache size (200 entries) with LRU-style eviction
- Current cleanup via setTimeout is adequate but add a size guard

**Stale-While-Revalidate NOT recommended for frontend:**
- Medical/financial data accuracy requirements make SWR risky
- Simple TTL with mutation invalidation is safer and simpler
- Backend Cache-Control `stale-while-revalidate` handles this at HTTP level

**Long-term: Consider TanStack Query migration**
- Production-ready cache management, mutation support, optimistic updates
- Not needed now — custom cache with these improvements is sufficient

## Implementation

File: `medsimples/frontend/src/utils/apiCache.ts`

Key changes:
1. Endpoint-aware TTL lookup table
2. Better cache key generation (sorted params)
3. Cache invalidation helper called from mutation functions
4. Don't cache rejected promises
5. Max cache size guard (200 entries)
6. Clear cache on logout (call clearApiCache from AuthContext)

```typescript
const MAX_CACHE_SIZE = 200;

const CACHE_DURATIONS: Record<string, number> = {
  '/api/doctors': 300_000,
  '/api/insurers': 300_000,
  '/api/procedures': 300_000,
  '/api/templates': 120_000,
  '/api/patients': 30_000,
  '/api/visits': 15_000,
  '/api/invoices': 15_000,
};
const DEFAULT_CACHE_DURATION = 30_000;

function getCacheDuration(url: string): number {
  for (const [prefix, duration] of Object.entries(CACHE_DURATIONS)) {
    if (url.startsWith(prefix)) return duration;
  }
  return DEFAULT_CACHE_DURATION;
}

function invalidateCachePrefix(url: string) {
  const basePath = url.replace(/\/\d+$/, '');
  for (const key of requestCache.keys()) {
    if (key.startsWith(basePath)) {
      requestCache.delete(key);
    }
  }
}
```

Mutation functions (`cachedAxiosPost/Put/Delete`) should call `invalidateCachePrefix(url)` after successful response.

Also call `clearApiCache()` on logout in AuthContext.

## Testing

- [ ] Verify reference data endpoints use 5-minute cache (doctors, insurers, procedures)
- [ ] Verify transactional endpoints use 15s cache (visits, invoices)
- [ ] Verify cache is invalidated after creating/updating/deleting a record
- [ ] Verify failed requests are NOT cached (mock a 500, verify retry works)
- [ ] Verify cache does not grow beyond 200 entries
- [ ] Verify logout clears all cached data
- [ ] Verify navigating back to a list page shows cached data (no loading spinner)
- [ ] Verify creating a new visit then navigating to visits list shows the new visit

## Validation

- Page navigation should feel instant for cached endpoints
- No stale data visible after mutations
- Memory usage should not grow unbounded during long sessions
title: Extend frontend apiCache duration from 2s to 30s

description:
## Issue
POST, PUT, DELETE, and PATCH responses fall through the Cache-Control elif chain in add_security_headers middleware (main.py:133) without any explicit header. Browsers may cache mutation responses.

## Severity
P2 IMPORTANT - Mutation responses without explicit no-store could be cached by browsers, leading to confusing replay behavior.

## Location
medsimples/backend/app/main.py:133-196 (add_security_headers middleware)

## Why This Matters
Without explicit no-store on non-GET responses, aggressive browser caches or proxies could cache POST/PUT/DELETE responses, causing stale or replayed data.

## Fix
Add a final else clause to the cache-control chain:
```python
# Non-GET requests (mutations) - never cache
else:
    response.headers['Cache-Control'] = 'no-store'
```

## Validation Criteria
- [ ] All POST/PUT/DELETE responses include Cache-Control: no-store
- [ ] Existing GET cache-control logic unchanged
title: Add no-store Cache-Control to POST/PUT/DELETE responses

title: Fix race condition in apiCache.ts catch handler

description:
## Issue
buildCacheKey uses template literal interpolation for param values, which produces '[object Object]' for nested objects or arrays. Different nested params would produce the same cache key.

## Severity
P2 IMPORTANT - Could serve wrong cached data when different nested query params collide to the same cache key.

## Location
medsimples/frontend/src/utils/apiCache.ts:30-43

## Why This Matters
If any API call passes nested objects as params (e.g., filters), different filter combinations could share a cache entry.

## Fix
Use JSON.stringify for non-primitive values:
```typescript
const sorted = Object.keys(params)
  .sort()
  .map((k) => {
    const v = params[k];
    return \`\${k}=\${typeof v === 'object' ? JSON.stringify(v) : v}\`;
  })
  .join('&');
```

## Validation Criteria
- [ ] Nested object params produce distinct cache keys
- [ ] Primitive params still work correctly
title: Fix buildCacheKey for nested object params

title: Fix invalidateCachePrefix to handle sub-resource paths

description:
## Issue
The cache-control middleware checks for path '/api/csrf-token' but the actual CSRF endpoint is '/api/csrf/token'. The intended short public cache never applies.

## Severity
P2 IMPORTANT - CSRF token endpoint gets the wrong cache-control header (private, no-cache instead of public, max-age=60).

## Location
medsimples/backend/app/main.py:179

## Why This Matters
Minor performance impact - CSRF tokens are re-fetched more often than necessary. More importantly, this indicates a maintenance risk if other paths are similarly mismatched.

## Fix
Change the path check:
```python
elif path in ('/api/health', '/api/csrf/token'):
```

## Validation Criteria
- [ ] GET /api/csrf/token returns Cache-Control: public, max-age=60
- [ ] /api/health cache behavior unchanged
title: Fix CSRF token path mismatch in cache rules

Replace the overkill PDF template editor with a lean system. Two changes: (1) archive the pdf_templates code to a git branch, (2) add logo_path to Organization and Doctor so all code-generated PDFs resolve branding via chain: doctor.logo_path > organization.logo_path > static/logo.png.

description:
## What

Delete the pdf_templates feature code from main branch. Tag the current commit first as the archive point. DB tables remain (no migration needed).

## Context

Files to remove:
- `backend/app/routers/pdf_templates.py`
- `backend/app/services/pdf_template_service.py`, `pdf_generation_service.py`, `field_resolver_service.py`, `anchor_resolver_service.py`, `pdf_template_storage_service.py`
- `backend/app/repositories/pdf_template_repository.py`
- `backend/app/schemas/pdf_template.py`
- Remove from `models.py`: PDFTemplate, PDFTemplateField, PDFTemplateVersion classes and their imports
- Remove from `main.py`: import and include_router for pdf_templates_router (lines 34, 314-315)

**Archive strategy:** Use a git tag, not a branch. The commit history is the archive.
```bash
git tag archive/pdf-templates        # tag current state before deletion
git push origin archive/pdf-templates
# then delete files, commit, push
```

**Before deleting, audit cross-dependencies:**
```bash
grep -rn 'pdf_template\|field_resolver\|anchor_resolver\|PDFTemplate' backend/app/ --include='*.py' | grep -v 'routers/pdf_templates\|services/pdf_template\|services/field_resolver\|services/anchor_resolver\|services/pdf_generation\|repositories/pdf_template\|schemas/pdf_template\|alembic/versions'
```
If anything outside the listed files imports from pdf_templates code, fix those imports first.

The 3 alembic migrations (36504d47802d, 72ab2a2beb89, d29f798a16a9) stay in alembic/versions/ — they are part of the migration chain.

## Testing

- [ ] `uv run ruff check .` passes with no errors after removal

- [ ] Verify clean: `grep -r 'pdf_template' backend/app/ --include='*.py'` returns nothing except alembic migration files

## Validation

description:
## Issue
`OrganizationUpdate` (schemas/organization.py:88) and `DoctorUpdate` (schemas/doctor.py:54) both expose `logo_path: str | None = None`. The update service methods iterate all non-unset fields and call setattr. This allows any authenticated user to write an arbitrary path string to the DB via PUT, bypassing the upload validation pipeline and Pillow re-encoding. Combined with the GET logo serve endpoint, a doctor can point their own logo_path at `logos/org_3/abc.png` (another org's file) and serve it.

## Severity
P1 CRITICAL — Security: within-storage path injection via update endpoint. logo_path must be server-controlled only.

## Location
- `medsimples/backend/app/schemas/organization.py:88`
- `medsimples/backend/app/schemas/doctor.py:54`

## Fix
Remove `logo_path` from both `OrganizationUpdate` and `DoctorUpdate`. Logo management is exclusively through the dedicated upload/delete endpoints.

## Validation Criteria
- [ ] `logo_path` absent from both update schemas
- [ ] PUT /organizations/{id} and PUT /doctors/{id} no longer accept logo_path
- [ ] Upload endpoint still sets logo_path correctly
- [ ] `uv run ruff check .` passes
title: Security: Remove logo_path from OrganizationUpdate and DoctorUpdate schemas

description:
## Issue
In both `organization_service.py` and `doctor_service.py`, `dest.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)` is called BEFORE the `Path.resolve().relative_to(base)` traversal check. A maliciously crafted `storage_path_logos` setting could create directories outside the storage root before the guard fires.

## Severity
P1 CRITICAL — Security: filesystem mutation occurs before containment check.

## Location
- `medsimples/backend/app/services/organization_service.py:395-411`
- `medsimples/backend/app/services/doctor_service.py:73-81`

## Fix
Move the path traversal guard (resolve + relative_to check) to run BEFORE `dest.parent.mkdir()`. Order: compute dest → validate containment → mkdir → write.

## Validation Criteria
- [ ] Traversal check runs before any filesystem mutation in both services
- [ ] Normal upload still works (200, file written)
- [ ] `uv run ruff check .` passes
title: Fix: mkdir runs before path traversal guard in upload_logo

description:
## Issue
`GET /organizations/{id}/logo` (organizations.py:258-271) only requires authentication but has no org-isolation authorization check. Any authenticated user from any organization can retrieve any other organization's logo by supplying an arbitrary organization_id. The POST and DELETE endpoints on the same resource have correct same-org/superadmin guards; GET is missing them.

## Severity
P1 CRITICAL — Cross-tenant data exposure in a multi-tenant medical system.

## Location
`medsimples/backend/app/routers/organizations.py:258-271`

## Fix
Add the same authorization guard as POST (lines 222-230): require `current_user.is_super_admin OR current_user.organization_id == organization_id`. Return 403 for unauthorized access.

## Validation Criteria
- [ ] User from org A cannot GET org B's logo (403)
- [ ] Superadmin can GET any org's logo
- [ ] Same-org user can GET their own org's logo
- [ ] `uv run ruff check .` passes
title: Security: Add auth check to GET /organizations/{id}/logo endpoint

description:
## Issue
`OrganizationService._to_response` (organization_service.py:38-62) constructs `OrganizationResponse` but never passes `logo_path=org.logo_path`. The field was added to the schema in this epic but is never wired up. logo_path is always None in org API responses even after a successful logo upload.

## Severity
P1 CRITICAL — Functional regression: the feature doesn't work (uploaded logo_path never returned to frontend).

## Location
`medsimples/backend/app/services/organization_service.py:38-62`

## Fix
This is best addressed together with bead .11 (OrganizationResponse should have logo_url not logo_path). Either: (a) add `logo_path=org.logo_path` to the _to_response constructor as a quick fix, or (b) replace logo_path with a computed logo_url field in OrganizationResponse and populate it here.

## Validation Criteria
- [ ] After uploading an org logo, GET /organizations/{id} response includes the logo reference
- [ ] `uv run ruff check .` passes
title: Fix: OrganizationService._to_response never populates logo_path (always None)

description:
## Issue
LogoUpload.tsx uses `uploading` boolean state as the only guard against concurrent uploads. React state updates are async and batch between renders. The `<Button disabled={uploading}>` does not reliably block the hidden `<input type=file>` from firing again via keyboard on some browsers. A user can open the file picker while an upload is in-flight, starting two overlapping async upload coroutines that share state.

## Severity
P1 CRITICAL — Race condition: second upload revokes the objectUrl from the first while it's still rendering, corrupts uploading state, leaves preview in broken state.

## Location
`medsimples/frontend/src/components/LogoUpload.tsx:89, 205, 217`

## Fix
Add a `uploadingRef = useRef(false)` guard that is set synchronously at the start of `handleFileChange` and cleared in `finally`. Check `if (uploadingRef.current) return;` before proceeding. Also add `tabIndex={0}` to VisuallyHiddenInput so it is the sole keyboard target.

## Validation Criteria
- [ ] Second file selection while upload in-flight is rejected
- [ ] No double objectUrl revocation
- [ ] `bun run tsc --noEmit` passes
title: Fix: concurrent upload race — uploadingRef guard missing in LogoUpload.tsx

description:

OrganizationDetail.tsx:82-107 calls fetchOrganization inside useEffect but omits it from the dependency array. This is an exhaustive-deps violation and creates a stale closure risk — if fetchOrganization ever reads state, it will use stale values.

description:
## Issue
organization_service.py:381 and doctor_service.py:60 both set Image.MAX_IMAGE_PIXELS = 25_000_000 inside the upload method body. This mutates global Pillow state on every upload call. In a concurrent app, any code path that opens an image without first setting the limit sees whatever value was last written. Move to module level.

## Location
- medsimples/backend/app/services/organization_service.py:381
- medsimples/backend/app/services/doctor_service.py:60

## Fix
Add Image.MAX_IMAGE_PIXELS = 25_000_000 at module level (top of file, after imports) in both service files. Remove from the method body.

## Validation Criteria
- [ ] Only one assignment per file at module level
- [ ] Upload still works correctly
title: Fix: Image.MAX_IMAGE_PIXELS mutated per-request (global state)

description:
## Issue
DoctorResponse.populate_logo_url (schemas/doctor.py:23-29) hardcodes the string '/api/doctors/{id}/logo' inside a Pydantic model_validator. Schemas must not know about API routing paths — changing the prefix silently breaks the field with no lint/test catching it. Also uses object.__setattr__ to bypass frozen model protections.

DoctorInfo (line 133) and DoctorProfileResponse (line 201) expose raw logo_path (filesystem-relative storage path) instead of a URL.

OrganizationResponse has no logo_url field at all (logo_path is declared but never populated by _to_response — see bead .13).

## Location
- medsimples/backend/app/schemas/doctor.py:23-29, 133, 201
- medsimples/backend/app/schemas/organization.py:130

## Fix
- Remove populate_logo_url model_validator from DoctorResponse
- Add logo_url: str | None = None to DoctorResponse, DoctorInfo, DoctorProfileResponse, OrganizationResponse (all nullable)
- Populate logo_url in service _to_response() methods: '/api/doctors/{id}/logo' if logo_path else None
- Remove logo_path from all response schemas (it is a server-side-only field)

## Validation Criteria
- [ ] No API route strings in any schema file
- [ ] logo_url populated correctly in all four response schemas
- [ ] logo_path absent from all API responses
title: Fix: logo_url model_validator hardcodes API route in Pydantic schema

description:
## Issue
InvoiceService._register_pdf_fonts() (invoice_service.py:46) calls pdfmetrics.registerFont(TTFont(...)) on every PDF generation with no guard. TTFont() reads and parses .ttf files from disk each call even though ReportLab's internal guard silently no-ops the registration. Additionally, generate_issued_invoices_pdf and generate_unpaid_invoices_pdf call pdfmetrics.registerFont inline (lines 1117, 1323) completely bypassing the helper.

MedicalService._register_pdf_fonts() has the same issue.

## Location
- medsimples/backend/app/services/invoice_service.py:46, 1117, 1323
- medsimples/backend/app/services/medical_service.py:1072

## Fix
Add a module-level boolean guard to both services:
  _PDF_FONTS_REGISTERED = False
  @staticmethod
  def _register_pdf_fonts():
      global _PDF_FONTS_REGISTERED
      if _PDF_FONTS_REGISTERED:
          return 'DMSans', 'DMSans-Bold'
      ...register...
      _PDF_FONTS_REGISTERED = True
      return 'DMSans', 'DMSans-Bold'

Remove the two inline registerFont calls in invoice_service.py and call _register_pdf_fonts() instead.

## Validation Criteria
- [ ] TTF files read only once per process lifetime
- [ ] All 5 PDF generators still produce valid output
- [ ] uv run ruff check . passes
title: Perf: Add font registration guard flag to prevent per-PDF TTF re-reads

description:
## Issue
LogoUpload.tsx uses both useEffect([previewUrl]) cleanup AND inline URL.revokeObjectURL(objectUrl) on the success path. This causes double/triple revocation of the same object URL in certain sequences (rapid file selection or concurrent upload). The effect also fires on every previewUrl transition, not just on unmount, adding fragility.

## Location
medsimples/frontend/src/components/LogoUpload.tsx:64-68, 85-100

## Fix
Use an unmount-only ref pattern:
  const activeObjectUrlRef = useRef<string | null>(null);
  useEffect(() => () => {
    if (activeObjectUrlRef.current) URL.revokeObjectURL(activeObjectUrlRef.current);
  }, []); // empty — unmount only
Assign activeObjectUrlRef.current = objectUrl after createObjectURL. Revoke via the ref in the upload handler on success/error. Remove the useEffect([previewUrl]) cleanup.

## Validation Criteria
- [ ] Object URL revoked exactly once per creation
- [ ] No memory leak on component unmount
- [ ] bun run tsc --noEmit passes
title: Fix: duplicate URL.revokeObjectURL — consolidate to unmount-only ref in LogoUpload.tsx

description:
## What

Add `logo_path` (nullable Text) to `organizations` and `doctors` tables via Alembic migration. Update SQLAlchemy models and Pydantic schemas.

## Context

- Latest migration: `n2c3d4e5f6a7_fix_invites_rls_policy.py` — use as `down_revision`
- Next revision ID in sequence: `o3d4e5f6a7b8`
- SQLite: project is PostgreSQL-only (CLAUDE.md). No SQLite compat code needed.
- RLS: doctors table has org-isolation RLS via `organization_id`. Adding `logo_path` requires NO RLS policy changes — it's data within existing context.
- Organizations table has NO RLS policy.
- Column is purely nullable, no constraints, no backfill, no server_default needed.
- Model type must match migration: `Column(Text, nullable=True)` in model, `sa.Text()` in migration.

Migration pattern (from existing migrations like `16235105ba7f_add_zip_code_city_code_to_doctors.py`):
```python
def upgrade() -> None:
    op.add_column('organizations', sa.Column('logo_path', sa.Text(), nullable=True))
    op.add_column('doctors', sa.Column('logo_path', sa.Text(), nullable=True))

def downgrade() -> None:
    op.drop_column('doctors', 'logo_path')
    op.drop_column('organizations', 'logo_path')
```

Files to update:
- `models.py` Organization class (~line 39): add `logo_path = Column(Text, nullable=True)`
- `models.py` Doctor class (~line 239): add `logo_path = Column(Text, nullable=True)`
- `schemas/organization.py`: add `logo_path: str | None = None` to OrganizationResponse and OrganizationUpdate
- `schemas/doctor.py`: add `logo_path: str | None = None` to DoctorResponse and DoctorUpdate

## Testing

- [ ] `uv run alembic upgrade head` succeeds
- [ ] `uv run alembic downgrade -1` succeeds and removes both columns
- [ ] `uv run alembic upgrade head` again succeeds (round-trip)
- [ ] `uv run ruff check .` passes

## Validation

- [ ] `\d organizations` in psql shows `logo_path text` column
- [ ] `\d doctors` in psql shows `logo_path text` column
- [ ] OrganizationResponse schema includes `logo_path: str | None`
- [ ] DoctorResponse schema includes `logo_path: str | None`
- [ ] Existing org/doctor API responses still work (logo_path is null for existing records)

## Files

- `medsimples/backend/app/models.py`
- `medsimples/backend/app/schemas/organization.py`
- `medsimples/backend/app/schemas/doctor.py`
- `medsimples/backend/alembic/versions/o3d4e5f6a7b8_add_logo_path_to_org_and_doctor.py` (new)

## Dependencies

Can run in parallel with bead .1. Bead .3 and .4 depend on this.
title: Add logo_path to Organization and Doctor models + migration

description:
## Issue
Two forms of duplication:
1. _ALLOWED_LOGO_TYPES, _LOGO_MAX_BYTES, and the 12-line chunked-read loop are copy-pasted between organizations.py and doctors.py.
2. upload_logo / delete_logo / get_logo_path are near-identical (~180 lines total) across OrganizationService and DoctorService — only the entity type and directory name differ.

2. Extract image processing + atomic write into logo_utils.py: process_and_save_logo(content, dest_dir, entity_subdir) -> str (returns relative path)

description:
## Issue
POST logo endpoints have @limiter.limit('5/minute') but GET and DELETE endpoints on both org and doctor routers have no rate limit. GET enables logo-presence enumeration across tenants; DELETE is inconsistent with POST.

## Location
- medsimples/backend/app/routers/organizations.py (GET:258, DELETE:274)
- medsimples/backend/app/routers/doctors.py (GET:143, DELETE:166)

## Fix
Add @limiter.limit('30/minute') to both GET endpoints and @limiter.limit('10/minute') to both DELETE endpoints.

description:
## Issue
`GET /organizations/{id}` uses `get_current_user` (no RLS) and has zero org-isolation check. Any authenticated user from Org A can fetch full OrganizationResponse for Org B, exposing CNPJ, email, phone, logo_path (filesystem path), enabled_modules.

## Severity
P1 CRITICAL — A01 Broken Access Control. Trivially exploitable by incrementing organization_id.

## Location
`medsimples/backend/app/routers/organizations.py` — `get_organization` endpoint

## Fix
Add before service call:
```python
if not current_user.is_super_admin and current_user.organization_id \!= organization_id:
    raise HTTPException(status_code=403, detail="Acesso negado")
```
Switch dependency to `get_current_user_with_rls` for consistency.

## Validation Criteria
- [ ] User from Org A receives 403 when fetching Org B's details
- [ ] Superadmin can still fetch any org
- [ ] uv run ruff check . passes
title: Security: GET /organizations/{id} has no org-isolation auth check

description:
## Issue
`OrganizationResponse.logo_path` returns the raw relative filesystem path (e.g. `logos/org_3/abc.png`) to API consumers. This leaks internal storage layout and is inconsistent with DoctorResponse which correctly returns `logo_url` (a URL). If storage is ever served statically, this path becomes a direct file access vector without auth.

## Severity
P1 CRITICAL — A01+A02. Filesystem path should never appear in public API contracts.

## Location
`medsimples/backend/app/schemas/organization.py` — OrganizationResponse.logo_path field
`medsimples/backend/app/services/organization_service.py` — _to_response()

## Fix
1. Remove `logo_path` from `OrganizationResponse`
2. Add `logo_url: str | None = None` to `OrganizationResponse`
3. In `OrganizationService._to_response()`: set `logo_url=f"/api/organizations/{org.id}/logo" if org.logo_path else None`

## Validation Criteria
- [ ] OrganizationResponse no longer contains logo_path
- [ ] logo_url returned as /api/organizations/{id}/logo when logo exists
- [ ] Frontend logo display still works
title: Security: OrganizationResponse exposes raw filesystem logo_path

description:
## What

Add logo upload and serve endpoints for organizations and doctors.

Endpoints:
- `POST /api/organizations/{id}/logo` — upload org logo (admin/manager for that org)
- `POST /api/doctors/{id}/logo` — upload doctor logo (self or admin in same org)
- `GET /api/organizations/{id}/logo` — serve logo (authenticated)
- `GET /api/doctors/{id}/logo` — serve logo (authenticated)
- `DELETE /api/organizations/{id}/logo` — remove org logo
- `DELETE /api/doctors/{id}/logo` — remove doctor logo

## Context

**Dependency:** Add `pillow` to `medsimples/pyproject.toml` before implementing upload logic.

**Storage:**
- Add `storage_path_logos` property to `settings.py`: `f"{self.storage_path}/logos"`
- Directory structure: `{storage_path_logos}/org_{id}/` and `{storage_path_logos}/doctor_{id}/`
- `dest.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)` inside the upload handler — NOT at startup (dirs don't exist until first upload)
- Store RELATIVE path in DB (`"logos/org_5/abc123.png"`), join with `storage_path` at read time
- Follow pattern from `organization_service.py upload_certificate()`

**Upload validation pipeline (simplified — Pillow is the single source of truth):**
1. Fast-reject on declared size: `if file.size and file.size > 5*1024*1024: raise 413`
2. Declared MIME gate: content_type must be in `{"image/png", "image/jpeg", "image/webp"}`
3. Bounded read: read in 256KB chunks, reject with 413 if total > 5MB (do NOT call `await file.read()` without limit — `file.size` is unreliable for chunked transfers)
4. Decompression bomb guard: `Image.MAX_IMAGE_PIXELS = 25_000_000` before any `Image.open()` call
5. Pillow decode + validate: `Image.open(BytesIO(content)); img.load()` — this catches non-image files, corrupt files, and wrong formats (replaces separate magic byte check)
6. Thumbnail: `img.thumbnail((800, 800))` — enforces size limit server-side (client-side resize removed from frontend)
7. Re-encode as PNG (strips EXIF/ICC payloads): `img.convert("RGB").save(out_buf, "PNG")`
   - Note: consider JPEG output (`img.save(out_buf, "JPEG", quality=85)`) for 5-10x smaller files and faster FileResponse. PNG is safest for quality, JPEG is better for performance. Choose based on quality requirements.

**Filename generation:** Always UUID, never use `file.filename` in path:
```python
from pathlib import Path
import uuid
fname = f"{uuid.uuid4().hex}.png"  # or .jpg if using JPEG
dest = Path(settings.storage_path_logos) / f"org_{org_id}" / fname
dest.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
```

**Path containment assertion** — use `Path.resolve()` + `Path.relative_to()` (relative_to raises ValueError if not under base, no string manipulation needed):
```python
from pathlib import Path
base = Path(settings.storage_path).resolve()
resolved = Path(dest).resolve()
try:
    resolved.relative_to(base)  # raises ValueError if not under base
except ValueError:
    raise HTTPException(status_code=500, detail="Storage path error")
```

**Async/Pillow:** Pillow re-encoding is CPU-bound and blocks the event loop in `async def`. Use one of:
- Make the endpoint `def` (sync — uvicorn runs it in a threadpool), OR
- `await asyncio.get_event_loop().run_in_executor(None, _process_image_sync, content)`
Preferred: `def` endpoint is simpler and consistent with other file-processing endpoints.

**Atomic write:** write to tempfile then `os.replace(tmp, dest)` (POSIX-atomic):
```python
import tempfile, os
from pathlib import Path
with tempfile.NamedTemporaryFile(dir=dest.parent, delete=False, suffix=".tmp") as tmp:
    tmp.write(encoded_bytes)
    tmp_path = tmp.name
os.replace(tmp_path, dest)
```

**Old file cleanup:** write new file first, then delete old:
```python
if old_logo_path:
    try:
        Path(settings.storage_path, old_logo_path).unlink(missing_ok=True)
    except OSError as e:
        logger.warning("logo_cleanup_failed", path=old_logo_path, error=str(e))
```

**Rate limiting:** Apply `@limiter.limit("5/minute")` to POST logo endpoints (consistent with export endpoints).

**Audit logging:** Log structured event in service layer:
```python
logger.info("logo_uploaded", org_id=..., doctor_id=..., user_id=current_user.id, filename=fname, ip=request.client.host)
```

**Authorization — org logo:**
- Dependency: `get_current_user_with_rls` (sets RLS context — NOT legacy `admin_only`/`RoleChecker`)
- Router-level checks (in order, before any file processing):
  ```python
  if not current_user.is_super_admin:
      if current_user.organization_id != organization_id:
          raise HTTPException(status_code=403, detail="Forbidden")
      if current_user.org_role not in (OrgRole.admin, OrgRole.manager):
          raise HTTPException(status_code=403, detail="Insufficient role")
  ```

**Authorization — doctor logo:**
- Dependency: `get_current_user_with_rls`
- Fetch doctor scoped to caller's org first — return 404 (not 403) if not found (avoids confirming existence of doctors in other orgs):
  ```python
  doctor = db.query(Doctor).filter(
      Doctor.id == doctor_id,
      Doctor.organization_id == current_user.organization_id
  ).first()
  if not doctor:
      raise HTTPException(status_code=404)
  ```
- Then role check: allow if `is_admin/manager` OR `doctor.user_id == current_user.id`

**RLS verification:** Before shipping, confirm UPDATE policies exist on `organizations` and `doctors` tables. Add check to the migration testing checklist: `SELECT * FROM pg_policies WHERE tablename IN ('organizations', 'doctors') AND cmd = 'UPDATE';`

**Serving logos — do NOT static-mount storage_path:**
- GET endpoints stream `FileResponse` with `media_type="image/png"` (or jpg)
- Path containment assertion before streaming (use `Path.resolve()` / `Path.relative_to()` — see above)
- Cache headers: `Cache-Control: private, no-cache, must-revalidate` (do NOT allow the `private, max-age=300` reference-data rule to apply — logos should be validated each time but cached by browser with ETag from FileResponse)
- FastAPI `FileResponse` sets `Last-Modified` automatically; browser uses it for 304 responses

## Testing

- [ ] Upload valid PNG → 200, logo_path stored as relative path in DB
- [ ] Upload JPEG → 200 (re-encoded and stored as PNG/JPEG)
- [ ] Upload file that fails Pillow decode (corrupted, non-image) → 400
- [ ] Upload file > 5 MB → 413
- [ ] Upload invalid content_type → 415
- [ ] Upload image > 800px → stored thumbnail (verify dimensions in stored file)
- [ ] Doctor uploads own logo → 200
- [ ] Doctor uploads doctor from another org (valid doctor_id, wrong org) → 404
- [ ] Org-admin uploads to different org → 403
- [ ] Non-admin/manager uploads org logo → 403
- [ ] Replace logo → old file deleted from disk, new stored
- [ ] DELETE logo → logo_path is None in DB
- [ ] GET logo → FileResponse with correct content-type, Cache-Control headers present
- [ ] GET logo unauthenticated → 401
- [ ] Burst of 6 uploads → 6th returns 429 (rate limited)
- [ ] Audit log entry present after upload

## Validation

- [ ] `pillow` in pyproject.toml
- [ ] Stored file is PNG (or JPEG) regardless of input format
- [ ] Stored path is relative
- [ ] Old file deleted on replacement
- [ ] Path containment uses `Path.resolve()` + `Path.relative_to()` (no string manipulation)
- [ ] Pillow called in sync context (endpoint is `def` or uses `run_in_executor`)
- [ ] RLS UPDATE policies verified on organizations and doctors
- [ ] `ruff check .` passes

## Files

- `medsimples/backend/app/routers/organizations.py`
- `medsimples/backend/app/routers/doctors.py`
- `medsimples/backend/app/services/organization_service.py`
- `medsimples/backend/app/services/doctor_service.py`
- `medsimples/backend/app/core/settings.py` (add storage_path_logos)
- `medsimples/pyproject.toml` (add pillow dependency)

## Dependencies

Depends on .2 (logo_path column must exist).
title: Logo upload endpoints (org + doctor)

description:
## Issue
When the path-containment guard fires (`dest.resolve().relative_to(base)` raises ValueError), both `logo_utils.py` and `get_logo_path` in both services raise a bare `RuntimeError("Storage path error")`. FastAPI has no handler for RuntimeError — it returns HTTP 500 with the exception detail, including a full traceback in debug mode. Exposes server internals.

## Severity
P1 CRITICAL — A05 Security Misconfiguration. Storage path errors should return 400, not 500 with stack trace.

## Location
- `medsimples/backend/app/utils/logo_utils.py:47`
- `medsimples/backend/app/services/organization_service.py:427`
- `medsimples/backend/app/services/doctor_service.py:122`

## Fix
Replace `raise RuntimeError("Storage path error")` with `raise ValidationError("Requisição inválida")` in all three locations. ValidationError already maps to HTTP 400 via the exception handler.

## Validation Criteria
- [ ] Path traversal trigger returns HTTP 400, not 500
- [ ] No stack trace exposed in response body
- [ ] uv run ruff check . passes
title: Security: RuntimeError from path traversal guard produces unhandled 500
Frontend: No unmount guard — setState called on dead component after upload

description:
## Issue
`handleRemove` has no synchronous guard: (1) double-tapping Delete on mobile fires two concurrent DELETE requests; (2) after a successful upload, `setPreviewUrl(null)` makes the Delete button reappear before the server image loads — user can immediately delete the logo just uploaded.

## Severity
P1 CRITICAL — Race condition produces data loss (logo deleted immediately after upload) and duplicate DELETE requests.

## Location
`medsimples/frontend/src/components/LogoUpload.tsx:115-128`

## Fix
1. Add `removingRef = useRef(false)` mirroring uploadingRef
2. At start of handleRemove: `if (removingRef.current || uploadingRef.current) return; removingRef.current = true;`
3. In finally: `removingRef.current = false;`

## Validation Criteria
- [ ] Double-tap on Delete button only fires one DELETE request
- [ ] Delete button not accessible while upload is in progress
- [ ] bun run tsc --noEmit passes
title: Frontend: handleRemove has no concurrent guard — double-tap and upload+remove race

description:
## Issue
`_fonts_registered = False` is a bare module-level bool in both `medical_service.py` and `invoice_service.py`. Under concurrent PDF generation requests sharing a uvicorn worker thread pool, two threads can simultaneously observe `False` and both call `pdfmetrics.registerFont()`. ReportLab font registration is not thread-safe. A second registration call can raise or produce corrupt font state, causing PDFs to fall back to Helvetica (wrong font in medical documents — patient safety / regulatory concern).

## Severity
P1 CRITICAL — Silent correctness failure in production PDF generation under concurrent load.

## Location
- `medsimples/backend/app/services/medical_service.py:30` (module-level flag)
- `medsimples/backend/app/services/invoice_service.py:38` (module-level flag)

## Fix
Add threading.Lock with double-checked locking:
```python
import threading
_font_lock = threading.Lock()
_fonts_registered = False

def _register_pdf_fonts():
    global _fonts_registered
    if _fonts_registered:
        return
    with _font_lock:
        if _fonts_registered:
            return
        # ... registerFont calls ...
        _fonts_registered = True
```
Apply to both service files.

## Validation Criteria
- [ ] Only one font registration occurs under concurrent PDF requests
- [ ] DMSans font used in all PDFs (not Helvetica fallback)
- [ ] uv run ruff check . passes
title: Perf: Font registration _fonts_registered flag is not thread-safe

description:
## Issue
Both upload endpoints check `file.content_type` which is the value the attacker sets in the multipart Content-Type field. Any file can pass with `Content-Type: image/png`. Pillow re-encoding mitigates the worst case but provides no layered defense against Pillow CVEs.

## Fix
Add magic-byte sniffing before passing to Pillow:
```python
PNG_MAGIC = b'\x89PNG'
JPEG_MAGIC = b'\xff\xd8\xff'
if not (content.startswith(PNG_MAGIC) or content.startswith(JPEG_MAGIC) or
        (content[:4] == b'RIFF' and content[8:12] == b'WEBP')):
    raise HTTPException(status_code=415, detail="Tipo de arquivo não permitido")
```
Add to `save_logo_file` in logo_utils.py before Image.open().

## Validation Criteria
- [ ] A PHP file with Content-Type: image/png is rejected
- [ ] Valid PNG/JPEG/WebP files still upload successfully
- [ ] uv run ruff check . passes
title: Security: file.content_type MIME check is client-supplied and bypassable

description:
## Issue
`Image.MAX_IMAGE_PIXELS = 25_000_000` permits a 25 MP image to fully decode to ~75 MB RSS before thumbnail reduces it. At 5 MB max upload, a crafted PNG can expand to ~150 MB of pixel data (still under the 25 MP cap). At 30 req/min rate limit, an attacker behind multiple IPs can force hundreds of MB of RAM allocations per minute.

## Fix
After `Image.open()` (lazy, before `img.load()`), check dimensions:
```python
w, h = img.size
if w * h > 4_000_000:  # 4 MP sufficient for any logo
    raise ValidationError("Imagem muito grande")
```
Also lower `MAX_IMAGE_PIXELS` constant to 4_000_000 to match.

## Validation Criteria
- [ ] Images > 4 MP rejected with 422
- [ ] Normal logo images (< 800x800 typically) still upload
- [ ] uv run ruff check . passes
title: Security: Decompression bomb — reject images before img.load() via dimension pre-check

description:

`DoctorService._to_response()` hardcodes `"/api/doctors/{doctor.id}/logo"`. Service layer should not encode router URL structure. If the API prefix changes (versioning, reorganization), this silently breaks.

Extract to a constant in a shared location (e.g. logo_utils.py or settings):

```python
DOCTOR_LOGO_URL_PATTERN = "/api/doctors/{doctor_id}/logo"
```
Or use a simpler approach: accept a `logo_url_fn: Callable[[int], str]` parameter, or move URL construction to the router layer.

## Validation Criteria

- [ ] logo_url still returned correctly in API responses

description:
## Issue
`DoctorService._to_response()` constructs `DoctorResponse(id=..., name=..., logo_url=...)`. If `DoctorResponse` has required fields beyond these three (from `BaseResponse`), instantiation will fail at runtime. Additionally `get_active_doctors()` and `get_doctor_by_id()` now return `DoctorResponse` (only 3 fields) while previously returning full `Doctor` ORM objects with all fields. Any frontend consuming these endpoints expecting the full doctor profile will receive truncated data.

## Severity
P2 — Silent regression risk. Verify that all callers of `get_active_doctors()` and `get_doctor_by_id()` only need id/name/logo_url.

## Location
`medsimples/backend/app/services/doctor_service.py:23-27, 36-37, 40-42`

- [ ] No runtime Pydantic validation errors on doctor list/detail endpoints

description:
## Issue
`upload_doctor_logo` and `upload_org_logo` are sync `def` endpoints that read `UploadFile` via `file.file.read(chunk_size)` (raw SpooledTemporaryFile access). `upload_certificate` in organizations.py correctly uses `async def` + `await file.read()`. Inconsistency means logo uploads run in the threadpool via direct file access while the documented FastAPI pattern for UploadFile is async.

## Location
`medsimples/backend/app/routers/doctors.py:93` (upload_doctor_logo)
`medsimples/backend/app/routers/organizations.py:214` (upload_org_logo)

## Fix
Convert both to `async def`, use `content = await file.read()`, add size check after read, then call `save_logo_file` via `asyncio.get_event_loop().run_in_executor(None, save_logo_file, content, dest, ...)` for the CPU-bound Pillow work.

## Validation Criteria
- [ ] Upload endpoints are async def
- [ ] await file.read() used instead of chunked sync read
- [ ] uv run ruff check . passes
title: Python: upload_logo sync/async inconsistency — should use async + await file.read()

description:
## What

Create `get_logo_path(doctor, org)` pure utility function and update PDF-generating functions to call it. Add `logo_url` field to DoctorResponse schema to avoid N+1 logo GET requests.

## Context

**File location:** `backend/app/utils/logo_utils.py` — NOT a service file. Pure function with no DB session or state. Matches `utils/date_utils.py` pattern.

**Path containment — use `pathlib.Path.resolve()` + `relative_to()`:**
```python
from pathlib import Path
from typing import TYPE_CHECKING
if TYPE_CHECKING:
    from ..models import Doctor, Organization

def get_logo_path(doctor: "Doctor | None", org: "Organization | None") -> str | None:
    """Resolve logo path: doctor logo > org logo > static fallback."""
    from ..core.settings import settings

    static_logo = Path(__file__).parent.parent.parent / "static" / "logo.png"
    base = Path(settings.storage_path).resolve()

    candidates = []
    if doctor and doctor.logo_path:
        candidates.append(Path(settings.storage_path) / doctor.logo_path)
    if org and org.logo_path:
        candidates.append(Path(settings.storage_path) / org.logo_path)

    for path in candidates:
        try:
            resolved = path.resolve()
            resolved.relative_to(base)  # raises ValueError if outside storage_path
        except ValueError:
            continue  # path traversal detected — skip
        if resolved.exists():
            return str(resolved)

    return str(static_logo) if static_logo.exists() else None
```

Note: `Path.relative_to()` is the clean pathlib containment check — no string `startswith()` + `os.sep` needed.

**Call get_logo_path() ONCE per PDF function** at the top (after loading objects), not inside loops or sub-helpers:
```python
# At the start of each PDF generation function:
logo_path = get_logo_path(doctor_obj, org_obj)
# Then use logo_path throughout the function
if logo_path:
    logo_img = Image(logo_path, width=1.5*cm, height=1.5*cm)
```

**PDF functions in scope and org context strategy:**

1. `medical_service.py:generate_visit_summary_pdf()` — line ~1298
   - Already loads `Visit.doctor` via joinedload
   - Add `joinedload(Visit.doctor, Doctor.organization)` to also load org
   - Call: `get_logo_path(visit.doctor, visit.doctor.organization)`

2. `medical_service.py:generate_prescription_pdf()` — line ~1501, currently NO logo
   - Same joinedload additions
   - Add logo header: logo (1.5×1.5 cm, LEFT) + title (right-aligned) in a two-column Table, matching visit_summary pattern
   - Call: `get_logo_path(visit.doctor, visit.doctor.organization)`

3. `invoice_service.py:generate_recibo_pdf()` — line ~834
   - Will be REDESIGNED to use ReportLab flowables (same architecture as the other 4 functions).
   - The old PyPDF2 canvas overlay approach (blank template PDF + `c.drawString()`) is being replaced.
   - The implementer should:
     - Remove the PyPDF2/canvas overlay approach entirely for this function
     - Replace with `SimpleDocTemplate` + platypus flowables matching the pattern in `medical_service.py`
     - Use `get_logo_path(None, invoice.organization)` for the logo
     - Load org via `joinedload(Invoice.organization)` in the query that fetches the invoice
   - This means all 5 PDF functions use the same ReportLab flowable architecture.

4. `invoice_service.py:generate_issued_invoices_pdf()` — line ~1038
   - Org-scoped report, no doctor or org param received from router.
   - Org context strategy: load org from `current_user.organization_id` using a single DB query inside the function: `org = self.db.query(Organization).filter(Organization.id == current_user.organization_id).first()` — this is RLS-scoped and adds only one query per report generation (not per PDF).
   - Alternatively: pass `current_user` as a parameter to the function (router already has it).
   - Call: `get_logo_path(None, org)`

5. `invoice_service.py:generate_unpaid_invoices_pdf()` — line ~1038+
   - Same pattern as #4.

**Schema update — add `logo_url` to DoctorResponse:**
Add `logo_url: str | None = None` to `DoctorResponse` in `schemas/doctor.py`. Populate in the service layer as `/api/doctors/{id}/logo` if `doctor.logo_path` is set, else `None`. This avoids N separate GET requests when displaying a list of doctors.

**DB values are relative paths** (e.g., `"logos/org_5/abc.png"`). Join with `settings.storage_path` at read time. Never store absolute paths in the DB.

## Testing

- [ ] `get_logo_path(doctor_with_logo, org)` → returns doctor logo path
- [ ] `get_logo_path(doctor_no_logo, org_with_logo)` → returns org logo path
- [ ] `get_logo_path(doctor_no_logo, org_no_logo)` → returns static/logo.png path
- [ ] `get_logo_path(None, None)` → returns static/logo.png or None if missing
- [ ] DB value with path traversal (`"../../etc/passwd"`) → skipped by Path.relative_to() containment check
- [ ] Symlink inside storage_path pointing outside → skipped (resolve() follows symlinks)
- [ ] Visit summary PDF: correct logo in header
- [ ] Prescription PDF: logo now appears in header (new addition)
- [ ] Invoice org-report PDFs: org logo in header
- [ ] All PDFs fall back to static/logo.png when no logo set
- [ ] DoctorResponse includes `logo_url` when logo_path is set
- [ ] Doctor list API response includes logo_url (no N+1 GET requests needed)

## Validation

- [ ] `get_logo_path()` called once per PDF function at the top (not inside loops)
- [ ] All applicable PDF functions call `get_logo_path()`
- [ ] Path containment uses `Path.resolve()` + `relative_to()` (no os.path.startswith)
- [ ] `grep -r 'static.*logo.png' backend/app/services/` returns nothing for the 5 flowable-based functions
- [ ] `utils/logo_utils.py` has no SQLAlchemy imports
- [ ] `ruff check .` passes
- [ ] `schemas/doctor.py` DoctorResponse has `logo_url: str | None`
- [ ] `generate_recibo_pdf` uses ReportLab flowables (not PyPDF2 canvas)

## Files

Depends on .2 (logo_path must exist on Doctor and Organization models).
title: Logo resolution helper + update PDF generators

description:
## Issue
`doctor_service.py:64` calls `update_data.dict(exclude_unset=True)`. Pydantic v2 deprecated `.dict()` in favour of `.model_dump()`. `organization_service.py:124` already uses `.model_dump()` correctly — this is an inconsistency.

## Location
`medsimples/backend/app/services/doctor_service.py:64`

## Fix
`update_dict = update_data.model_dump(exclude_unset=True)`

## Validation Criteria
- [ ] No Pydantic deprecation warnings in test output
- [ ] Doctor profile updates still work
- [ ] uv run ruff check . passes
title: Python: .dict() deprecated — use .model_dump() in update_doctor_profile

description: ## Issue On successful upload, `setPreviewUrl(null)` is called while `setHasLogo(true)` in the same batch. This causes displaySrc to switch from the local blob preview to the server URL immediately. If the server image hasn't loaded yet (slow connection, large logo), there is a 1-frame flash of the 'Nenhum logotipo cadastrado' placeholder. ## Location `medsimples/frontend/src/components/LogoUpload.tsx:105` ## Fix Keep previewUrl until the server `` fires onLoad: ```tsx setPreviewUrl(null)} ... /> ``` Only clear previewUrl in the onLoad callback, not in the upload success handler. ## Validation Criteria - [ ] No placeholder flash visible after successful upload on slow connection (test with DevTools throttling) - [ ] bun run tsc --noEmit passes
title: Frontend: post-upload image flicker — clear previewUrl only after server image loads
Frontend: OrganizationDetail fetch has no unmount cancellation guard

description:
## Issue
`DoctorRepository.get_by_id_in_org()` does not filter on `Doctor.active`. Logo upload/get/delete endpoints work on inactive (anonymized/soft-deleted) doctor records. While the LGPD export endpoint intentionally needs inactive records, logo operations on inactive doctors should arguably be blocked.

## Location
`medsimples/backend/app/repositories/doctor_repository.py:21-27`

## Fix
Decide whether logo endpoints should require active doctors. If yes, add `.filter(Doctor.active.is_(True))` to the query, or perform the active check in the service layer.

## Validation Criteria
- [ ] Logo upload endpoint returns 404 for inactive doctor IDs (or explicitly documented that it should not)
- [ ] uv run ruff check . passes
title: Python: get_by_id_in_org doesn't filter for active doctors

description:
## Issue
`_fonts_registered = False` and registration logic is duplicated in both `medical_service.py` and `invoice_service.py`. ReportLab font registration is process-global — registering once makes fonts available everywhere. Two independent flags waste memory and can race.

## Fix
Create `medsimples/backend/app/utils/pdf_fonts.py` with a single `ensure_fonts_registered()` function using one module-level flag + threading.Lock. Both services call `ensure_fonts_registered()` instead of maintaining their own flags.

## Validation Criteria
- [ ] One registration flag/lock in the entire codebase
- [ ] Both medical and invoice PDFs still use DMSans
- [ ] uv run ruff check . passes
title: Simplify: Consolidate _fonts_registered into shared pdf_fonts utility

description:
## Issue
`save_logo_file(storage_path=None)` silently skips the path traversal guard and old-file cleanup when storage_path is None. No caller passes None. The optional default creates a footgun — a future caller that forgets storage_path bypasses security with no warning.

## Location
`medsimples/backend/app/utils/logo_utils.py:23-24, 42-47, 67`

## Fix
Make storage_path required: `def save_logo_file(content, dest, old_path=None, storage_path: Path)`
Remove the `if storage_path is not None:` conditional guards — always run them.

## Validation Criteria
- [ ] storage_path parameter has no default value
- [ ] Both callers still pass storage_path explicitly
- [ ] uv run ruff check . passes
title: Simplify: Make storage_path required in save_logo_file (remove Optional footgun)

description:
## Multiple P3 cleanup items from review:

### 1. organization_service.py inline re-import
Line 405-406: `from ..utils.exceptions import ValidationError as VE` — ValidationError is already imported at top of file. Replace with direct `raise ValidationError(...)`.

### 2. Portuguese accents in organization_service.py
Multiple user-facing strings missing accents (pre-existing). Examples:
- 'Organizacao nao encontrada' → 'Organização não encontrada'
- 'CNPJ ja cadastrado' → 'CNPJ já cadastrado'
- 'Endereco obrigatorio' → 'Endereço obrigatório'

### 3. structlog extra={} bug in doctor_service.py
Lines 162-166, 196-203: structlog ignores Python logging-style `extra={...}` kwargs — fields are nested under 'extra' key instead of top-level. Use direct kwargs: `logger.info('event', field=value)` not `logger.info('event', extra={'field': value})`.

### 4. tabIndex=0 on VisuallyHiddenInput
LogoUpload.tsx:223 — hidden input has tabIndex=0, trapping keyboard focus. Change to tabIndex={-1} since the Button parent handles keyboard activation.

## Validation Criteria
- [ ] No inline re-imports in organization_service
- [ ] Accents correct in organization error strings
- [ ] structlog calls use direct kwargs
- [ ] Hidden input not keyboard-focusable
title: P3: Add magic byte sniff + inline re-import cleanup + Portuguese accents in org strings
Frontend: logo upload UI in org settings and doctor profile

description:
## What

Replace the PyPDF2 canvas-overlay implementation of `generate_recibo_pdf()` with a ReportLab `SimpleDocTemplate` + platypus flowables approach, matching the architecture of all other PDF generators in the codebase. Add logo support using `get_logo_path()` from bead .4.

## Context

**Current implementation** (`invoice_service.py:444`):
- Loads `backend/static/recibo-em-branco.pdf` as a blank template
- Overlays text using `canvas.Canvas` + `c.drawString()` with hardcoded coordinates
- Uses `letter` pagesize (not A4 — inconsistency with the rest of the codebase)
- Has a `# TODO: replace this with the template system` comment
- Uses PyPDF2 to merge the canvas overlay onto the blank template
- Cannot use logo branding (canvas coordinates would need to match the template layout exactly)

**Target architecture** (from `medical_service.py:generate_visit_summary_pdf()` and `pdf-generation.md` rules):
- `SimpleDocTemplate` with A4 pagesize, 2 cm margins
- Platypus flowables: `Paragraph`, `Table`, `HRFlowable`, `Spacer`, `Image`
- DMSans font (registered via `pdfmetrics`)
- Design system colors: DARK, ACCENT, LIGHT_BG, BORDER, TEXT_MUTED, WHITE
- Logo header: two-column `Table` with `get_logo_path(None, org)` left + title right-aligned
- Info cards for patient/doctor/insurer data (two-column Table with alternating LIGHT_BG/WHITE rows)
- No dependency on a blank template PDF — generated entirely in code

**Logo:** `get_logo_path(None, invoice.organization)` — recibo is org-branded, not doctor-branded.
Load org via `selectinload(Invoice.organization)` in the query that fetches the invoice (add to repository if not already present).

**Content to preserve from current implementation:**
- Doctor name + professional_info
- Patient name
- Insurer info (name, business_name, contact_name, address)
- Visit dates and descriptions
- Invoice items (description, quantity, unit price, total)
- Total amount
- Payment method
- Current date
- Signature line / footer

**File to delete:** `backend/static/recibo-em-branco.pdf` — no longer needed once redesign is complete.

**Function signature stays the same:** `generate_recibo_pdf(self, invoice: Invoice) -> str`

## Testing

- [ ] Recibo PDF generates without errors for an invoice with visits
- [ ] Logo appears in header when org has logo set
- [ ] Falls back to static/logo.png when no org logo
- [ ] All existing data fields present (doctor, patient, insurer, items, total, date)
- [ ] A4 pagesize (not letter)
- [ ] DMSans font used
- [ ] No reference to `recibo-em-branco.pdf` remains in codebase
- [ ] `uv run ruff check .` passes

## Validation

- [ ] `grep -r "recibo-em-branco" backend/` returns nothing
- [ ] `grep -r "canvas.Canvas" backend/app/services/invoice_service.py` returns nothing
- [ ] PDF visually matches design system (ACCENT headers, LIGHT_BG alternating rows)
- [ ] `generate_recibo_pdf` uses `get_logo_path()` from `utils/logo_utils.py`

## Files

- `medsimples/backend/app/services/invoice_service.py`
- `medsimples/backend/app/static/recibo-em-branco.pdf` (delete)

## Dependencies

Depends on .4 (logo_utils.py must exist with get_logo_path()).
title: Redesign generate_recibo_pdf from PyPDF2 canvas to ReportLab flowables

description:
## Issue
LogoUpload.tsx:95 manually sets `Content-Type: 'multipart/form-data'` on the Axios request. When using FormData, Axios auto-generates the Content-Type header including the multipart boundary. Overriding it removes the boundary, causing FastAPI to reject every logo upload with a 400 error in production.

## Severity
P1 CRITICAL — Every logo upload fails in production. The feature is non-functional.

## Location
`medsimples/frontend/src/components/LogoUpload.tsx:95`

## Fix
Remove the `headers` config object entirely from the axios post call. Axios sets the correct multipart Content-Type with boundary automatically when passed a FormData object.

## Validation Criteria
- [ ] Logo upload succeeds in production (200 response)
- [ ] No explicit Content-Type header in the request
- [ ] Both org and doctor upload endpoints work
- [ ] `bun run tsc --noEmit` passes
title: Fix: Content-Type override breaks multipart upload in LogoUpload.tsx

description:
## Issue
`organization_service.py:443` and `doctor_service.py:119` both do `from fastapi import HTTPException` inside their `get_logo_path` methods and raise HTTPException(404). Services must raise domain exceptions (`NotFoundError` from `utils/exceptions.py`). This violates the layered architecture contract (routers/ → services/ → repositories/), makes services dependent on the HTTP transport layer, and makes them untestable without a running ASGI app.

## Severity
P1 CRITICAL — Architecture violation. Services already raise NotFoundError everywhere else (e.g. organization_service.py:76). These two methods break the contract.

## Location
- `medsimples/backend/app/services/organization_service.py:443-444`
- `medsimples/backend/app/services/doctor_service.py:119-120`

## Fix
Replace `from fastapi import HTTPException; raise HTTPException(status_code=404, ...)` with `raise NotFoundError('Logo não encontrado')`. The router's existing exception handler already translates NotFoundError to HTTP 404.

## Validation Criteria
- [ ] No `HTTPException` import in either service file
- [ ] GET logo endpoints still return 404 when logo_path is None
- [ ] `uv run ruff check .` passes
title: Fix: HTTPException raised inside service layer (arch violation)

description:
## Issue
`doctors.py:103, 150, 173` all perform `db.query(Doctor).filter(Doctor.id == doctor_id, Doctor.organization_id == current_user.organization_id).first()` directly in the router. This bypasses DoctorService and DoctorRepository, duplicates the org-scoping logic in three places, and makes the IDOR guard unreachable from non-HTTP callers (Telegram bot, CogniTest backend).

## Severity
P1 CRITICAL — Architecture violation. Every other endpoint in the file delegates to the service layer. The org-scoping logic should be encapsulated in `DoctorService`.

## Location
- `medsimples/backend/app/routers/doctors.py:103-108`
- `medsimples/backend/app/routers/doctors.py:150-155`
- `medsimples/backend/app/routers/doctors.py:173-178`

## Fix
Add `DoctorService.get_doctor_by_id_in_org(doctor_id, org_id) -> Doctor` method that performs the scoped query and raises NotFoundError if not found. Replace the three inline queries in the router with calls to this method.

## Validation Criteria
- [ ] No `db.query(Doctor)` in `routers/doctors.py`
- [ ] IDOR guard still works (wrong org returns 404)
- [ ] `uv run ruff check .` passes
title: Fix: Raw db.query(Doctor) in router bypasses service/repository layers

description:
Create developer documentation for working with RLS policies and writing RLS-compliant code.

DOCUMENTATION SCOPE:

1. Developer Guide (docs/rls-guidelines.md):
   - What is RLS and why we use it
   - How RLS context is set (set_rls_context)
   - Rules for creating org-scoped records
   - Common pitfalls and how to avoid them
   - How to run RLS tests locally

2. Service Method Patterns:
   - Pattern: Receive current_user parameter
   - Pattern: Derive org_id from doctor.organization_id
   - Pattern: Inherit org_id from parent (visit → invoice)
   - Anti-pattern: Optional current_user without validation
   - Anti-pattern: Hardcoded org_id = None

3. Testing Guidelines:
   - How to write RLS integration tests
   - Setting up PostgreSQL test database
   - Mocking RLS context in tests
   - Test cases to cover (valid org, NULL org, cross-org)

4. Migration Guidelines:
   - Adding new org_id columns (with backfill)
   - Creating RLS policies for new tables
   - Testing migrations don't break RLS

5. Troubleshooting:
   - Debugging RLS violations in production
   - Reading RLS error messages
   - Using EXPLAIN to see RLS policy evaluation
   - Checking RLS context variables

EXAMPLES:
Include code examples for:
- Correct service method signature with current_user
- Setting org_id from doctor vs current_user
- Writing RLS integration test
- Creating table with RLS policy in migration

DELIVERABLES:
- docs/rls-guidelines.md
- Update CLAUDE.md with RLS patterns
- Code examples in docs/examples/rls/
- Add to onboarding checklist
title: Document RLS testing patterns and developer guidelines

description:
CogniTest backend correctly initializes a shared httpx.AsyncClient via FastAPI lifespan (cognitest/backend/app/main.py). MedSimples creates per-call httpx clients in routers/services, which is less efficient.

WHAT: Add a lifespan context manager to medsimples/backend/app/main.py that creates app.state.http_client on startup and closes it on shutdown. Update any medsimples services/routers that make outbound HTTP calls to accept an optional http_client param (falling back to per-call for backward compat).

REFERENCE IMPLEMENTATION: cognitest/backend/app/main.py lifespan block and MedSimplesClient http_client param pattern (cognitest/backend/app/services/medsimples_client.py).

KNOWN CALLERS in medsimples to check: any service using httpx.AsyncClient or httpx.Client directly.
title: Add httpx AsyncClient singleton via lifespan to MedSimples backend

description:
Build automated validation script that runs before deployment to catch RLS policy violations.

PURPOSE:
Gate deployments on RLS policy compliance. Script should fail deployment if:
- Any table with org_id lacks RLS policy
- Any service method creates records without setting org_id
- Migration adds org_id column without backfilling existing records

VALIDATION CHECKS:

1. Database Schema Validation (SQL queries):
   - Check all tables with org_id column have RLS enabled
   - Verify RLS policies exist for all org-scoped tables
   - Confirm FORCE ROW LEVEL SECURITY is set
   - List tables missing RLS (should be empty)

2. Code Pattern Validation (static analysis):
   - Grep for 'org_id.*=.*None' patterns (potential bugs)
   - Find service create methods missing current_user parameter
   - Detect SQLAlchemy inserts without org_id in columns

3. Migration Validation:
   - Check new org_id columns include data migration
   - Verify CHECK constraints added with cleanup SQL
   - Ensure downgrade() handles org_id removal

IMPLEMENTATION:
- Create backend/scripts/validate_rls.py
- Add to CI pipeline before deployment
- Exit code 1 if any check fails
- Detailed report of violations

OUTPUT FORMAT:
✓ RLS enabled on 15/15 org-scoped tables
✓ All org_id columns have policies
✗ FAILED: Found 2 create methods without current_user
  - medical_service.py:123 create_xyz()
  - patient_service.py:456 create_abc()

DELIVERABLES:
- validate_rls.py script
- CI integration (run in test stage)
- Documentation on adding new org-scoped tables
title: Create pre-deployment RLS validation script
